student.description: Launched on 1st January 1966, the scheme "Senior Hostel (Scheduled Tribes)" by the Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh provides free accommodation and mess facilities to students from studying in Class 9th to 12th in a government/recognized school.

student.name: Madhya Pradesh Professional Talent Examination Award Scheme
student.description: "M.P. Professional Talent Examination Award Scheme" aims to recognize and reward backward-class students who excel in professional entrance examinations. Monetary awards are provided to backward-class students who secure the top three positions in entrance examinations like PET, PPT, and MCA.

student.name: Ambedkar Fellowship Scheme
student.description: The objective of this scheme is to provide financial assistance to meritorious students from the Scheduled Caste category for research at the Ph.D. level. The selected students receive a monthly fellowship grant to support their research in specific subjects.

student.name: Foreign Education Scheme
student.description: The "Foreign Education Scheme" provides financial assistance for non-reserved category students pursuing higher education abroad after Standard 12.

student.name: Financial Assistance for UPSC/APSC Mains Exam
student.description: The scheme provides financial assistance to ST and OBC/MOBC students of Assam who have cleared the APSC/UPSC preliminary exam and are appearing for the mains exam in the current year.

student.name: Freeship Staff Ward
student.description: The scheme aims to support the education of students who are wards of university staff and are pursuing undergraduate, postgraduate, or PhD courses by providing 50% of tuition fee concessions.

student.name: Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose Residential Hostel
student.description: The scheme aims to provide free hostel facilities to children (both boys and girls) aged 6 to 18 years who are enrolled in nearby schools for formal education. In addition, children are provided with a monthly stipend of ₹200/-.

student.name: Orientation Courses in Dramatics
student.description: The "Orientation Courses in Dramatics" scheme by the Tiatr Academy of Goa aims to improve the quality of tiatr performances. It offers preliminary training and workshops in various aspects of dramatics, such as script writing and acting, to individuals and existing tiatr artistes.

student.name: Educational Travel Promotion Scheme
student.description: The "Educational Travel Promotion Scheme" by the Department of Education, aims to promote personality development, observation skills, and curiosity among students through funding educational tours.

student.name: Vidhyadeep Yojana
student.description: The scheme aims to provide accidental insurance cover to students studying in all Primary, Secondary, and Higher Secondary schools across Gujarat State. It offers financial assistance up to ₹50,000/- in case of accidental death or permanent disability to the student's parent or successor.

student.name: Organization of Various Conclaves and Felicitations to Encourage Women, Women Scientists, Teachers and Young Students
student.description: The scheme aims to promote and enhance scientific attitude and skill development of women, women scientists, teachers, and young students of the state by organizing conclaves and felicitating them.

student.name: Pre-Matric & Post-Matric Scholarship
student.description: The scheme aims to provide stipend and financial assistance to students belonging to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community. The benefits include financial assistance and stipends for poor students from Class 9th up to normal Three Years Degree Courses (e.g. Bachelor of Arts, Bachelor of Science, etc.

student.name: Financial Assistance for Higher Studies
student.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance for students belonging to the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community. This assistance supports students pursuing higher education like MBBS, MBA, and Engineering, including studies within and outside the State or Country.

student.name: The CM IT Fellowship Program
student.description: The scheme aims to empower young professionals with technical expertise to address state-level governance challenges through technology. It offers a 2-year M.Tech program in DSAI, hands-on government project experience, and a monthly stipend of ₹50,000/- to eligible youth of Chhattisgarh.

student.name: Scholarship for Ph.D. Students of Socially and Educationally Backward Classes
student.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC) students pursuing M.Phil. or Ph.D. to encourage higher education and research.

student.name: Pandit Omkarnath Music Competition
student.description: The scheme aims to honour the memory of Pandit Omkarnath and promote classical music among young artists by organizing competitive platforms for singing, vocal, and percussion performances. Classical music artists in two age categories can participate.

student.name: A Scheme to Provide Free Tablets to Std.12th Pass Students
student.description: Under the scheme, students in the 12th standard belonging to the Scheduled Caste category will receive a tablet by paying a token fee of ₹1,000/-.

student.name: Mass Communication Internship Programme
student.description: The Mass Communication Internship Programme by the Department of Water Resources, River Development, and Ganga Rejuvenation. The intern program will offer work culture and environment knowledge about the functioning of the Government and its departments in information, education, and communication.

student.name: Agri-Clinics And Agri-Business Centres Scheme
student.description: A welfare scheme by the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare was launched in 2002. AC&ABC aims at agricultural development, supplementing the efforts of public extension by providing extension and other services to farmers either on a payment basis or free of cost as per the business model.

student.name: Financial Aid For Providing Hostel Facility To Transgender Students
student.description: The scheme “Financial Aid for Providing Hostel Facility to Transgender Students” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala.

student.name: Award of Opportunity Cost to Parents of Scheduled Caste Girl Students
student.description: The objective of the scheme is to financially assist the SC parents for sending school their daughters who otherwise earn livelihood for the family engaging themselves in menial labour.

student.name: Post-Matric Scholarships for Scheduled Castes Students- Gujarat
student.description: The objective of the scheme is to appreciably increase the Gross Enrolment Ratio of SC students in higher education with a focus on those from the poorest households, by providing financial assistance at post-matriculation or postsecondary stage to enable them to complete their education.

student.name: Mukhyamantri Divyangjan Sashaktikaran Chhatra Yojana
student.description: "Mukhyamantri Divyangjan Sashaktikaran Chhatra Yojana" aims to provide comprehensive support to the disabled community in Bihar. A significant component of this scheme involves the distribution of battery-operated tricycles to beneficiaries to enhance their mobility and independence.

student.name: Kanya Saksharta Protsahan Yojana (For Scheduled Caste Girls)
student.description: Under this scheme, Scheduled Caste girls who pass the Class 10th and enroll in the Class 11th at a government or recognized institution and study regularly are given an incentive of ₹3,000/- per year.

student.name: Scholarship for Children of Deceased/Disabled/Retired Government Employees
student.description: The objective of this scheme is to motivate children of government employees who are deceased, retired, or disabled. Under this scheme, scholarships ranging from ₹50/- to ₹250/- are provided to children of these government employees who are studying in Class 1 to Class 12 in government schools.

student.name: Mukhyamantri Vidyarthi Protsahan Yojana
student.description: Under this scheme, an incentive amount is provided to minority students who pass the Intermediate/Matriculation/Fauquania/Maulvi examinations with first-class marks from the Bihar School Examination Board/Bihar Madrasa Education Board.

student.name: Training Centre for Specially-Abled Persons
student.description: The Training Centre for Specially-Abled Persons scheme offers vocational courses for specially-abled individuals, funded by the Government of Mizoram. The centers provide courses in tailoring for females and tailoring/shoemaking for males, with a monthly stipend of ₹250 for the training period.

student.name: Haryana State Meritorious Incentive Scheme for the Students Who Attain First Position in CBSE Annual Exams in the State
student.description: The scheme "Haryana State Meritorious Incentive Scheme" aims to encourage meritorious students who attain top positions in CBSE annual exams by providing them with financial incentives. Selected students receive an annual incentive covering their school/college fees up to their graduation.

student.name: Free Books Scheme for Scheduled Caste Students Pursuing Higher Education
student.description: The scheme was launched by the Department of Higher Education, Govt. of Haryana, on 1st July, 2008, with the aim of attracting SC students towards higher education. Under this scheme, SC students studying in govt. colleges will receive ₹2,000/- per annum for purchasing books.

student.name: Consolidated Stipend Scheme for Scheduled Caste Students Pursuing Higher Education
student.description: The scheme was launched by the Department of Higher Education, Government of Haryana, on 1st July, 2008, with the aim of attracting SC students towards higher education. Under this scheme, SC students studying in Govt. Colleges will receive a stipend of ₹1,000 per month.

student.name: Devnarayan Pratibhawan Chhatra Protsahan Yojana
student.description: The scheme aims to encourage academic excellence among students belonging to Special Backward Classes (SBC). It provides an annual financial incentive of ₹3,500/- to students pursuing education from Class 10th to postgraduate level in government schools or colleges.

student.name: Incentive Scheme for Meritorious Students of Economically Backward Class of General Category
student.description: Under this scheme, a one-time financial assistance of ₹15,000/- along with a certificate of appreciation is awarded to top 100 meritorious students of Class 10 & each stream of Class 12, who are listed in state-level merit list of Rajasthan Board of Secondary Education, Ajmer.

student.name: Research Fellowship Scheme for Scheduled Tribe Candidates
student.description: The scheme aims to support the Scheduled Tribe candidates from Scheduled Areas and the Saharia Area of Rajasthan to register themselves as research scholars with all recognized Central/State/Deemed/Private University located in any part of India for pursuing Ph.D. Programme.

student.name: Dr. Bhimrao Ambedkar Sanskrit Scholarship Scheme for Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe Students
student.description: The scheme aims to encourage and motivate students belonging to Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) communities who are studying in Sanskrit schools from Class 6 to Class 12 in Uttarakhand by providing financial scholarships to support their education.

student.name: Financial Incentive to SC Meritorious Boy Students
student.description: The scheme provides a financial incentive of ₹5,000/- to the meritorious boys from Scheduled Castes who have secured 80% or above marks in the HSSLC examination. The objective is to motivate and encourage SC students to pursue higher studies and bring them at par with the rest.

student.name: Pandit Deendayal Educational Excellence Award
student.description: The scheme aims to promote academic excellence among schools & students by providing cash awards to the best-performing schools at the high school & intermediate levels at the state level, as well as to students securing top positions in Uttarakhand Board High School & Intermediate Examinations.

student.name: Mukhya Mantrir Nijut Moina Aasoni
student.description: The scheme aims to support ten lakh girl students with financial grants as admission incentives for Higher Secondary, Graduation, and Post-Graduation studies to eradicate child marriage. It benefits unmarried girl students domiciled in Assam with monthly financial assistance.

student.name: Swanath Scholarship Scheme
student.description: The scheme aims to provide financial support to diploma and graduate-level students who are orphans, whose parents or either parent has died due to COVID-19, or who are dependents of martyrs from the military or central armed forces.

student.name: Seemant Parvatiya Janpad Baal Vigyan Mahotsav
student.description: It aims to provide students from classes 6 to 12 of government/non-government schools in six border districts of the state (Uttarkashi, Chamoli, Rudraprayag, Champawat, Bageshwar,Pithoragarh) with an opportunity to participate in science competitions & to promote scientific awareness in these areas.

student.name: “Ishan Uday” Special Scholarship Scheme For North Eastern Region
student.description: A scholarship scheme by the Ministry of Education and the University Grants Commission. Taking special interest with regard to the promotion of higher education in the NER, i.e. for improving the GER, promoting higher education, and encouraging children belonging to the economically weaker sections.

student.name: Mukhya Mantri Vidyarthi Kalyan Yojana
student.description: The objective of the scheme is to provide financial assistance to school and college students in the form of scholarships of upto ₹2,400/- per month to students from IRDP/BPL Families bonafide in Himachal Pradesh, and pursuing their study in Govt / Govt Aided Institutions in Himachal Pradesh.

student.name: Thakur Sen Negi Utkrisht Chatravriti Yojana
student.description: The objective of this scheme is to provide scholarships of ₹11,000/-per annum to the top 100 Girls and top 100 Boys students belonging to the Tribal Community of Himachal Pradesh who have passed Class 10th and presently studying in a recognized institution within or outside the State.

student.name: Scheme of Internship of Students to DRDO Labs/Estts for Project Training
student.description: The scheme aims to provide an opportunity and exposure to future graduates and postgraduates in Engineering and General Sciences to the research and development activities carried out by DRDO in state-of-the-art technology in fields relevant to the defense sector.

student.name: NEET/JEE Coaching For Tribal Students
student.description: NEET/JEE Coaching for tribal students scheme launched in Rajasthan by Tribal Area Development in the year 2001.

student.name: Financial Assistance to ST Students under "Gagan Bharari Shiksha Yojana"
student.description: Under the scheme "Gagan Bharari Shiksha Yojana", financial assistance of upto ₹ 15,000/- is provided to the Scheduled Tribe Students as the maintenance allowance given under the Post Matric Scholarship is inadequate to meet the expenses on food & travel.

student.name: One Time Financial Support for Economically Weaker Meritorious ST Students
student.description: The "One Time Financial Support for Economically Weaker Meritorious ST Students" scheme aims to provide financial assistance ₹1,00,000/- to the economically weaker meritorious schedule tribe students to pursue different professional & other courses in government-recognized institutes/colleges.

student.name: Bihar State Alpsankhyak Awasiya Vidyalaya Yojana
student.description: The objective of this scheme is to provide quality education to meritorious students from minority communities in the state and to make them mentally, physically, socially, and emotionally capable, fostering their all-round development in residential schools.

student.name: Educational Assistance to the Children of the Registered Worker Studying in 11th and 12th Standard
student.description: The "Educational Assistance" scheme provides financial support of ₹10,000 per year to children of registered construction workers who are studying in the 11th or 12th standard. The benefit applies to a maximum of two children per worker and extends to the wife of a registered male worker.

student.name: Educational Assistance for Degree Course
student.description: The Educational Assistance for Degree Course scheme provides financial assistance to children of registered construction workers pursuing their undergraduate degree (1st, 2nd, 3rd, and 4th year, if applicable). This benefit is also extended to the wife of a registered male worker.

student.name: Educational Assistance to the 10th to 12th Students
student.description: The Educational Assistance to the 10th to 12th Students scheme was launched by the Maharashtra Building And Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (MBOCWW), Labour Department Maharashtra.

student.name: Hon’ble Chief Minister’s Merit Scholarship Scheme-Sikkim
student.description: This scheme aims to provide free education up to the Graduation level to talented and deserving children from underprivileged families with an opportunity to study in reputed public schools both within and outside the state.

student.name: Hostel Rent Assistance Scheme for Tribal Students
student.description: The scheme provides financial assistance towards hostel rent for Scheduled Tribe students pursuing undergraduate and postgraduate courses in government colleges, who are staying in rented accommodation for the purpose of regular studies.

student.name: Rani Laxmi Bai Self-Defense Training
student.description: The scheme aims to provide free self-defense skills training (including judo, karate, martial arts, and boxing) to girl students in upper primary and secondary schools across Uttarakhand.

student.name: Promotion of Tiatr Through Academic Activities
student.description: The scheme aims to foster interest in Tiatr among students and enthusiasts through academic activities like talks, symposia, seminars, and workshops. It covers activity expenditures, honorarium for resource persons, and provides materials, benefiting educational institutions and individuals.

student.name: Smt. Uma Gupta Fellowship for M.Sc. Agriculture (Genetics & Plant Breeding) Students
student.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance and encourage meritorious students pursuing the M.Sc. Agriculture (Genetics & Plant Breeding) course by offering a fellowship of ₹1000/- for three semesters, thereby reducing their economic burden.

student.name: Development and Dissemination of e-Content
student.description: The scheme aims to provide free, high-quality e-learning materials to students of classes 9 to 12, covering subjects such as Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, English, etc. in collaboration with reputed ed-tech platforms.

student.name: Technical Education Scholarship Scheme For Degree Level
student.description: The Scholarships will be awarded to the P.G. Courses/Degree Courses level Courses in Engineering/Technology including Architecture or any other equivalent course approved by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE).

student.name: Post Graduate Scholarship for Ph.D./LLM Students
student.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to students of Assam pursuing PhD and LLM degree courses in recognized institutions. It benefits academically meritorious students who have obtained at least 55% marks in their last qualifying examination and are domiciled in Assam.

student.name: Scholarship/Merit Scholarship Schools SC/ST/OBC/Min (Studying in College/Technical/ Professional Institutions)
student.description: The scheme provides monthly financial aid to SC/ST/OBC/Minority students pursuing higher education. Benefits include scholarships for hostellers and day scholars, with eligibility based on academic performance and income criteria.

student.name: Mukhyamantri Vidyarthi Pratibha Yojna
student.description: This scheme is to provide scholarships to the students studying in classes 9th to 12th for enabling them to bear the expenses required for their studies.

student.name: Assistance for Purchasing Study Materials for Medical and Engineering Students from Socially and Educationally Backward Classes
student.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to students from socially and educationally backward classes pursuing medical and engineering diploma or degree courses to support their education. Under this scheme, eligible students are provided a one-time grant for purchasing study materials.

student.name: Post Graduate Scholarship for GATE/GPAT Qualified Students for Pursuing M.Tech/M.E./M.Pharm-UGC
student.description: University Grants Commission is implementing the scheme of Post Graduate Scholarship for GATE/GPAT Qualified Students for Pursuing M.Tech/M.E./M.Pharm courses (including integrated dual degree courses).

student.name: Pre-Matric Scholarship
student.description: Under this scheme to improve the educational status of students belonging to Other Backward Classes (OBC) by providing fee reimbursement and financial assistance, thereby promoting access to education and supporting their social and economic upliftment.

student.name: Economic Help To Tribal Girls For Higher Education (College Level)
student.description: Rajasthan Government started a scholarship for tribal girls higher education (College Level) In the year 1994-95.

student.name: Economic Help To Tribal Girls For Higher Education (Class 11 & 12)
student.description: Rajasthan Government started a scholarship for tribal girls higher education (Class 11 & 12) In the year 2011.

student.name: Saphalam Scheme For Transgender Students Pursuing Professional Courses
student.description: The scheme “Saphalam Scheme for Transgender Students Pursuing Professional Courses” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala.

student.name: Nirman Shramik Kalyan Yojana
student.description: The scheme “Nirman Shramik Kalyan Yojana” was launched by the Labour and Employee's State Insurance Department, Government of Odisha. The objective of the scheme is to extend financial assistance to eligible building and other construction workers for continuing education of their wards/children.

student.name: BPL Scholarship for College Students
student.description: The scheme “BPL Scholarship For College Students” was implemented by the Department of Higher Education, Government of Chhattisgarh since 2005 to provide financial assistance to meritorious students from BPL category families to meet a part of their day-to-day expenses while pursuing higher studies.

student.name: Scheme for Reimbursement of Educational Expenses to Hearing Impaired Students Pursuing Special Higher Education Courses Inside or Outside the Union Territory of Puducherry
student.description: This scheme was introduced by the  Dept of Social Welfare, Govt of Puducherry to provide financial assistance to Hearing Impaired and Visually Impaired students for pursuing their special higher studies. Through this scheme, the reimbursement of the entire fee is granted from the first year onwards.

student.name: Food Bill Assistance to SC Students
student.description: The food bill assistance at the rate of ₹1,500/- per month (Up to 10 Months) will be granted to the Scheduled Caste (SC) students admitted in college-affiliated hostels.

student.name: Dr. Baba Saheb Ambedkar Loan for Higher Study in Foreign Countries
student.description: Under this scheme, a loan of ₹15 lakhs is provided at an interest rate of 4% to offer financial assistance to bright career students of the Scheduled Castes who cannot go abroad for higher studies due to the their poor economic condition.

student.name: Special Scholarship to Students Belonging to the Most Backward Castes among Scheduled Castes (Vandaniya Sant Shri Vasiyadada Scholarship)
student.description: This scheme offers special scholarships to students from the Most Backward Castes among the Scheduled Castes category who are enrolled in Standards I to X.

student.name: Swami Vivekananda Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme (Class 11th & 12th)
student.description: Under this scheme, scholarships are provided to students studying in Class 11 and Class 12 in government schools, whose annual family income is less than ₹1,00,000/- and who belong to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.

student.name: Bharati Scheme for Education - Post-Graduation
student.description: The "Bharati Scheme for Education - Post-Graduation (Any course of 2 Years or more duration, after Graduation (10+2+3) will be considered under this category, i.e., M.Sc, M.Com, M.A., M.Tech, M.D., M.Pharm, MBA, etc.)" encourages Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh to continue their education.

student.name: Bharati Scheme for Master’s Degree Overseas
student.description: The "Bharati Scheme for Master’s Degree Overseas" aims to provide financial assistance to Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh, who secured admission for any master's degree course overseas in reputed institutions for the year or students pursuing part of their course abroad.

student.name: Chhatrawas (Scheduled Caste)
student.description: Launched in 1966-67, the scheme "Chhatrawas" is a welfare scheme by the Scheduled Caste Welfare Department, Govt of MP. The scheme provides free residential facilities in Scheduled Caste hostels for boys and girls studying in educational institutions.

student.name: Eklavya Prashikshan Yojana
student.description: The "Eklavya Prashikshan Yojana" by the Directorate of Higher Education, Government of Jharkhand, aims to provide free coaching and financial aid for competitive exam preparation. Eligible students receive free coaching and ₹2,500/- monthly assistance during the coaching period.

student.name: State Merit Scholarship to Under Graduate Girls Students
student.description: "Merit Scholarship to Undergraduate Girl Students"  aims to promote higher education among meritorious girl students by providing a scholarship amount of ₹3,000 per annum to 10 eligible girl students per government college who have excelled in their 10+2 examination.

student.name: Financial Assistance to SC Students Appearing in the Mains Examination of UPSC
student.description: The scheme provides financial grants to the SC students for appearing in the UPSC Mains Exam. An amount upto ₹50,000/- is provided to each beneficiary, subject to budget provision. The assistance is extended depending on the applicant satisfying the eligibility criteria and the documents submitted.

student.name: Mukhyamantri Bhavishyalakshi Kaushalya Vikas Yojana
student.description: The scheme aims to develop skills of youth through new age technology-based courses in manufacturing, service and other sectors to create skilled manpower in the state. It provides quality training and certification to Gujarat domicile students.

student.name: Scheme for Goa Rajya Vidnyanik Puraskar
student.description: The scheme aims to introduce and recognize Goan Innovators/Scientists who have achieved excellence in the fields of Science, Technology, and Innovation. The scheme rewards eligible Students, Startups, Innovators, Research Faculty, and Scientists with three annual cash awards of ₹2,00,000/- each.

student.name: Dr. Dhyanpal Singh Memorial Award (for Final-Year Undergraduate Students of All Colleges)
student.description: The scheme aims to support and encourage meritorious students by providing them with monetary assistance of ₹30,000/- in their final year of undergraduate studies, thereby reducing financial burdens.

student.name: Supply Free Laptops to Visually Impaired Students Pursuing Higher Education
student.description: The scheme aims to provide free laptops to visually impaired students pursuing courses. The scheme is provisioned for providing laptops to the visually impaired students to pursue professional courses after XII, under-graduate and post-graduate courses.

student.name: State Horticulture Scholarship
student.description: The “State Horticulture Scholarship” is a scholarship scheme, launched by the Department of Horticulture, Government of Nagaland to provide scholarship/stipend to students who are pursuing B.Sc. (Horticulture) M.Sc. (Horticulture) Ph.D. (Horticulture) courses.

student.name: BSR Fellowship Scheme for Science Students - UGC
student.description: The objective of the scheme is to provide opportunities to meritorious candidates to undertake advanced studies and research leading to Ph.D. degrees in sciences.

student.name: Special Incentive Assistance to Socially and Educationally Backward Class Trainees for Pre-preparation of Competitive Examination
student.description: Under this scheme, to provide financial assistance of up to ₹20,000/- to socially and educationally backward class students preparing for state and central government job exams.

student.name: Pragati Scholarship Scheme For Girl Students (Technical Degree)
student.description: A scholarship scheme by MoE for Girl Students admitted in 1st or 2nd Year of the technical degree course in an AICTE approved institution. The benefits are provided for max. 4 years for first-year admitted students and max. 3 years for second-year admitted students through lateral entry.

student.name: Mukhyamantri Yuba Yogayog Yojana-Special
student.description: The scheme aims to digitally empower students and help them leverage the advantages of an interconnected world.

student.name: Shaikshanik Chatravriti Yojana
student.description: Under MP Shram Kalyan Shaikshanik Chatravriti Yojana, scholarships will be provided to the students in the state to continue their education.

student.name: Summer Internship Scheme Of DGFT
student.description: The Summer Internship Scheme by the Directorate General of Foreign Trade is for the interaction of the officers of the Directorate with young scholars and researchers with brilliant academic backgrounds from reputed academic institutions pursuing Studies/Research in Public Policy/Economics/Law etc.

student.name: Award of Adhoc Merit Grant (Special Incentive) to Scheduled Caste Students
student.description: The scheme is implemented as a sort of encouragement for the Scheduled Caste students who secure 65% or above marks in matriculation/SSLC Board of Examinations.

student.name: Reimbursement of Tuition Fees to the Wards of Ex-Servicemen/Widows (Sainik Welfare)
student.description: "Reimbursement of Tuition Fees to the Wards of Ex-Servicemen/Widows (Sainik Welfare)" is a Welfare Scheme by the Dept of Sainik Welfare, Puducherry. The wards of the ESM / Widows of ESM studying in Government-recognized Institutions are provided reimbursement of their tuition fees.

student.name: Sikkim Grant of Scholarship to Students with Disabilities Scheme
student.description: The Government of Sikkim has introduced the Sikkim Grant of Scholarship to Students with Disabilities Scheme, 2014. This scheme aims to provide financial assistance to differently-abled students to help them secure academic education and technical or professional training.

student.name: Bharati Scheme for Education Professional Education Course - Chartered Accountancy Intern
student.description: The "Bharati Scheme for Education Professional Education Course - Chartered Accountancy Intern (IPCC)" encourages Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh to continue their education. Under this scheme, financial assistance for the academic year will be granted to eligible poor Brahmin students.

student.name: Mukhyamantri Anusuchit Jati Evam Anusuchit Janjati Chhatrawas Anudan Yojana
student.description: The “Mukhyamantri Anusoochit Jaati Evam Anusoochit Janajati Chhatrawas Anudan Yojana” was launched by the SC and ST Welfare Department, Government of Bihar aims to provide financial assistance in every month to the students studying in Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe hostels.

student.name: Schooling Education (JKBOCWWB)
student.description: The scheme "Schooling Education" by the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Dept., J&K, provides financial assistance for the education of children of registered construction workers, from Class Nursery/1st to 5th, and from Class 6th to 8th.

student.name: State Scholarship of Class 1st to 5th (Scheduled Caste)
student.description: Launched in 1966-67, the "State Scholarship of Class 1st to 5th" scheme is a welfare initiative by the Scheduled Caste Welfare Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme provides a scholarship amount of ₹200/- to Scheduled Caste students, which is disbursed until 31st October every year.

student.name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for Backward Class Students
student.description: The scheme "Pre-Matric Scholarship for Backward Class Students" aims to provide financial assistance to backward class students studying at the pre-matriculation level in India. Monthly scholarships of varying amounts are provided to eligible students.

student.name: Civil Service Incentive Scheme (Madhya Pradesh)
student.description: The "Civil Service Incentive Scheme (Madhya Pradesh)" scheme by the Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department of the Government of Madhya Pradesh provides an incentive amount to encourage SC/ST candidates to appear in Civil Services Examinations.

student.name: Ambedkar International Scholarship - SC
student.description: The "Ambedkar International Scholarship - SC" Scheme by the Rajasthan Social Justice and Empowerment Department provides financial assistance to meritorious SC students for pursuing Ph.D. studies in foreign universities in Humanities and Social Sciences.

student.name: Additional Financial Assistance for the Children of Persons Engaged in the Cleaning Profession
student.description: This scheme aims to provide additional financial assistance of ₹500/- per month to children of individuals working in cleaning/sanitation profession. This assistance is given to students already receiving a scholarship under Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme, ensuring their continued education.

student.name: Mukhyamantri Atyant Pichhda Varg Medhavriti Yojana
student.description: The scheme aims to provide ₹10,000/- as a one-time scholarship to extremely backward class students passing class 10th in the first division from the Bihar School Examination Board.

student.name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for the Students of Notified Tribes and Denotified Tribes Castes
student.description: The scheme supports school education by providing annual scholarships to students from Notified Tribes and Denotified Tribes studying in standards 1st to 10th.

student.name: Mukhyamantri Medhavi Chhatra Puraskar Yojana
student.description: The scheme aims to encourage academic excellence among students and provides cash prizes to those who secure first, second, or third positions in their graduation or post-graduation studies at the university level.

student.name: Vidhya Sadhana Yojana Gujarat
student.description: Under this scheme, free bicycles are gifted to girl students across Gujarat to reduce school dropouts among scheduled tribe girls after class 8.

student.name: Higher Education Assistance Scheme
student.description: The scheme aims to encourage children of workers and reduce financial burden of education through higher educational assistance. It provides ₹20,000/- for Bachelor of Medicine Bachelor of Surgery admission and ₹10,000/- for other graduate courses to merit students.

student.name: Ashramshalas (Grant in Aid)
student.description: The scheme aims to provide schooling, boarding and lodging to Scheduled Tribe students at a single location, enabling better employment opportunities and competitive equality with other sections of society. The scheme offers residential education with complete support including free education.

student.name: Establishment and Operation of STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics) Laboratories
student.description: The scheme aims to establish STEM laboratories in schools across Uttarakhand to provide students of classes 6 to 12 with hands-on science, technology, engineering, and mathematics learning opportunities and strengthen their practical understanding of scientific concepts.

student.name: Nishadraj Scholarship Scheme
student.description: The scheme aims to promote higher education among children of fisher families working in federation reservoirs by providing scholarships of up to ₹20,000 for technical courses and up to ₹10,000 for non-technical courses.

student.name: Uchcha Shiksha Protsahan Chhatravriti Yojana
student.description: The scheme aims to promote higher education among meritorious students by providing financial support to regular undergraduate and postgraduate students in government colleges and state universities of Uttarakhand.

student.name: Scholarship on Merit Basis
student.description: The scheme aims to encourage meritorious students pursuing undergraduate and postgraduate studies at Uttarakhand Sanskrit University by awarding scholarships to those who secure the highest marks in the relevant course, class, or subject.

student.name: Training Programme for Entrepreneurship Development in Biotechnology
student.description: The scheme aims to train youth in biotechnology and develop entrepreneurial skills by providing technical knowledge, mentorship, and guidance to help them initiate biotech startups.

student.name: Priyank Pathak Scholarship for B.Sc. Agriculture (4th Year) Students
student.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to meritorious students pursuing a B.Sc. in Agriculture who are in their fourth year of study, by awarding them a monthly scholarship of ₹ 800/- for one year.

student.name: Financial Assistance To Disabled Students Pursuing (10th, 11th, 12th Equivalent Exams)
student.description: The scheme “Financial Assistance to Disabled Students Pursuing (10th, 11th, 12th Equivalent Exams)” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala.

student.name: Scheduled Tribe Dashamottar Scholarship (Starting from Class 11) - Uttarakhand
student.description: The scheme aims to support Scheduled Tribe students of Uttarakhand from Class 11th onwards by providing financial assistance for their education, including tuition fees and other academic expenses.

student.name: Study in India Programme
student.description: The Study in India Programme aims to promote India as a global education hub by offering centralized admissions, quality education, and visa support to international students.”

student.name: Saraswati Sadhana Yojana to Economically Backward Class Girls studying in Class 9
student.description: Under this scheme, free bicycle assistance is provided to economically backward class girls studying in Class 9 in government and grant-in-aid schools across Gujarat.

student.name: Top Class Education For Scheduled Caste Students
student.description: The Scheme aims at recognising and promoting quality education amongst students belonging to SCs by providing financial support. The Scheme will cover SC students for pursuing studies beyond the 12th class.

student.name: Special Education And Vocational Training Through Government Institutions
student.description: In this scheme, Special Education is provided to children with disability between the age group of 6 to 18 years in the Government Special Schools and Vocational Training is provided to special children above the age of 18 years. Only citizens who are permanent residents of Maharashtra are eligible.

student.name: Award to Scheduled Castes Students Who Secured Top Ranks at State and District Level in Standard X & XII (Sahuji Maharaj Assistance)
student.description: Under the scheme, Scheduled Castes Category students who have Secured Top Ranks at State and District Levels in Gujarat in Standard X & XII are given cash prizes. Cash prizes are awarded to the first three students who secure the highest ranks in both Standard X and Standard XII across all streams.

student.name: Education Loan Scheme- Credit Line 2 for Minority Community
student.description: The scheme is implemented by Haryana Backward Classes & Economically Weaker Sections Kalyan Nigam, aims to provide education loans to sections of minority population on the basis of "Creamy Layer". These loans support pursuing general/professional/technical courses at graduate levels or higher.

student.name: Sanction of Motorized Three Wheeler to Orthopedically Challenged
student.description: The "Sanction of Motorized Three Wheeler to Orthopedically Challenged" scheme aims to provide motorized three wheelers to orthopedically challenged persons. This initiative ensures that eligible individuals receive support to enhance their mobility and independence.

student.name: Educational Assistance for Government Recognised Diploma Courses
student.description: The Educational Assistance for Government Recognised Diploma Courses scheme is designed to financially support children of registered construction workers, providing ₹20,000 per year for diploma courses and ₹25,000/- per year for postgraduate diploma courses.

student.name: Financial Incentive to SC Meritorious Girls Students
student.description: The scheme provides financial incentives worth ₹5,000/- to the meritorious girls from Scheduled Castes who have secured 80% or above marks in the HSLC examination. To objective is to motivate and encourage SC students to pursue higher studies and bring them at par with the rest.

student.name: Swami Vivekanand Scholarship for Technical Diploma in Professional Courses
student.description: The scheme aims to support ST students enrolled in ITIs and recognized training institutes by providing a monthly stipend of ₹400 for a duration of 12 months.

student.name: Solar Based Hydroponic System
student.description: The system aims to develop and promote an innovative solar-based hydroponic system that integrates solar energy with modern greenhouse technology and mobile application support.

student.name: RIMC Scholarship (Military Scholarship)
student.description: The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹1,000/- per month to students from Uttarakhand who are pursuing their education at the Rashtriya Indian Military College (RIMC).

student.name: Dr. Y.V. Singh Award for Best Thesis in Vegetable Science
student.description: The scheme aims to recognize and encourage students for their outstanding research work in the field of Vegetable Science. Under this, a cash award of ₹20,000/- is provided annually to the selected students.

student.name: Healthy Lifestyle for School Children through Ayurvidya-AYUSH
student.description: The scheme aims to promote and develop a healthy lifestyle among school children through the principles and practices of AYUSH. Under this initiative, free AYUSH-based health services and medicines are provided to children registered in government schools.

student.name: Prime Minister’s Scholarship Scheme for the Wards of Central Armed Police Forces & Assam Rifles
student.description: The scheme aims to encourage higher technical and professional education for dependent wards and widows of CAPFs/Assam Rifles personnel martyred during Terror/Naxal attacks. Through this scheme, Financial support per year for girls and boys is provided.

student.name: NAMO E-Tablet Scheme
student.description: The Namo Tablet Yojana in Gujarat is a state-specific version of the central government's PM Tablet Scheme. The scheme was launched in 2017 by the Gujarat government and aims to distribute tablets to around 3.5 lakh students in the state.

student.name: Non-Governmental Aided Organization (Special Schools And Vocational Training Centre) For Disabled
student.description: In this scheme, Special Education is provided to children with disability who are below the age of 18 years. The child should be enrolled in a special school run by a Non-Governmental Aided Organization. Vocational Training is given to special children above the age of 18 years.

student.name: Vidya Scheme
student.description: Vidya scheme under the Education Department, Government of Arunachal Pradesh is a scheme where a girl child who is studying in Class VI is provided with an incentive of Rs. 10,000/- as fixed deposit in SBI in the name of individual girl child till she completes Class – XII.

student.name: Grant to Meritorious Wards of Ex-Servicemen in Academics (Lump Sum Grant)
student.description: Through this scheme, the academically meritorious wards of the ESM / Widows of ESM who have secured the top three ranks in academics are provided with lump sum grants of upto ₹18,000. The application should be submitted within three months from the date of publication of the result.

student.name: Hostel Scheme for Disabled Students
student.description: The objective of the scheme is to provide hostel facilities to such disabled students who are taking admission in class 11th and above on a regular basis and in case hostel facilities are not available in the schools/colleges, by renting private buildings.

student.name: Grant of Stipends to the Physically Handicapped Students
student.description: "Grant of Stipends to the Physically Handicapped Students" scheme aims to improve the educational status of handicapped children studying in standards 1st to 10th. Monthly stipends (₹200 to ₹300) are provided to parents who are unable to send their children to school due to economic reasons.

student.name: Reimbursement of the MS-CIT Course
student.description: The Reimbursement of the MS-CIT Course scheme was launched by the Maharashtra Building And Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (MBOCWW), Labour Department Maharashtra.

student.name: Jharkhand State Scheduled Tribe Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme
student.description: The "Jharkhand State Scheduled Tribe Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme" aims to promote education and reduce dropout rates among Scheduled Tribe students. It provides financial assistance to students from Class 1st to 10th who are studying in government schools in Jharkhand.

student.name: e-Adhigam Scheme - Free Tablet
student.description: This scheme was launched with objective of promoting digital education & providing online learning to children from poor & needy families, proving to be a boon for them. Under this scheme, free tablets are provided to students, enabling access to state-of-the-art software & learning materials.

student.name: Free Passport Scheme
student.description: The scheme aims to facilitate higher education and global career opportunities for students by covering the cost of passport issuance. Under this scheme, final-year UG and PG students in government colleges receive reimbursement for passport application fees.

student.name: Financial assistance to Meritorious Scheduled Tribe Students who have Passed First Division from Board and University
student.description: The objective of the scheme is to motivate meritorious Scheduled Tribe students of Rajasthan by providing one-time financial assistance of ₹3,500/- to those pursuing regular college courses and achieving first division.

student.name: Mukhyamantri Hunar Vikas Yojana- Higher Education/Technical Education
student.description: Under this scheme, the State Government provides financial assistance to eligible boys and girls enrolled under the Palanhar Scheme to pursue higher or technical education, based on their qualifications and interest in employment-oriented courses.

student.name: Subsidy for Battery Operated 2-Wheelers
student.description: This scheme is introduced by the Climate Change Department, Gujarat, aims to promote renewable energy and clean transportation by providing a subsidy to students for the purchase of battery-operated two-wheelers from authorized manufacturers.

student.name: Loans to Tribal Students for Higher Studies Abroad
student.description: The scheme's purpose is to offer financial assistance to tribal students for higher education abroad. It provides a loan of up to ₹15,00,000 at a 4% annual interest rate.

student.name: Weekly Iron Folic Acid Tablets Free Distribution - Uttarakhand
student.description: This aims to improve the health and nutritional status of school-going children by preventing and reducing iron deficiency anemia. Under this scheme, teachers distribute free iron and folic acid tablets every Monday to all students enrolled in government & government-aided schools across the state.

student.name: Snehapoorvam Scheme
student.description: Kerala Government has launched a noble initiative “Snehapoorvam Scheme” to provide financial assistance to orphans who are living in the family, with their relatives, friends, or the support of the community under the Social Welfare Department implemented through Social Security Mission.

student.name: Attendance Scholarship Scheme for Girl Students with Disabilities of Rural Areas
student.description: The objective of this scheme is to uplift the status of the girls with disabilities in the rural areas & also to make them reliant by paying incentives in the form of Attendance Scholarship to these girls.

student.name: Post Matric Scholarship for Students belonging to Scheduled Caste Community
student.description: The scheme "Post Matric Scholarship for Students belonging to Scheduled Caste Community" was launched by the Mizoram Scholarship Board, Government of Mizoram. The objective of the scheme is to provide financial assistance to students belonging to Scheduled Castes pursuing post-matriculation.

student.name: Assistance for Purchase of Educational Instruments for Medical, Engineering and Technical Diploma Courses
student.description: The scheme aims to provide annual financial assistance of ₹15,000, ₹10,000, and ₹5,000 for ST students in medical, engineering, and diploma courses, respectively.

student.name: Post-Matric Scholarship for the Students Belonging to Scheduled Tribe for Studies in India
student.description: The scheme aims to enable eligible Scheduled Tribe students to undertake quality education from the post-matriculation to the post-graduate level. It provides financial assistance covering compulsory course fees and monthly stipends for intended beneficiaries, including hostellers and day scholars.

student.name: Dr. Ambedakar Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Post-Matric Scholarships for the Economically Backward Class (EBC) Students
student.description: The objective of the scheme is to provide financial assistance to the Economically Backward Class (EBC) students studying at the post-matriculation or post-secondary stage to enable them to complete their education. These scholarships shall be available for studies in India only.

student.name: State Pre-metric Scholarship For Disabled
student.description: The "State Pre-metric Scholarship for Disabled" is a scheme by the Department of Social Justice & Special Assistance, Govt. of Maharashtra. The objective of this scheme is to encourage students with disability (SwDs) to pursue education. This scheme is 100% funded by the Govt. of Maharashtra.

student.name: Laptop Scheme for Meritorious Students of SC/ST
student.description: The scheme "Laptop Scheme for Meritorious Students of SC/ST" by the Department of Information Technology, Electronics and Communication, Government of Goa aims to provide laptops to the meritorious students who were born in Goa.

student.name: Cash Prizes Scheme (BBOCWWB)
student.description: "Cash Prizes Scheme" was launched by the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. Students securing 60% or more in the 10th and 12th exams receive cash prizes of up to ₹25,000/- per year. Upto two children of a registered construction worker are eligible.

student.name: Vishesh Yogyajan Chhatravriti Yojana
student.description: Under this scheme, scholarships and tuition fee reimbursement are provided to specially-abled students belonging to families with an annual income not exceeding ₹2.00 lakh, who are regularly studying in government and recognized educational institutions.

student.name: Vijayamritham Scheme - Cash Award For Meritorious Children With Disabilities
student.description: The scheme “Vijayamritham Scheme - Cash Award for Meritorious Children with Disabilities” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala.

student.name: Educational Assistance To Children Of Prisoners (Pursuing Professional Course)
student.description: The scheme “Educational Assistance to Children of Prisoners (Pursuing Professional Course)” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala.

student.name: Research (PhD) Scholarship for SC/ST Students
student.description: The scheme is implemented by the Higher Education Department, Govt. of Madhya Pradesh, offers financial support for PhD research work to SC/ST students. Eligible students receive ₹16,000/- per month, totaling ₹1,92,000/- per year, which is directly transferred to their bank accounts.

student.name: Exposure Visits To The Assam Legislative Assembly
student.description: The Exposure Visits to the Assam Legislative Assembly scheme was introduced in the budget speech during the first Budget Session in July 2016. It aims to provide an opportunity for Political Science students in Assam to visit and observe the functioning of the Assam Legislative Assembly.

student.name: New Akanksha Scheme (Higher Education Loan) (GGDC)
student.description: This scheme provides educational loans of up to ₹15.00 lakh to students belonging to the Rabari and Bharwad castes from socially and educationally backward classes for pursuing higher professional courses such as medical, dental, engineering, pharmacy, etc.

student.name: Financial Assistance for Education to the Wards of Beedi/Cine/IOMC/LSDM Workers – Pre & Post-Matric
student.description: The scheme aims to fund the pre-matric and post-matric education of wards belonging to specific mine, beedi, and cine workers. It provides annual scholarships between ₹1,000/- and ₹25,000/- based on the student's academic level and course type.

student.name: Chief Minister's Overseas Scholarship Scheme for Minorities
student.description: The scheme “Chief Minister's Overseas Scholarship Scheme for Minorities” was launched by the Minorities Welfare Department, Government of Telangana in the year 2015-16 for students belonging to the Minority community to pursue Higher Studies in Post Graduate Courses in Foreign Universities.

student.name: Educational Assistance (A.B.O.C.W.W.B)
student.description: The “Educational Assistance” scheme was started by the Assam Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Welfare Department, Government of Assam to provide educational assistance to the children of the registered construction workers who are studying in Class I to Post Graduate.

student.name: Fellowship Scheme for M.Phil & Ph.D for Scheduled Caste Students (Maharaja Sayajirao Gayakvad Scholarship)
student.description: Under this scheme, Scheduled Caste students pursuing M.Phil and Ph.D. programs at any recognized universities will receive a monthly fellowship.

student.name: Educational Assistance for Medical and Engineering Courses
student.description: The Maharashtra Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board provides educational assistance to the children and spouses of registered construction workers pursuing medical and engineering degrees.

student.name: Girls Literacy Promotion Scheme for Class 11th
student.description: The "Girls Literacy Promotion Scheme Class 11th" by the Madhya Pradesh Government encourages girls from De-notified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic communities to study. Girls enrolling in Class 11 for the first time receive ₹3,000/-

student.name: Incentive Scheme in Civil Service (Chhattisgarh)
student.description: "Incentive Scheme in Civil Service"  aims to encourage bright and meritorious persons with disabilities to pass competitive exams in the field of civil services. Financial support is provided on passing the different stages of the Union/Chhattisgarh Public Service Commission Competitive Exams.

student.name: Kasturba Gandhi Special Term Deposit Receipt Scheme
student.description: Under this scheme, girls who pass Class 8 from Kasturba Gandhi Schools, secure at least 50% marks in Class 10, & enroll in Class 11 receive an STDR of ₹2,000/- (5-year term). Those who score 50% in Class 12 and enroll in graduation receive an STDR of ₹4,000/- (3-year term).

student.name: Pre-Examination Coaching Scheme
student.description: Under this scheme, free coaching and monthly scholarships are provided to eligible Scheduled Tribe students appearing for exams like IAS, PCS, engineering, medical, NDA, Group ‘C’ exams and other competitive examinations.

student.name: Maths Wizard & Spelling Genius - Uttarakhand
student.description: The scheme aims to enhance the interest of Class 5 students in Mathematics and English by organizing competitions at the school, block, district, and state levels, and encouraging them through rewards.

student.name: Ambedkar Social Innovation and Incubation Mission (ASIIM)
student.description: The scheme aims to boost entrepreneurship and innovation among SC students and SC differently-abled youth by providing focused support to translate innovative, technology-oriented business ideas into successful commercial enterprises. It offers equity funding and hand-holding support.

student.name: Post-Matric Scholarship To VJNT Students - Maharashtra
student.description: The Government of Maharashtra has introduced a scheme namely “Post-Matric Scholarship to VJNT students” to encourage the students of the Vimukta Jatis & Nomadic Tribes (VJNT) to undergo Post-Matric Courses.

student.name: Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Pre-matric Scholarship to Other Backward Classes (OBC) for Studies in India
student.description: "Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Pre-matric Scholarship to Other Backward Classes for Studies in India" is a Scholarship Scheme by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment. The objective is to award scholarships to school-going children of poorer OBC parents.

student.name: Agriculture Academic Studies Scheme
student.description: The objective of the Scheme is to sponsor students for undergoing 4 years of Graduate/degree courses in Agriculture/Horticulture against State quota seats in different Agriculture Universities in the country.

student.name: Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam Meritorious Students Incentive Scheme (Class 12th Passed)
student.description: The objective of the scheme is to motivate students from the lower economic backgrounds & support them economically. Under this scheme, cash award of ₹5,000/- is given to the students who have obtained the highest marks in the 12th class examination in government schools within their district.

student.name: Pratibha Yojana
student.description: This scheme provides financial assistance to Scheduled Tribe students who have passed national competitive examinations such as JEE, NEET, CLAT, AIIMS, and NDA, and have taken admission in IIT, NIT, NLU, MBBS (government colleges located in Madhya Pradesh), AIIMS or NDA.

student.name: Educational Assistance to the Children Studying in 1st to 7th and 8th to 10th Class
student.description: Under this scheme, educational assistance is provided of ₹2,500/- to the children studying in 1st to 7th standard and ₹5,000/- for the children studying in 8th to 10th standard of the registered construction worker. The scheme covers up to two children and the wife of a registered male worker.

student.name: Financial Assistance to SC Students for Pursuing Higher Studies Abroad
student.description: The scheme aims to support SC students for higher education abroad in fields like Engineering, Sciences, and Humanities. Benefits include up to ₹20,00,000/- for Ph.D. and ₹10,00,000 for Masters, with eligibility criteria including residency and income below ₹8,00,000/-.

student.name: Scholarship for Children of Ex-Servicemen for Vocational and Technical Education
student.description: Under this scheme, a scholarship of ₹15,000 per year is provided to the children of ex-servicemen, from the rank of Sepoy to Havildar, who are pursuing vocational & technical education & have not received scholarship assistance under the Prime Minister's Scholarship Scheme or from any other source.

student.name: Rajya Pratibha Khoj Pariksha Chhatravriti Yojana
student.description: Under this scheme, students are provided with scholarships or a one-time incentive for pursuing education up to the postgraduate level, after being selected through the State Talent Search Examination organized by the Board of Secondary Education Rajasthan, Ajmer.

student.name: 6% Interest Subsidy on Loans taken through Banks for Study Abroad
student.description: The scheme aims to reduce financial stress for ST students pursuing foreign education by providing up to ₹1,50,000 subsidy on interest for 3 years.

student.name: Sweekruti: Pre & Post Matric Scholarship
student.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to Transgender students studying at post matriculation or post-secondary stage to enable them to complete their education. These scholarships are available to transgender students  for studies in India only and awarded where the applicant is studying.

student.name: Pratibha Kiran Scholarship
student.description: Pratibha Kiran Scholarship has been launched by the Government of Madhya Pradesh. Through this scheme, scholarship will be provided every year to girl students living below the poverty line.

student.name: Mukhyamantri Medhavi Vidhyarti Yojana
student.description: The scheme aims to cover the tuition fees for meritorious students from Madhya Pradesh admitted to graduate courses. Financial assistance for admission and course fees is provided to eligible students through this scheme.

student.name: E-YUVA Scheme: BIRAC’s E-YUVA Fellows (for under graduate students)
student.description: BIRAC’s E-YUVA Fellows shall apply as a team and pursue their research work at their College/University labs and will also be allowed access to facilities at EYC and EYC Knowledge Partners as and when required.

student.name: Cash Award (A.B.O.C.W.W.B)
student.description: Cash awards shall be given to the three male & three female children of the registered construction workers in each district to those students who score 75% or above, 65% or less than 75%, and 55% or less than 65% mark in HSLC, AHM and H.S. examinations.

student.name: Coaching Assistance to Scheduled Castes Students for Preparation of Examination Like IIM, NIFT, NLU, CEPT
student.description: Under the scheme, financial assistance is provided to Students of the Scheduled Castes category who are preparing for examinations like IIM, NIFT, NLU, and CEPT.

student.name: Coaching Assistance Scheme for Preparation of Recruitment Exams for Scheduled Caste Students
student.description: The scheme provides financial assistance to Students of SC category who are preparing for all State Government and Central Government Recruitment Examinations of Class– 1, 2 & 3 conducted by UPSC/GPSC/State Commission/Bank/LIC/Subordinate Services Selection Board/District Panchayat Selection Board.

student.name: Jharkhand State Scheduled Caste Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme
student.description: The "Jharkhand State Scheduled Caste Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme" enhances the participation of Scheduled Caste students in pre-matric education and provides them with equal educational opportunities. The scheme aims to encourage students to continue their education and reduce dropout rates.......

student.name: Devnarayan Chhatra Cycle Scheme
student.description: The scheme provides free bicycles to girl students studying in Class 9 in government schools of Rajasthan who belong to the Special Backward Classes or specific communities such as Banjara, Baldia, Labana-Gadiya Lohar, Gadolia, Gujar Gurjar, Raiko, Rebari (Debasi).

student.name: Cash Amount for Encouraging Scheduled Tribe Sportsperson Students
student.description: Under this scheme, cash awards, certificates, and mementos are provided as a form of encouragement to Scheduled Tribe  sportsperson students who secure the top three positions in national and international sports events.

student.name: Right to Education Act 2009 - Uttarakhand
student.description: The scheme aims to provide free education, textbooks, uniforms, and the benefits of the PM Poshan Yojana to children up to Class 8 in private schools, targeting rural and urban children from weaker and deprived sections of Uttarakhand.

student.name: Samriddhi – Scheme for Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe Students for Setting Start-Ups
student.description: The scheme aims to provide opportunities for Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe students to design, launch, and run their own business or startup after formal education is completed or during their final year of education. The scheme provides a limited one-time financial assistance of ₹20,00,000/-.

student.name: USERC Establishment and Operation of Entrepreneurship Development Centers
student.description: The scheme aims to establish and operate Entrepreneurship Development Centers to provide structured training in entrepreneurship to students, women, and the general public of Uttarakhand.

student.name: Skill Development Programme- Uttarakhand
student.description: The scheme aims to enhance the employability and entrepreneurial skills of students, researchers, and farmers in the field of biotechnology, thereby promoting self-employment and entrepreneurship in emerging areas of the biotechnology sector.

student.name: Maharishi Balmiki Chatravriti Yojana
student.description: The scheme “Maharishi Balmiki Chatravriti Yojana” by the Dept. of Education, Govt. of Himachal Pradesh is a Scholarship Scheme for female students belonging to the Balmiki family. The beneficiary receives ₹ 18,000 per annum for college-level studies and professional courses.

student.name: Mukhiya Mantri Protsahan Yojna
student.description: The scheme “Mukhiya Mantri Protsahan Yojna” was launched by Dept. of Higher Education, H.P. during the year 2013-14. Under the scheme, an Incentive/Award will be given to students of the State who are selected and take admission for a degree course in any IIT/AIIMS/IIM/ISM Dhanbad/IISc Bangalore.

student.name: Varnam Scheme For Transgender Students Pursuing Distance Education Courses
student.description: The scheme “Varnam Scheme for Transgender Students Pursuing Distance Education Courses” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala to provide financial assistance to Transgender students pursuing Degree/Diploma/ PG courses through distance education.

student.name: Scholarship Scheme for Students Studying in Government Colleges
student.description: Under this scheme, scholarships of ₹3,000, ₹2,000, and ₹1,000 are provided annually to Science, Commerce, and Arts students to promote higher education.

student.name: SITARE-Appreciation Grant
student.description: SITARE-Appreciation Grant: Residential workshops called as Biotech Innovation Ignition School (BIIS) for undergraduate students are organized to provide hands-on technical training and mentorship for problem identification in 3-4 weeks duration.

student.name: Free Coaching for Students with Disabilities
student.description: A Scheme "Free Coaching for Students with Disabilities" was initiated by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities under the "Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment".

student.name: Special Scholarship to SC Students in Selected Schools
student.description: The scheme provides financial assistance to SC students studying in famous private selected schools i.e. Doon School, Dehradun; Sofiya School, Abu; Mayo school, Ajmer; Sainik School, Balachadi; Mahila Sainik School, Kherva; Dist. Mehsana, etc. will be paid actual fees upto limit of ₹50,000/- p.a.

student.name: Distribution of Free Books and Stationery to SC/ST Students
student.description: Under this scheme, free books and stationery are provided to Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe students who are pursuing undergraduate and postgraduate courses at government colleges. Eligible students receive books worth ₹1,500/- and stationery worth ₹500/- per year.

student.name: Merit Based Scholarship Scheme for Dhangar Students
student.description: "Merit Based Scholarship Scheme for Dhangar Students" by the Directorate of Social Welfare, Government of Goa aims to motivate students by providing merit-based awards and financial incentives (ranging from ₹5,000/- to ₹20,000/-) to high-performing students who are economically weak.

student.name: Valmiki Chhatravritti Yojana
student.description: The "Valmiki Chhatravritti Yojana" by the Department of Higher and Technical Education, Government of Jharkhand, aims to provide financial assistance and encouragement to orphaned and differently-abled students to pursue higher education and benefits include full tuition fee reimbursement.

student.name: Vocational Education Programme
student.description: The scheme aims to impart vocational education alongside academic learning to students of classes 9 to 12 in selected government secondary schools. Vocational education is offered in 08 sectors approved by the Government of India under the NSQF framework.

student.name: Son/Daughter Education Assistance (UKBOCWWB)
student.description: The scheme provides financial assistance to the children of registered building and construction workers pursuing education from Class I up to higher education or professional courses.

student.name: Chief Minister BC & EBC Chhatravas Anudan Yojana
student.description: The "Chief Minister BC & EBC Chhatravas Anudan Yojana", implemented by the Backward Classes and Extremely Backward Classes Welfare Department, provides financial assistance to students from Backward Classes (BC) and Extremely Backward Classes (EBC) residing in government hostels.

student.name: Scheduled Caste Dashamottar Scholarship (Starting from Class 11) - Uttarakhand
student.description: The scheme aims to support Scheduled Caste students of Uttarakhand from Class 11th onwards by providing financial assistance for their education, including tuition fees and other academic expenses.

student.name: Internship Programme for Women Students / Scholars / Social Activists / Teachers
student.description: The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched the Internship Programme aims to harness the talent of the trainees, giving them an opportunity to work towards bringing more efficiency in the implementation of the schemes.

student.name: DPIIT Internship Scheme
student.description: DPIIT has introduced an internship scheme for students pursuing Under Graduate/Post Graduate degrees or Research Scholars enrolled in recognized universities/Institutions within India or abroad. The applicants can choose the  period of their choice ranging from one/two/three months.

student.name: Short Term Fellowship under the Human Resource Development Programme for Health Research
student.description: The scheme aims to provide short-term advanced training and financial support to medical and health research personnel to enhance their skills and promote research in priority health areas.

student.name: Private Tuition Assistance to Scheduled Caste Students Studying in Standard 11th & 12th (Science Stream)
student.description: Under the scheme, financial assistance is provided to Scheduled Caste (SC) students who are studying in Standard 11th and 12th (Science Stream) and have obtained 70% or more marks in the 10th Standard for private coaching.

student.name: Financial Assistance to SC Students Studying in Medical, Engineering and Diploma Course for Purchasing Educational Instrument
student.description: Under this scheme ₹10,000/- for Medical students, ₹8,000/- for Engineering students and ₹3,000/- Diploma students granted for purchase of study instrument to the Scheduled Caste students studying in Medical, Engineering, Diploma courses.

student.name: Financial Assistance for Education of Children of Registered Construction Worker
student.description: The "Financial Assistance for Education of Children of Registered Construction Worker" scheme for education benefit is to give financial assistance for the education of children of registered construction workers having continuous registration for a period of six months under TBOCWWB.

student.name: Promotion of Science Education (POSE) Scholarship Scheme
student.description: The scheme aims to encourage meritorious students to pursue higher studies in Basic and Natural Science subjects, such as B.Sc., B.S., B.Stat., B.Math., Integrated M.Sc., or M.S., in India. The scheme is implemented by the Haryana State Council for Science, Innovation & Technology.

student.name: Operation of Ashram Hostels
student.description: Under this scheme, free accommodation, nutritious meals, educational materials, uniforms, personal hygiene items, and other facilities are provided to Scheduled Tribe (ST) students residing in ashram hostels operated by the Tribal Area Development Department.

student.name: Mukhyamantri Uchcha Shiksha Shodh Protsahan Yojana
student.description: The scheme aims to promote high-quality, interdisciplinary research in higher education institutions across state. The broad subject areas for research include Science, Arts & Humanities, Home Science, & Commerce & Management, while proposals in interdisciplinary subject areas are also encouraged.

student.name: Monsanto Scholarship (M.Sc. Agriculture Science and Agriculture Biotechnology)
student.description: he scheme aims to provide financial assistance to meritorious students pursuing an M.Sc. in Agricultural Science & Agricultural Biotechnology by awarding them scholarships for three semesters.

student.name: Coaching Sahay for the Preparation of NEET, JEE, GUJCET Examination to both Socially and Educationally Backward Class Trainees/Students and Female Students and Economically Backward Class Students
student.description: The scheme aims to support Socially and Educationally Backward Class (SEBC) and Economically Backward Class (EBC) students with coaching fee assistance. Up to ₹20,000 or actual fee is provided for one exam preparation.

student.name: Post Doctoral Fellowship to Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe Candidates
student.description: This scheme has been initiated keeping in view social background of candidates from the deprived section of society and to provide them an opportunity to undertake advanced studies and research in Science, Engineering & Technology, Humanities and Social Sciences in Indian Universities/Institutions.

student.name: PM-YASASVI: Pre-Matric Scholarship for OBC, EBC and DNT Students
student.description: The objective of the Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Pre-Matric Scholarship for OBC, EBC, and DNT students (who are not covered under SC, ST, or OBC) is welfare and motivation to the children of these categories by providing financial assistance at pre-matriculation or secondary stage.

student.name: Private Tuition Assistance to Scheduled Caste Students Studying in Standard 11th & 12th (General Stream)
student.description: Under the scheme, financial assistance is provided to Scheduled Caste (SC) students who are studying in Standard 11th and 12th (General Stream) and have obtained 75% or more marks in the 10th Standard for private coaching fees, as per merit prepared at state level up to 100 students.

student.name: Retrofitted Petrol Scooters - Tamil Nadu
student.description: The 'Retrofitted Petrol Scooters by Welfare of Differently Abled Persons Department' scheme was launched by the Department for the Welfare of Differently Abled Persons, Govt. of Tamil Nadu. The scheme aims to provide a retrofitted petrol scooter to orthopedically differently-abled persons.

student.name: Rajiv Gandhi Scholarship Scheme
student.description: The scheme aims to encourage brilliant students and inculcate a spirit of competition among them. Under this scheme, scholarships/incentives are provided to meritorious students studying in Class VI to Class XII in government schools.

student.name: Hostel Facility for Students Availing Education in Delhi
student.description: The objective of the scheme is to provide facilities for residential and other facilities such as water, reimbursement of electricity expenditure, scholarship etc. for scheduled caste students of Madhya Pradesh studying in higher institutions located in Delhi.

student.name: Kanya Dhan Scheme for SC Students
student.description: The scheme "Kanya Dhan Scheme for SC Students" by the Directorate of Higher Education, Government of Goa. Through this scheme, an amount of ₹ 25,000/- is provided to the girl students of the Scheduled Caste Communities once they pass the 12th standard board examination (or equivalent).

student.name: Aapki Beti Yojana
student.description: Under the scheme, financial assistance of ₹2,100/- per year is provided to girls studying in Class 1 to 8 in government schools, belonging to families living below the poverty line (BPL) and whose both or one parent has passed away. Girls studying in Class 9 to 12 receive ₹2,500/- per year.

student.name: Empowering Schedule Tribe/Schedule Caste Communities Using Information And Communication Technology Tools
student.description: The scheme aims to provide Information Technology related trainings free of cost to the Schedule Tribe (ST) and Schedule Caste (SC) communities. Benefits include basic computer skills and job-oriented Information Technology training courses, intended for citizens, students, local youth, etc.

student.name: Grants To Patients Suffering From Tuberculosis/Cancer And Other Major Diseases
student.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance for medical treatment to patients suffering from Tuberculosis, Cancer, and other major diseases. It provides a one-time financial grant to patients of the Tea Tribes and Adivasi community.

student.name: Admission Process in All Government and Non-Government Schools of the State
student.description: The scheme aims to provide free and accessible education for children from Class 1 to 8 by offering free education and essential learning materials, including books, shoe-bags, and meals, across all government and non-government schools in the state.

student.name: Rehabilitation of Cured Leprosy Persons: Pre & Post-Matric Scholarship
student.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to the parents of Cured Leprosy Persons in order to support such parents in successfully bringing up their children against societal intolerance, stigma, discrimination and violence.

student.name: Vidyajyothi Scheme- Financial Aid For Uniforms And Study Materials To PH Students
student.description: The scheme “Vidyajyothi Scheme- Financial Aid for Uniforms and Study Materials to PH Students” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala.

student.name: The Meghalaya Tribal Students’ Book Grant (Post-Matric Education)
student.description: "The Meghalaya Tribal Students’ Book Grant (Post-Matric Education)" scheme was launched by the Directorate of Higher and Technical Education, Meghalaya. Through this scheme, financial assistance is provided to the meritorious tribal students for the purchase of books for their studies.

student.name: Post-Graduate Scholarships for Professional Courses for SC/ST Candidates
student.description: The scheme has been initiated to provide opportunity to deprived sections of the society, viz.
