farmer.description: Under the Khet Talai scheme, the Rajasthan Government gives subsidies to the farmers on the construction of farm ponds so that the farmers can collect rainwater and irrigate their fields by making farm ponds in their fields.

farmer.name: Pashu Bima Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme 'Pashu Bima Yojna' was launched by the Directorate of Dairy Development, Government of Bihar. The scheme aims to provide insurance security to farmers against the financial loss caused by cattle death and to improve the rural economy.

farmer.name: Matsya Pokhari Yojana
farmer.description: The "Machha Chasa Pain Nua Pokhari Khola Yojana (Matsya Pokhari Yojana)" aims to boost fish production in Odisha by creating new water bodies through pond excavation. It targets income generation & employment via freshwater aquaculture. Subsidies are available for pond areas between 0.2 to 2 hectare

farmer.name: Organizing Block Demonstration
farmer.description: The “Organizing Block Demonstration” scheme provides a 50% subsidy or ₹2,000/- (whichever is less) on input costs for demonstrating the cultivation of Pulses crops over 5 hectares.

farmer.name: Organizing Block Demonstration Through Department And Also Through TNAU
farmer.description: The scheme "Organizing Block Demonstration Through Department And Also Through TNAU" offers grants to farmers for maize cultivation. Farmers can avail a subsidy for demonstrating cultivation practices on a 1-hectare plot.

farmer.name: Quality Paddy Seed Distribution
farmer.description: The “Quality Paddy Seed Distribution” Scheme offers a subsidy of ₹5/- per kg on certified paddy seeds.

farmer.name: Primary Cooperative Agriculture and Rural Development Bank: For Horticulture
farmer.description: The scheme offers a horticulture loan to support farmers with funds ranging from ₹25,000/- to ₹2,00,000/- per 0.4 hectare at an interest rate of 11%-12.75%.

farmer.name: Development of Manures & Fertilizers: Assistance for use of Soil Conditioner
farmer.description: The scheme aims to amend the soil based on soil test reports, address pH and nutrient deficiencies in fertilization practices, and address important soil deficiencies like micronutrients that affect crop productivity. The payment of the sanctioned subsidy is through the Electronic Clearance System.

farmer.name: Namami Gange Yojana- Uttarakhand
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote sustainable and organic farming practices among farmers residing in villages along the banks of the Ganga and its tributaries.

farmer.name: Development of Manures & Fertilizers:  Assistance for construction/Installation of Organic Manure Units
farmer.description: The scheme aims to aid in the conversion of available on-farm organic waste into chemical-free organic manure, providing quality organic farming inputs for sustainable agriculture. The applications for this scheme are accepted offline via the Zonal Agriculture Officer.

farmer.name: Mukhya Mantri Krishak Samriddhi Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to incentivize farmers for improving production and quality of specific Rabi and Kharif crops by providing an incentive payment over and above the Minimum Support Price received. It provides varying financial incentives per quintal to registered farmers selling wheat, paddy, etc.

farmer.name: Mushroom Production and Marketing Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote mushroom cultivation among farmers by providing subsidized inputs, training, and support for marketing, thereby increasing their income and encouraging entrepreneurship in the sector.

farmer.name: Akhil Bharatiya Samanvit Anusandhan Pariyojana - Veer Chandra Singh Garhwali Uttarakhand University of Horticulture and Forestry
farmer.description: The scheme aims to conduct research and improve important horticultural and forestry species, conserve their germplasm, provide improved seeds and saplings, and train farmers in using better techniques to enhance productivity.

farmer.name: Spice Area Expansion
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote and expand the cultivation of high-value spices such as ginger, chili, turmeric, and garlic among farmers by providing quality seeds and tubers at a subsidized rate, thereby increasing spice production and enhancing farmers’ income in Uttarakhand.

farmer.name: Vegetable Area Expansion
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote and expand vegetable cultivation across the state by providing seasonal vegetable seeds to farmers at a 50% subsidy.

farmer.name: Seed Testing Laboratory Scheme
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to assist seed testing services to farmers to ensure the viability and purity of seed to be sown.

farmer.name: Maize Development through Cluster Approach Scheme
farmer.description: The objectives of the scheme is to increase food grain production in order to attain self-sufficiency by growing high yielding varieties of maize and also by adopting the crop and location specific production technology.

farmer.name: Shednet House
farmer.description: The Shednet House provided subsidies to farmers so they could earn more income by cultivating horticultural crops like vegetables, flowers, fruits, etc. by controlling the agro-climatic factors, temperature, humidity, and sunlight.

farmer.name: Primary Cooperative Agriculture and Rural Development Bank: For Animal Husbandry
farmer.description: The scheme provides loans to farmers for the purpose of animal husbandry, allowing them to improve their income through livestock.

farmer.name: Scheme to Provide Financial Assistance to Purchase Solar Power Unit/Kit for Solar Fencing around the Farm
farmer.description: This scheme has been introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat. It aims to provide financial assistance to farmers for purchasing solar power units/kits for solar fencing around their farms to protect crops from damage caused by wild and stray animals.

farmer.name: Annapurna Scheme: Seed Exchange Programme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to increase agricultural productivity and production for SC/ST farmers who cannot afford improved seeds. It provides high-yielding improved seed varieties, up to one hectare, in exchange for existing crop or seed, backed by a 75% subsidy with a maximum limit of ₹1,500/-.

farmer.name: Registration of Aromatic Plant Farmers - Centre for Aromatic Plants
farmer.description: The registration ensures that farmers are eligible to access the benefits of all schemes offered by CAP, including state and central government subsidies, technical guidance, training programs, and product clearance support.

farmer.name: Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana - Uttarakhand
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote & support organic farming among farmers in Uttarakhand. The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹9,000/- per hectare to farmers for the purchase of organic inputs, including seeds, compost, green manure, biofertilizers, biopesticides, neem oil, etc.

farmer.name: Fruit Area Expansion
farmer.description: The scheme aims to increase fruit production by encouraging farmers to establish new orchards, providing fruit plants at a 50% subsidy to support the expansion of fruit cultivation across Uttarakhand.

farmer.name: Agricultural Extension
farmer.description: Extension Division endeavours towards successful implementation of Agricultural Extension activities. Through its various programmes, schemes and activities, Extension Division helps farmers to get information regarding scientific research and new knowledge in agricultural practices.

farmer.name: Development Support for Coffee in Traditional Areas: Water Augmentation
farmer.description: The scheme "Development Support for Coffee in Traditional Areas: Water Augmentation" by the Coffee Board, Department of Commerce, aims to improve the production and productivity of coffee plantations by encouraging water augmentation in traditional areas.

farmer.name: Masala Shetra Vistar Yojana
farmer.description: Masala Shetra Vistar Yojana was launched by the Government of Madhya Pradesh the whose objective is to expand the area and increase the production of selected spice crops through quality high yielding varieties.

farmer.name: "Integrated Farming for Productivity Improvement Scheme" by the Coconut Development Board
farmer.description: Through the “Integrated Farming for Productivity Improvement Scheme" by the Coconut Development Board, the coconut farmers are provided financial assistance through DBT (Direct Benefit Transfer) mode for the Laying of the Demonstration Plot, and for the Construction of the Organic Manure Unit.

farmer.name: Distribution of Rhizobium Packets-Oilseeds
farmer.description: The “Distribution of Rhizobium Packets-Oilseeds” scheme provides a subsidy on the cost of Rhizobium, to eligible farmers who produce and supply Foundation and Certified Class seeds.

farmer.name: Training of Farmers on Pulses Production Technologies
farmer.description: The “Training of Farmers on Pulses Production Technologies” scheme aims to educate farmers on advanced pulses production technologies to enhance crop yield.

farmer.name: Coconut Development Scheme
farmer.description: The "Coconut Development Scheme" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat, aims to support farmers by providing quality coconut saplings and financial assistance for coconut cultivation.

farmer.name: Flower Development (Demonstration)
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote flower cultivation through demonstrations of major flowers like rose, tuberose, and marigold by providing financial assistance to farmers.

farmer.name: Weather Based Crop Insurance Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide crop insurance protection to farmers against weather-related risks under the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana. It ensures financial support to farmers affected by adverse weather conditions.

farmer.name: Annapurna Scheme: Seed Multiplication Programme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to enhance the economic status and productivity of SC/ST farmers by providing high-quality Breeder or Certified I grade seeds for seed production. Beneficiaries receive a 75% subsidy on the cost of the seed for up to 1 hectare of land.

farmer.name: Gomantak Govsamvardhan Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to support the conservation and rearing of Local Breeds of Cattle in the State of Goa. It provides financial assistance of ₹80/- per animal per day to Dairy Farmers or Individuals who are bonafide residents of Goa to encourage responsible stall feeding and conservation practices.

farmer.name: Promotion of Exportable surplus of identified spices in NE: Cultivation of Lakadong turmeric (Ha)
farmer.description: To promote the cultivation of Lakadong turmeric for exports by providing 50% of the cost of planting material, subject to a maximum of ₹ 30,000 per Ha, to turmeric growers of the North East having land holding size of 0.10 Ha to 8 Ha.

farmer.name: Scheme For Subsidy On Interest For Establishment Of 1 To 20 Milch Animal Farm
farmer.description: The scheme “Scheme for Subsidy on Interest for Establishment of 1 to 20 Milch Animal Farm for the State Cattle Breeders” was started by the Dept. of Agriculture, Farmers Welfare & Co-operation, Government of Gujarat.

farmer.name: Assistance to Scheduled Caste Farmers for Installation of Submersible / Centrifugal Motor Pumpsets With Accessories
farmer.description: Through this scheme, the farmers from the Scheduled Caste Category are provided with a grant of 90% subsidy towards the installation of suitable Submersible / Centrifugal motor pump sets with accessories, subject to a maximum of ₹60,000/‐ per tubewell per individual.

farmer.name: Distribution of Certified Seeds-Oil Seeds
farmer.description: The "Distribution of Certified Seeds-Oil Seeds" scheme offers subsidies to farmers for purchasing certified oil seeds, helping them improve their crop yield and quality.

farmer.name: Production Of Certified Seeds
farmer.description: The "Production of Certified Seeds" scheme, offers a subsidy to farmers for producing certified seeds of Pulses.

farmer.name: Kisan Parivahan Yojana
farmer.description: This scheme has been introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat. It aims to provide financial assistance to farmers for purchasing medium-sized goods vehicles to facilitate the transportation of agricultural produce.

farmer.name: PMMSY: Live Fish Vending Centres Scheme - Haryana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to support fishers by providing financial assistance for live fish vending centers. A subsidy per unit is provided under PMMSY.

farmer.name: Nishadraj Scholarship Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote higher education among children of fisher families working in federation reservoirs by providing scholarships of up to ₹20,000 for technical courses and up to ₹10,000 for non-technical courses.

farmer.name: State Fisheries Input Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to aims to enhance the productivity and income of fish farmers by providing financial support in the form of subsidies on essential fishery inputs such as hand nets, hapa, nets, mini kits, etc.

farmer.name: Establishment of Vermicompost Units
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote sustainable agriculture and organic farming by providing financial assistance for setting up vermicompost units to farmers and women self-help groups.

farmer.name: Assistance for Planting Vegetable Crops for Raw and Ripe Mandap
farmer.description: The scheme provides financial assistance to farmers in Gujarat for setting up vegetable trellises (Mandap) for raw, semi-ripe, and ripe vegetables, with varying support for General, SC, and ST farmers.

farmer.name: RKVY Soil Health and Fertility - Soil Health Card
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote soil test-based nutrient management by providing Soil Health Cards containing soil analysis results and fertilizer recommendations to farmers across India.

farmer.name: AGR-50 Scheme-Assistance To Purchase Tractors
farmer.description: The scheme “AGR-50 Scheme-Assistance to Purchase Tractors” was started by the Department of Agriculture, Farmers Welfare & Co-operation, Government of Gujarat in the year 2011-12. The scheme was implemented for the farmers to help in buying the tractor in the state.

farmer.name: "Sugarcane Nursery Premium" Sub-Scheme of the "Crop Production Technology" Scheme
farmer.description: The "Sugarcane Nursery Premium" Sub-Scheme of the "Crop Production Technology" Scheme is introduced by the Dept of Agriculture and Farmer's Welfare, UT of Puducherry. Through this scheme, the farmers are provided Nursery Premium for Raising Seed Material to Plant One Hectare of Sugarcane.

farmer.name: Krshak Prashikshan Tatha Bhraman Karyakram
farmer.description: Under the scheme, Horticulture and Food Processing Department provide visit to the different location to make the farmers aware of the new technologies of agronomy by giving them tours.

farmer.name: Agriculture Information Unit Scheme
farmer.description: The key objective is to disseminate information on Scientific methods of cultivation to extension staff and farming community through media such as pamphlets, leaflets, radio, television etc. and participating/holding awareness programs by means of exhibitions, seminars etc.

farmer.name: Fasal Pradarshan
farmer.description: Under this scheme, the Agriculture Department organizes field and crop demonstrations to showcase advanced crop production methods and the latest technologies to farmers.

farmer.name: Nalkoop Khanan Yojana- Madhya Pradesh
farmer.description: Under this scheme, farmers from SC and ST categories are eligible for a subsidy covering 75% of the unit cost (up to ₹25,000/-) for digging a tube well and 75% of the unit cost (up to ₹15,000/-) for the successful installation of a tube well submersible pump.

farmer.name: Micro Nutrient Spray
farmer.description: The “Micro Nutrient Spray” scheme provides a 50% subsidy or ₹70/- per hectare to eligible farmers, with a focus on ensuring benefits to SC/ST and women farmers.

farmer.name: Primary Cooperative Agriculture and Rural Development Bank: For Fishing Boats (Catamarans)
farmer.description: The scheme provides loans to farmers for purchasing fishing boats (Catamarans) ranging from ₹15,000/- to ₹60,000/- with an applicable interest rate subject to change.

farmer.name: Vats Palan Protsahan Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to improve Indian indigenous high-yielding cattle breeds by providing eligible livestock farmers with financial incentives and monthly support for calf maintenance.

farmer.name: Sheti Samrudhi Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to facilitate community level action for agriculture growth and management of natural resources. It offers financial assistance, wages for personnel, and funding for necessary office equipment for Tribal Farmers Co-operative.

farmer.name: Seed Program
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide free vegetable seeds to small and marginal farmers and agricultural laborers living below the poverty line.

farmer.name: Kisan Credit Card Scheme for Fisheries- Chief Minister Fishermen Prosperity Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide short-term loans for fisheries. It offers zero-percent interest working capital to the fish farmers through cooperative banks.

farmer.name: Training Programme for Sugarcane Farmers
farmer.description: The scheme aims to enhance the knowledge and skills of sugarcane farmers by providing free training and information on advanced and modern sugarcane cultivation techniques.

farmer.name: Meghalaya Goat Farming Scheme
farmer.description: The Meghalaya Goat Farming Development Scheme is a component of the Chief Minister’s CM-ELEVATE Program, launched by the Govt of Meghalaya. It aims to employ, engage, and empower youth and rural communities by promoting goat farming entrepreneurship, providing easy access to credit and subsidies.

farmer.name: Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Consolidation of Coffee
farmer.description: The scheme "Consolidation of Coffee" aims to increase the productivity of the existing coffee holdings by gap filling or rejuvenation, followed by improved cultivation methods such as application of compost, bush management, shade management, pest and disease control, etc.

farmer.name: Price Support Scheme
farmer.description: The Government's price policy for agricultural commodities seeks to ensure remunerative prices to the growers for their produce with a view to encourage higher investment and production and to safeguard the interest of consumers by making available supplies at reasonable prices.

farmer.name: Zero Budget Natural Farming
farmer.description: The Rajasthan State government started a scheme "Zero Budget Natual Farming" in 2020-21 to promote  natural farming.

farmer.name: Assistance to Scheduled Caste Farmers for Sinking Tubewell
farmer.description: “Assistance to Scheduled Caste Farmers for Sinking Tubewell" was launched by Dept of Agriculture and Farmer's Welfare, UT of Puducherry. Through this scheme, the farmers from the SC Category are provided with a grant of 90% subsidy towards the construction of tubewell/filter points per individual.

farmer.name: Farm Storage Structure Scheme
farmer.description: This scheme has been introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Dept., Gujarat. It aims to help farmers to protect their harvested crops from damage due to natural calamities and store them safely for better financial returns by selling at optimal prices.

farmer.name: PMMSY: Large Fish Feed Mill of Production Capacity of 20 Ton /Day Scheme - Haryana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to strengthen aquaculture through fish feed production. It provides a grant per unit to the beneficiaries under PMMSY.

farmer.name: PMMSY: Insulated Vehicles - Haryana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide a subsidy per unit for purchasing insulated vehicles for the beneficiaries to promote cold-chain logistics in fish transportation.

farmer.name: AGR-4 Scheduled Castes Sub-Plan for Distribution of Seeds and Fertilizers Input Kits at Subsidised Rates to Scheduled Caste Cultivators
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide subsidised seeds, fertilizers, and organic inputs to Scheduled Caste farmers to improve agricultural productivity.

farmer.name: Tour Program of Modern Laboratories
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote biotechnology education and research by providing students, researchers, and farmers with exposure to modern laboratories, advanced research activities, and innovative technologies.

farmer.name: Nandi Shala Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to improve local cattle breeds through natural insemination. Under this, indigenous breeding bulls are provided on subsidy to livestock farmers in rural Gram Panchayats.

farmer.name: Madhya Pradesh Fasal Vividhikaran (Crop Diversification) Encouragement Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to encourage crop diversification by providing necessary inputs to farmers who shift from growing wheat and paddy. Benefits are provided primarily to the implementing Company, Firm, or Institution to cover costs related to Human Resources and Marketing, benefiting farmers.

farmer.name: Him Kukkut Palan Yojna
farmer.description: The scheme “Him Kukkut Palan Yojna” was launched by the Department of Animal Husbandry, Government of Himachal Pradesh to provide the subsidy to the entrepreneurs/farmers who are interested and engaged in poultry farming in the state.

farmer.name: AGR 2 (Farm Mechanization) Scheme Of Farmers Other Than SC/ST
farmer.description: The scheme “AGR 2 (Farm Mechanization) Scheme of Farmers Other Than SC/ST” was started by the Department of Agriculture, Farmers Welfare & Co-operation, Government of Gujarat.

farmer.name: "Input Assistance to Farmers for Taking up Fish Farming in Farm Ponds" Component of the "Mukhyamantri Maschyajibi Kalyan Yojana" Scheme
farmer.description: The objective of this scheme is to increase the inland fish production, and to enhance the income of farmers in the State though aquaculture. Input assistance is provided to the farmers who have excavated farm ponds under MGNREGA for facilitating the production of fingerling and fish.

farmer.name: Fodder Minikits under Schedule Castes Sub Plan (SC Category)
farmer.description: The scheme “Fodder Minikits under Schedule Castes Sub Plan” is implemented by the Directorate of Animal Husbandry, Government of Gujarat to provide an improved variety of fodder seeds to Scheduled Castes beneficiaries for widespread publicity, demonstration, and adaptation by farmers.

farmer.name: Seed Multiplication Scheme of Paddy, Millets, Pulses, Oilseeds and Cotton
farmer.description: The “Seed Multiplication Scheme of Paddy, Millets, Pulses, Oilseeds, and Cotton” offers subsidies to farmers to produce seeds under contract with the Department of Agriculture.

farmer.name: Production of Foundation Seeds
farmer.description: The "Production of Foundation Seeds" scheme, provides subsidies to farmers cultivating seed farms for pulses, offering specific benefits to SC/ST farmers and farm women.

farmer.name: Pipes Carrying Water From Source To Field-Pulses ISPOM
farmer.description: The scheme provides subsidies to farmers for installing pipes to carry water from the source to their fields, offering a 50% subsidy or up to ₹15,000/- for pipes (up to 800m), including PVC, HDPE, and others.

farmer.name: Bari (Kitchen Garden) Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote kitchen gardening among poor and small/marginal farmers and agricultural laborers living below the poverty line by providing free vegetable seed packets.

farmer.name: Assistance Towards Sugarcane Supplied By The Sugarcane Growers
farmer.description: The scheme aims to assist Sugarcane Growers to take up sugarcane cultivation in the State of Goa. It provides financial support to farmers cultivating sugarcane, paid based on actual tonnage supplied at the factory weighbridge as per the Fair & Remunerative Price.

farmer.name: Annapurna Scheme: Seed Production Programme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to enhance the production and economic status of Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe small and marginal farmers. It provides Foundation or Certified Grade I seeds for high-yielding food grain crops with a 75% subsidy to registered farmers operating near Government agricultural farms.

farmer.name: Tubewell Installation /Pond Construction
farmer.description: The scheme aims to enhance irrigation facilities for farmers in Uttarakhand by providing financial assistance for the construction of tubewells and ponds, thereby improving water availability, supporting horticultural development, and increasing agricultural productivity.

farmer.name: State Millet Mission
farmer.description: The mission aims to promote millet cultivation, ensure fair prices for farmers, and strengthen food security in the state. It seeks to support farmers, particularly in mountain districts, by procuring millet crops such as Mandua and Jhangora at the minimum support price through cooperative groups.

farmer.name: Allotment of Shops in Kisan Bazaar
farmer.description: The scheme aims to facilitate trade in products other than agricultural produce by providing commercial shops in Kisan Bazaars. The shops are allotted to eligible residents of Uttarakhand through public notices published in newspapers and displayed at public places.

farmer.name: Flower Area Expansion
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote and expand flower cultivation by providing cultivators in Uttarakhand access to quality flower planting material at a subsidized cost, thereby increasing flower production and enhancing farmers’ income.

farmer.name: Integrated Scheme for Export Promotion & Quality Improvement in Spices and Research and Development of Cardamom: Export Oriented Production: Large Cardamom Certified Nursery (Unit)
farmer.description: The scheme "Export Oriented Production: Large Cardamom Certified Nursery (Unit)" is a Sub-Scheme of the Integrated Scheme for Export Promotion & Quality Improvement in Spices and Research and Development of Cardamom by the Spice Board, Department of Commerce and Industry, Ministry of Commerce.

farmer.name: Assistance for Power Driven Chaff Cutter Scheme under Integrated Fodder Development Scheme (General Category)
farmer.description: The scheme provides financial assistance to dairy farmers belonging to General Caste category on purchase of electric powered chaff cutters. By using power driven chaff cutter, dairy farmers can reduce wastage of green & dry fodder which is economically important for their profitable dairy farming.

farmer.name: Organizing Block Demonstration on Polythene Mulch Technology in Groundnut
farmer.description: The scheme provides financial assistance to farmers for adopting polythene mulch technology in groundnut cultivation to improve productivity.

farmer.name: Mahila Kisan Yojana (Maharashtra)
farmer.description: "Mahila Kisan Yojana" is implemented by the Dept. of Social Justice & Special Assistance, Govt. of Maharashtra. The scheme aims to uplift the lifestyle of economically disadvantaged Charmakar communities. it supports the production and sale of various types of footwear and leather articles.

farmer.name: Bhavantar Bharpai Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme is implemented by the Department of Horticulture, Government of Haryana, aims to protect horticultural farmers from market price fluctuations and compensate them if they receive low prices for their produce in the market.

farmer.name: Mukhyamantri Ghasyari Kalyan Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide affordable and nutritious packed silage and total mixed animal feed to livestock farmers, especially women, in the remote hilly areas of Uttarakhand. Under this scheme, silage is made available to livestock farmers at a subsidized rate of ₹2.75 per kg.

farmer.name: District Plan: Inter-Village Road Construction Scheme on Contributory Basis
farmer.description: The scheme aims to facilitate the easy and timely transportation of sugarcane from farms to purchasing centers and sugar mills by improving inter-village road connectivity in sugarcane-growing areas.

farmer.name: Coconut Palm Insurance Scheme
farmer.description: The “Coconut Palm Insurance Scheme (CPIS)” is being implemented by the Coconut Development Board, Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Government of India with the objective of insuring coconut palms against natural calamities, climatic risks, pests, diseases, and other perils.

farmer.name: Floriculture Development Scheme
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to motivate the farmers to take up floriculture (traditional and non-traditional flowers) as a commercial venture through the distribution of planting materials and other inputs.

farmer.name: "Back Ended Investment Subsidy to Paddy Growers" component of the "Crop Production Technology" scheme
farmer.description: "Back Ended Investment Subsidy to Paddy Growers" component of the "Crop Production Technology" scheme was introduced by the Dept of Agriculture and Farmer's Welfare, UT of Puducherry. The paddy farmers residing in the Yanam region are provided a back-ended investment subsidy at ₹4,000/- per hectare.

farmer.name: Farm Pond (Khet Talai) Nirman Karyakram
farmer.description: Under the Farm Pond (Khet Talaee) Nirmaan Kaaryakram scheme provied to help the farmers of Rajasthan to collect rain water and use it for irrigation purposes so that the farmers can get sufficient water for irrigation in their fields.

farmer.name: Mini Sprinkler Sanyantr
farmer.description: The Mini Sprinkler Sanyantr scheme provided a subsidy to the farmers can irrigate more land by getting sprinkler subsidy. The subsidy given by the state government to the farmers will be sent directly to their bank account through DBT.

farmer.name: Jharkhand Rajya Fasal Rahat Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme was launched by the Department of Agriculture, Animal Husbandry & Cooperative, Government of Jharkhand. This scheme is mainly to provide financial assistance to the farmers in the event of crop damage due to any natural calamity and natural accidents.

farmer.name: Construction of Godown for Grain Storage
farmer.description: Launched on 24th December 2013, the scheme "Construction of Warehouse for Grain Storage" by the Panchayat and Rural Development Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh aims to provide modern and scientific food grain storage facilities under Food Security Act 13 to applicants in rural areas.

farmer.name: Assistance to Farmers to Purchase Heavy Farm Equipment (Tractor)
farmer.description: This scheme has been introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat. It aims to provide financial assistance to farmers for purchasing tractors, promoting farm mechanization and increasing farm power availability.

farmer.name: Personal Accident Assistance Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide financial support and compensation to farmers, agricultural laborers, and Mandi laborers in Uttarakhand who suffer injury or death due to accidents while engaged in agricultural work or operating agricultural equipment.

farmer.name: Soil Health Card Scheme - Uttarakhand
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide Soil Health Cards to all farmers in the state, helping them understand the nutrient composition of their soil and the type and quantity of fertilizers suitable for their crops.

farmer.name: Dr. Bhimrao Ambedkar Kamdhenu Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide business opportunities in the dairy sector especially to unemployed youths, increase milk production, strengthen economic condition and farmers will get economic benefits even in adverse circumstances.

farmer.name: Cultivation of Aromatic Plants under MGNREGA - Centre for Aromatic Plants
farmer.description: The scheme aims to encourage farmers in rural areas to grow aromatic plants by providing them free planting material, technical help, and wage support through MGNREGA.

farmer.name: Scheduled Caste Sub Plan (SCSP): Electrification of Wells of Scheduled Caste Farmers
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide agricultural electricity connections on a priority basis to Scheduled Caste farmers. Benefits include electrification of wells and free domestic electricity connections for Scheduled Caste farmers and beneficiaries across Gujarat.

farmer.name: Fodder Minikits under Tribal Area Sub Plan (TASP) Scheme (ST Category)
farmer.description: The scheme “Fodder Minikits under Tribal Area Sub Plan (TASP) Scheme” is implemented by the Directorate of Animal Husbandry, Government of Gujarat to provide an improved variety of fodder seeds to Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries for widespread publicity, demonstration, and adaptation by farmers.

farmer.name: Millet Development Scheme
farmer.description: The "Millet Development Scheme" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Dept., Gujarat, aims to promote millet cultivation in Gujarat by organizing promotional activities, expos, processing units, demonstrations(seed/input kit ), and taluka-level melas.

farmer.name: Mukhyamantri Rajya Krishi Vikas Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to help sugarcane farmers grow better crops, increase production, improve sugar quality, and provide support through high-quality seeds, modern techniques, mechanization, crop protection, and advanced farming practices.

farmer.name: National Mission on Edible Oil- Oil Palm: Drip Irrigation
farmer.description: The scheme component "Drip Irrigation" launched under the "National Mission on Edible Oil–Oil Palm" scheme aims to increase the production of oil palm in Gujarat and reduce imports from abroad by providing drip irrigation facilities to farmers under subsidy norms.

farmer.name: Procurement of Food Grains From Farmers at Support Price
farmer.description: The scheme aims to ensure fair and assured prices for farmers by enabling direct government procurement of wheat, paddy, maize, and jowar from registered farmers at the centrally declared Minimum Support Price (MSP) along with any applicable state bonus during the designated procurement period.

farmer.name: Dairy Equipment Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to help Goan dairy farmers purchase essential equipment by offering a 75% subsidy on approved dairy machinery, promoting efficiency and productivity in dairy farming.

farmer.name: Silk Tree Plantation
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote mulberry cultivation and silkworm rearing among farmers in Uttarakhand by providing financial assistance, technical support, and organized cluster-based sericulture activities to enhance their income and livelihood.

farmer.name: Agricultural Skill Development Training Programme For Women Farmers And Farmers
farmer.description: The scheme “Agricultural Skill Development Training Programme for Women Farmers and Farmers” was started by the Department of Agriculture, Farmers Welfare & Co-operation, Government of Gujarat.

farmer.name: Mission for Integrated Development of Horticulture Sub Scheme Horticulture Mission for North East and Himalayan Region- Meghalaya
farmer.description: The scheme is implemented by the Department of Agriculture & Farmers’ Welfare, Government of Meghalaya to promote holistic growth of Horticulture sector thorough promotion, extension, post-harvest management, processing and marketing.

farmer.name: Regional Centre for Training and Production of Mushroom
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to train farmers in the method of mushroom cultivation and to supply quality spawn and pasteurized compost to farmers.

farmer.name: Agriculture Engineering (Mechanical) Scheme
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to provide agricultural machinery such as power tillers/ bulldozers/ tractors etc. on hiring at subsidised rate.

farmer.name: Integrated Farming in Micro Watershed Scheme
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to promote integrated farming activities for improved livelihood in a complementary manner.

farmer.name: Mukhyamantri Svadeshi Gau Samvardhan Yojana
farmer.description: The "Mukhyamantri Svadeshi Gau Samvardhan Yojana" under the "Nand Baba Milk Mission" aims to enhance milk productivity and per capita milk availability in Uttar Pradesh by encouraging the purchase of indigenous improved breed cows from outside the state and employment opportunities in rural areas.

farmer.name: Intensive Fisheries Development Programme - Subsidy on Insurance Premium for Shrimp Crop Insurance
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote sustainable aquaculture by reducing financial risk of shrimp farmers through subsidized insurance coverage.Under this scheme, a 50% subsidy is provided on insurance premium paid by eligible fish farmers for shrimp crop insurance, helping them mitigate potential crop losses

farmer.name: Pashupalan Dhiran Yojana
farmer.description: "Pashupalan Dhiran Yojana" by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department provides loan assistance for animal husbandry to Rabari and Bharwad castes from the socially and educationally backward classes.

farmer.name: Sabji Shetra Vistar Yojana
farmer.description: Under the Sabji Shetra Vistar yojana, selection of vegetables through quality high yielding varieties to expand the area of crops and increase production.

farmer.name: Seed Farms Scheme
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is the production of high-quality seeds of cereals, pulses, oilseeds, etc. and for raising Horticultural seedlings such as orange, coconut, arecanut etc. for distribution to cultivators.

farmer.name: Farmers Training Institute Scheme
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to provide training to farmers at the village level on updated and innovative agriculture technology. The Training Institute facilitates exposure visits for farmers to agriculturally advanced states.

farmer.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana: Accident Insurance Scheme - Uttarakhand
farmer.description: Under this scheme, fish farmers are provided insurance coverage of ₹5 lakh in case of accidental death/total permanent disability, ₹2.5 lakh for partial disability, & ₹50,000 for hospitalization. No premium is required to be paid by fish farmers, entire cost of the insurance is fully borne by Dept.

farmer.name: Special Scheme for Increasing the Storage Capacity of Perishable Products
farmer.description: The scheme aims to increase the storage capacity of perishable products by providing grant assistance for cold storage facilities.

farmer.name: District Plan - Sugarcane Development Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to enhance sugarcane productivity & quality in Uttarakhand by providing financial & technical support to farmers for improved seed production, seed & soil treatment, pest & disease management, thereby promoting sustainable farming & strengthening the livelihoods of sugarcane growers.

farmer.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana - Uttarakhand
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote growth of fisheries sector by providing financial assistance & technical support for modern fisheries activities, thereby increasing fish production,improving livelihood opportunities,& encouraging adoption of advanced fisheries infrastructure among eligible beneficiaries.

farmer.name: Rotavator/Thresher Machine Equipment Assistance Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to support BPL Scheduled Tribe farmers in tribal areas by providing financial assistance to purchase rotavators and thresher machines.

farmer.name: Large cardamom Irrigation Land Development : Irrigation equipment (nos)
farmer.description: The scheme aims to motivate the growers to bring more area under irrigation by constructing water storage structures, rain water harvesting structures and installation of irrigation equipments for increasing the productivity and improving quality of large cardamom.

farmer.name: Improved Cultivation and Advanced Retting Exercise (I-CARE)
farmer.description: The scheme aims to support small and marginal jute growers with adequate pre and post harvesting operations to improve fiber quality and receive higher prices. The scheme provides 100% certified seeds at subsidized rates, free mechanical equipment, microbial consortiums, retting ponds.

farmer.name: Mukhya Mantri Krishi Sa Sajuli Yozana - Assam
farmer.description: The scheme “Mukhya Mantri Krishi Sa Sajuli Yojana” is a State Owned Priority Development (SOPD) scheme for farmers launched by the Assam government in FY 2018-19.  The main purpose of launching this scheme for farmers is their holistic development by implementing several farm mechanization schemes.

farmer.name: Agriculture Research Stations and Laboratories Scheme
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to conduct adaptive research trials on different crops in the Research Farms and in Farmers' Field for adaptability and use of bio pesticides/bio agents.

farmer.name: Financial Assistance Scheme for Purchase of Agricultural Land to Scheduled Caste Farmers
farmer.description: The scheme was launched on in the year 1986. Under the scheme, financial assistance is provided to farmers belonging to the scheduled castes to purchase agricultural land, with a maximum limit of 2 acres, for self-cultivation.

farmer.name: Replanting & Rejuvenation of Coconut Garden" by the Coconut Development Board
farmer.description: Through the scheme “Replanting & Rejuvenation of Coconut Garden (R&R)" by the Coconut Development Board, the coconut farmers are provided financial assistance through DBT (Direct Benefit Transfer) mode for the cutting & removal, replanting, and for integrated management practices.

farmer.name: Integrated Agriculture Training Centre Scheme
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to provide training for enhancing the skills of farmers besides imparting updated agricultural technologies to departmental functionaries.

farmer.name: Visit of Contract Farming Farmers to the Research Stations in Karnataka
farmer.description: The scheme “Visit of Contract Farming Farmers to the Research Stations in Karnataka” facilitates visits for contract farming farmers to research stations across Karnataka to gain agricultural knowledge. Financial support is provided for a 3-day visit.

farmer.name: Kisan Suryoday Yojana (KSY)
farmer.description: "Kisan Suryoday Yojana (KSY)" is a Gujarat government initiative launched in 2020-2021 to provide daytime electricity for agricultural activities, utilizing solar and non-conventional energy.

farmer.name: Solar Based Hydroponic System
farmer.description: The system aims to develop and promote an innovative solar-based hydroponic system that integrates solar energy with modern greenhouse technology and mobile application support.

farmer.name: Integrated Scheme for Export Promotion & Quality Improvement in Spices and Research and Development of Cardamom:Large cardamom Irrigation Land Development : Irrigation / water storage structures(nos)
farmer.description: The scheme aims to motivate the growers to bring more area under irrigation by constructing water storage structures, rain water harvesting structures and installation of irrigation equipments for increasing the productivity and improving quality of large cardamom.

farmer.name: AGR 4 (Farm Mechanization) Scheme For SC Farmers
farmer.description: The scheme “AGR 4 (Farm Mechanization) Scheme for SC Farmers” was started by the Department of Agriculture, Farmers Welfare & Co-operation, Government of Gujarat.

farmer.name: Ramie Crop Scheme
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to encourage cultivation of perennial and remunerative textile crop such as Ramie in suitable areas by providing necessary inputs and training.

farmer.name: Destitute Agricultural Labourers Pension Scheme
farmer.description: The "Destitute Agricultural Labourers Pension Scheme" was launched by the Social Welfare and Nutritious Meal Programme Department, Government of Tamil Nadu. The scheme aims to give pensions of ₹1,000/- to destitute agricultural labourers of 60 years and above and dhoti, saree supplied on festivals.

farmer.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana: Establishment of Backyard mini RAS Units - Haryana
farmer.description: Under this scheme, financial assistance in the form of subsidies is provided to eligible beneficiaries for establishing these units. Various surface and groundwater sources such as canals, rivers, springs, open wells, and tube wells can be utilized for operating RAS units.

farmer.name: Intensive Fisheries Development Programme - Subsidy on Solar System for fish/shrimp culture
farmer.description: Under this scheme, subsidies on solar systems for fish/shrimp culture are provided to eligible fish and shrimp farmers. The subsidy is given at 60% of the total project cost, or ₹30,000 per kW, whichever is lower. The maximum subsidy is limited to 30 kW for shrimp farmers and 10 kW for fish farmers.

farmer.name: Horticulture Mission for North Eastern Hill Scheme- Meghalaya
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to improve productivity, quality planting material, canopy management of fruit trees, support marginal and small farmers, upscaling protected cultivation, and drip irrigation.

farmer.name: Distribution of Gypsum-Oil Seeds
farmer.description: The "Distribution of Gypsum-Oil Seeds" scheme provides a subsidy on the cost of gypsum material and transportation cost, to farmers who produce and supply Foundation and Certified Class seeds.

farmer.name: Distribution of Nuclear Poly Hedrosis Virus
farmer.description: The "Distribution of Nuclear Poly Hedrosis Virus" scheme provides subsidies for the distribution of Nuclear Poly Hedrosis Virus (NPV) to farmers raising seed farms with Pulses crops.

farmer.name: Production of Certified Seeds of Maize
farmer.description: The "Production of Certified Seeds of Maize" scheme, aims to promote the production of certified maize seeds by providing subsidy to farmers in select districts of Tamil Nadu.

farmer.name: Fal Podharopan Yojana
farmer.description: The objective of this scheme is to expand the area under fruit plantation in the district and increase the fruit production. This scheme is implemented in all the 52 districts of the state.

farmer.name: To Assist the Farmers for Construction of Community Base Water Tank/Storage Structure for Efficient Use of Water Through Drip Irrigation
farmer.description: This scheme has been introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat. It aims to provide financial assistance to farmers for constructing community-based water tanks/storage structures to ensure efficient water use through drip irrigation.

farmer.name: Scheme to Encourage Farmers to Add Value to Crops
farmer.description: The scheme provides financial assistance to farmers across Gujarat in adding value to their crops by providing financial assistance for purchasing machinery.

farmer.name: Mukhyamantri Dairy Animal Distribution Programme (Special Backward Tribes Baiga, Bharia & Sahariya)
farmer.description: The scheme aims to improve the livelihood and nutritional security of tribal communities by providing two dairy animals at a 90% subsidy to eligible tribal livestock farmers.

farmer.name: Financial Assistance To Dairy Farmers Belonging To Scheduled Tribes And Scheduled Caste For Purchase Of Basic Dairy Equipments
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide assistance to the Scheduled Tribes and Scheduled Caste communities for the purchase of basic dairy equipments needed to run a small scale dairy farming, and to encourage the tribal population to adopt Animal Husbandry activities, especially Dairy Development Schemes.

farmer.name: Quality Test Report - Centre for Aromatic Plants
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide quality testing facilities at a subsidized rate to registered aromatic farmers, enabling them to obtain authentic quality reports for their produce or essential oils.

farmer.name: Revised Scheme For Incentives To Milk Producers
farmer.description: BRIEF DESCRIPTION: The scheme aims to encourage farmers and youth in Goa to take up Dairy business to increase local milk production and achieve state self-sufficiency. It offers a 40% composite subsidy on milk proceeds (incentive and cattle feed subsidy) to eligible resident farmers.

farmer.name: Agricultural Investment Grant (in Case of Crop Damage of 33% or More)
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to farmers who suffer crop losses of 33% or more due to natural calamities such as floods, heavy rainfall, drought, hailstorms, or other notified disasters.

farmer.name: Cattle Shed Subsidy Scheme under Scheduled Castes Sub Plan (SC Category)
farmer.description: The scheme “Cattle Shed Subsidy Scheme under Scheduled Castes Sub Plan (SCSP)” is implemented by the Directorate of Animal Husbandry, Government of Gujarat. Under this scheme, the financial assistance is given to Scheduled Caste beneficiaries for construction of Cattle Shed for 2 animals.

farmer.name: Various Training and Support Schemes of Kisan Vigyan Kendra - Veer Chandra Singh Garhwali Uttarakhand University of Horticulture and Forestry
farmer.description: The scheme aims to enhance the knowledge, skills, and productivity of farmers in Uttarakhand by providing them with the latest technical training in agriculture, horticulture, and forestry.

farmer.name: Chief Minister’s Shasakt Kisan Yojana
farmer.description: The Govt. of Arunachal Pradesh started the scheme “Chief Minister’s Shasakt Kisan Yojana (CM-SKY)” for uplifting the status of women, marginal and small farmers of the State for self-employment generation & commercialization of Horticulture activities to double their income.

farmer.name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana: Per Drop More Crop
farmer.description: The scheme “Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana: Per Drop More Crop” was launched by the Dept. of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare, Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare, Govt. of India on 1st July 2015. The scheme mainly focuses on water use efficiency at farm level through Micro Irrigation.

farmer.name: Use of Advanced Drone Technology (Agriculture Aircraft) in Agriculture Sector
farmer.description: The scheme "Use of Advanced Drone Technology (Agriculture Aircraft) in Agriculture Sector" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Dept., Gujarat, aims to promote the use of advanced drone technology for spraying crop protection chemicals and bio-fertilizers, etc.

farmer.name: Kisan Credit Card (Pashupalan)
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide short-term working capital loans for livestock activities by offering eligible livestock farmers interest-subsidized loans for operational purposes through banks.

farmer.name: Drip Irrigation Sprinkler
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote efficient and sustainable irrigation for horticultural crops by providing farmers with modern irrigation systems and financial subsidies to enhance productivity and water conservation.

farmer.name: Assistance in Packing Materials under Post Harvest Management of Horticultural Produce
farmer.description: The scheme provides financial assistance to farmers to procure packing materials like corrugated and wooden boxes for post-harvest management of horticultural produce.

farmer.name: Bihar Rajya Fasal Sahayata Yojna
farmer.description: The scheme strives to back the farmers with financial incentives in the event of a crop loss due to natural calamities.

farmer.name: Fruit Development Scheme
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to make available to the farmers good quality planting materials in convergence mode and sale at subsidized rates.

farmer.name: Assistance for Power Driven Chaff Cutter Scheme under Scheduled Castes Sub Plan (SC Category)
farmer.description: The scheme provides financial assistance to dairy farmers belonging to Scheduled Caste category on purchase of electric powered chaff cutters. By using power driven chaff cutter, dairy farmers can reduce wastage of green &dry fodder which is economically important for their profitable dairy farming.

farmer.name: Production of Foundation and Certified Seeds
farmer.description: The "Production of Foundation and Certified Seeds" scheme, provides subsidy to farmers who produce and supply Foundation and Certified seeds to the Department of Agriculture.

farmer.name: Wire Fencing Around the Farm for Crop Protection
farmer.description: This scheme has been introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Dept., Gujarat. It aims to protect farmers' crops from damage caused by wild and stray animals by providing financial assistance for wire fencing around farms.

farmer.name: Promotion of Organic Farming under State Organic Policy
farmer.description: The scheme "Promotion of Organic Farming under State Organic Policy" by the Agriculture and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to promote organic agriculture by providing financial assistance for certification, transportation, and organic inputs to farmers across the state.

farmer.name: Skill Development Programme- Uttarakhand
farmer.description: The scheme aims to enhance the employability and entrepreneurial skills of students, researchers, and farmers in the field of biotechnology, thereby promoting self-employment and entrepreneurship in emerging areas of the biotechnology sector.

farmer.name: Sugarcane Seed Replacement Programme / Production Enhancement Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to increase sugarcane productivity & improve sugar recovery by promoting cultivation of high-quality, high-yielding, & newly developed sugarcane varieties, while providing farmers with financial support, modern cultivation techniques, & access to advanced agricultural technology.

farmer.name: AGR 3 (Farm Mechanization) Scheme For ST Farmers
farmer.description: The scheme “AGR 3 (Farm Mechanization) Scheme for ST Farmers” was started by the Department of Agriculture, Farmers Welfare & Co-operation, Government of Gujarat.

farmer.name: Tuber Crops Potato, Tapioca, Colocasia
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to boost up production and productivity of potato per unit area by providing quality HYV seeds, and equipment at a 50% subsidy.

farmer.name: Rajya Samekit Sahkari Vikas Yojana - Ganga Gaay Mahila Dairy Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote dairy entrepreneurship among women, Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and general category members of cooperative societies by providing financial assistance through subsidies and loans for the purchase of milch cows and buffaloes.

farmer.name: Top Working Program
farmer.description: The scheme aims to transform local varieties of Mango, Ber, Amla, and other indigenous plants into improved varieties. It also provides 20 days of Top Working training to unemployed rural youth.

farmer.name: Primary Cooperative Agriculture and Rural Development Bank: For Minor Irrigation
farmer.description: The scheme offers loans to farmers for minor irrigation projects, aiming to enhance water access for agriculture. Loan amounts range from ₹65,000/- to ₹1,20,000/- at an interest rate of 11% to 11.75%.

farmer.name: Primary Cooperative Agriculture and Rural Development Bank: For Power Tiller
farmer.description: The scheme offers loans to farmers covering up to 90% of the cost of a power tiller, with an interest rate between 11% and 12.25%.

farmer.name: Vegetable Development Scheme
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to promote vegetable production through HYV Seeds/ Hybrids/ Improved/ Open Pollinated seedlings, including assistance to farmers.

farmer.name: Sub mission on Seeds and Planting Material
farmer.description: The scheme aims to increase the production of certified/quality seed; strengthen Seed Testing Laboratories; assist Seed Producing Institutes for the adoption of new technologies, ensuring farmers get quality seeds at the right time and reasonable prices.

farmer.name: Promotion of Post-Harvest Management, Processing, and Value Addition of Aromatic Plant Produce to Farmers/Institutions/Groups in the State - Centre for Aromatic Plants
farmer.description: The scheme aims to encourage and support farmers, institutions, and groups engaged in aromatic plant cultivation to establish post-harvest management and processing facilities.

farmer.name: Primary Cooperative Agriculture and Rural Development Bank: For Handloom and Other Non Farm Sector Activities
farmer.description: The scheme offers loans for handloom and other non-farm sector activities, with a maximum loan amount of up to ₹2,00,000/- at an interest rate of 11% to 12.5%. The scheme is designed to support farmers engaged in non-farm activities.

farmer.name: Primary Cooperative Agriculture and Rural Development Bank: For Inland Fishing
farmer.description: The scheme offers loans to farmers for inland fishing projects, with a loan amount of up to ₹3,30,000/- per hectare at an interest rate between 11% and 12%.

farmer.name: Krishak Utpadak Kshati Sahayata Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance and compensation to farmers in the event of loss or damage to crops, livestock, or agricultural land caused by fire, flood, or heavy rains in the notified areas of the state.

farmer.name: Subsidy on Rehri alongwith (Stove, Gas Chulha, Utensil etc.) for the Fish/Fish Product Sale
farmer.description: Under this scheme, financial assistance will be provided for purchase of a handcart along with essential items such as stove, gas chulha, utensils, etc., for fish and fish product sales. A subsidy of 60% on the total project cost of ₹60,000/- will be granted, amounting to ₹36,000/- per beneficiary.

farmer.name: Sub-Mission on Agricultural Extension (SMAE)
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote the adoption of modern and scientific farming practices among farmers across the state through continuous training, practical demonstrations, and exposure visits.

farmer.name: Atal Bhujal Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to improve the management of groundwater resources in water-stressed areas. It provides institutional strengthening and financial incentives to Gram Panchayats and local communities for sustainable groundwater management and water-efficient practices.

farmer.name: Arun Pig Development Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme “Arun Pig Development Scheme” was launched by the Department of Animal Husbandry, Veterinary & Dairy Development, Government of Arunachal Pradesh in the financial year 2019-20 with a focus on pig rearing.

farmer.name: Plantation Crop Development Scheme
farmer.description: The objectives of the scheme are to boost up the area and production of all plantation crops like Arecanut, Cashewnut, Coconut, Pineapple etc., and to provide a 50 % subsidy for planting materials and construction of a soakage tank.

farmer.name: Integrated Technology Enabled Agriculture Management System Or iTEAMS
farmer.description: The scheme was launched by the Department of Agriculture & Farmers’ Welfare, Govt. of Meghalaya to address critical issues of enabling & facilitating access to remunerative & sustainable markets for farmers produce in most transparent & effective manner through facilitation service iTEAM Dial 1917.

farmer.name: Kisan-Vaigyanik Sangoshthi
farmer.description: The scheme aims to guide farmers and youth in biotechnology-based farming and animal husbandry by providing technical training and solutions to enhance self-employment and income generation.

farmer.name: Meghalaya Agriculture Response Vehicle Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme “Meghalaya Agriculture Response Vehicle Scheme” was launched by the Department of Agriculture & Farmers’ Welfare, Government of Meghalaya to provide Small Commercial Vehicles and pickups at each CD Block across Meghalaya with 50% financial aid from the Government of Meghalaya.

farmer.name: Subsidy Scheme On Establishment Of 25 R.I.R. Birds' Unit For Weaker Section
farmer.description: The scheme “Subsidy Scheme on Establishment of 25 R.I.R. Birds' Unit for Weaker Section” was launched by the Department of Agriculture, Farmers Welfare & Co-operation, Government of Gujarat to provide employment, nutritious diet and to raise the income of weaker section people of the society.

farmer.name: National Mission on Edible Oil- Oil Palm: Establishment of Seed Garden
farmer.description: The scheme component "Establishment of Seed Garden" launched under the "National Mission on Edible Oil–Oil Palm" scheme aims to increase oil palm production in Gujarat and reduce imports by establishing seed gardens through infrastructure support to farmers.

farmer.name: Plant Protection Scheme
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to control pests and diseases including weeds and rodents of horticultural crops by the distribution of bio-pesticides/Bio-agents and equipment at 50% subsidy.

farmer.name: Fruit Processing Centre
farmer.description: The objective of scheme is to disseminate technical knowhow to youth entrepreneurs, farmers & housewives through practical demonstration on importance of fruit preservation & to manufacture quality processed fruit products such as Squashes, Jams, Jelly & Juices under trade name ‘Meg Fruits Product'.

farmer.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana: Medium Fish Feed Mill of Production Capacity of 8 Ton/Day
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote fish feed self-sufficiency and affordability among fish farmers. Under this scheme, financial assistance is provided for setting up medium-sized fish feed mills with a production capacity of 8 tons per day, benefiting entrepreneurs and fish farmers.

farmer.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana: Mini Fish Feed Mills of Production Capacity of 2 Ton /Day
farmer.description: Under this scheme, financial assistance, in form of subsidy, is provided to individuals for setting up mini feed mills, ensuring the local availability of quality fish feed at affordable prices for fishers and fish farmers.

farmer.name: Primary Cooperative Agriculture and Rural Development Bank: For Sericulture In Irrigated Area
farmer.description: The scheme provides loans to farmers for sericulture activities in irrigated areas, with a loan amount ranging from ₹49,000/- to ₹79,000/- per acre, and interest rates from 11% to 12.75%.

farmer.name: Spices Development Ginger, Turmeric, Black Pepper, Cardamom, Coriander, Cinnamon and Chillies
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to encourage farmers to produce good quality and diseased-free planting materials and to grow improved varieties of high-yielding spices.

farmer.name: Fish Farming Diversification (Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe) Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote economic empowerment and livelihood diversification among Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe communities through fisheries-based activities.

farmer.name: Investment Grant for Farmers with More than 2 Hectares of Agricultural Land
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to farmers owning more than 2 hectares of agricultural land who suffer crop losses due to natural disasters.

farmer.name: Minimum Support Price for Aromatic Oils/Products - Centre for Aromatic Plants
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide registered farmers engaged in aromatic cultivation with a fair price and a guaranteed market for their produce.

farmer.name: National Bio Energy Programme - Uttarakhand Renewable Energy Development Agency
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote the use of biogas as a clean and renewable energy source, help livestock farmers set up biogas plants, reduce the use of conventional fuels, and support eco-friendly rural development in the state.

farmer.name: National Gopal Ratna Award
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote scientific dairy practices and AI coverage. It provides awards, recognition, and monetary incentives to farmers, dairy organizations, and AI technicians.

farmer.name: Primary Cooperative Agriculture and Rural Development Bank: For Plantation (Tea)
farmer.description: The scheme offers financial assistance of up to ₹33,000/- per acre to farmers for tea plantations, with an interest rate of 11%-12.5%.

farmer.name: Post Harvest Marketing Scheme
farmer.description: The “Post Harvest Marketing Scheme” was launched by the Department of Agriculture & Farmers’ Welfare, Government of Meghalaya for the creation of post harvest marketing infrastructure.

farmer.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana: Medium Scale Ornamental Fish Rearing Unit (Fresh Water) - Haryana
farmer.description: Under this scheme, financial assistance in the form of a subsidy is provided to eligible beneficiaries for setting up infrastructure such as sheds, breeding units, and rearing and culture tanks for ornamental fish.

farmer.name: Tea Development Programme in the Hilly Districts of Uttarakhand - Uttarakhand Tea Development Board
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote tea cultivation in the mountainous regions of Uttarakhand, providing sustainable employment, optimal utilization of barren and unused land, and economic upliftment of local farmers.

farmer.name: Basic Agriculture Training Centre Scheme
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to provide short-term vocational training for rural educated unemployed youths in agriculture and allied sectors to develop entrepreneurship skills and employment generation.

farmer.name: Distribution of Manually Operated Plant Protection Equipment
farmer.description: The "Distribution of Manually Operated Plant Protection Equipment" scheme provides farmers with a subsidy of 50% on the cost of equipment to enhance agricultural practices.

farmer.name: Assistance for Area Expansion in Fruit crops
farmer.description: The scheme "Assistance for Area Expansion in Fruit Crops" aims to promote fruit crop cultivation through financial assistance for high density and ultra-high density plantations, tissue culture bananas, and papayas to farmers across the state.

farmer.name: Compensation for Loss of Life and Property Caused by Wild Animals in and around Forest Areas: Assistance for Crop Losses Due to Wild Animal Attacks
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to farmers for crop losses caused by wild animals in forest areas and their surrounding regions, thereby mitigating economic losses and supporting farmers’ livelihoods.

farmer.name: Integrated Horticulture Development Plan Scheme for Scheduled Caste Families
farmer.description: Under this scheme, Scheduled Caste farmers will be provided with quality planting material and other inputs for mushroom cultivation, bamboo stacking in vegetables, and protected cultivation with a subsidy of up to 90%. Additionally, training will be offered on various aspects of these practices.

farmer.name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Maandhan Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide old-age social security through a minimum assured monthly pension of ₹ 3,000/- to Small and Marginal Farmers after attaining the age of 60 years.

farmer.name: Plant Protection including Integrated Pest Management Scheme
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to mobilize and support farmers to use bio-pesticides and bio- agents for plant health management through distribution of bio-pesticides and other IPM tools and through sale of Sprayers and Plant Protection Equipment to encourage organic crop production.

farmer.name: Primary Cooperative Agriculture and Rural Development Bank: For Poultry Development
farmer.description: The scheme offers loans to farmers for poultry development and cattle development, with funding of up to ₹14,800/- for 200 birds or ₹17,000/- for 2 cows, with an interest rate of 14.5% to 16.5%.

farmer.name: Distribution of Materials to Promote Herbal Cultivation in Uttarakhand - Herbal Research and Development Institute, Uttarakhand
farmer.description: Under this scheme, there is a special provision for the free distribution of seeds or saplings of medicinal plants up to 3 nali and seeds or saplings of aromatic plants up to 5 nali to farmers belonging to border districts, Scheduled Castes/Tribes, and Below Poverty Line (BPL) categories.

farmer.name: Assistance to Scheduled Castes for Fisheries: Training of Scheduled Caste trainees Gujarat
farmer.description: The scheme aims to increase fish production and provide employment opportunities to Scheduled Caste (SC) people engaged in inland fisheries activities.

farmer.name: Sthaniya Fasal Protsahan Karyakram
farmer.description: The program aims to promote the cultivation of local crops, enhance productivity, ensure the use of quality seeds, and provide fair prices to farmers in the mountain districts of Uttarakhand.

farmer.name: Scheme for Loan and Subsidy for Manure and Fertilizer-DNH&DD
farmer.state: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote fertiliser use for higher productivity. Subsidy-cum-loan assistance is provided in kind to small and marginal farmers.

farmer.name: Winter Cropping & Development of Cultivable Land Scheme
farmer.description: The objectives of the scheme are to bring more areas under cultivation during the normally lean winter season under different crops other than paddy and increase production and productivity of various crops like cereals, pulses, oilseeds, and vegetables during the Rabi season.

farmer.name: Farmer Suicide Ex-Gratia
farmer.description: "Farmer Suicide Ex-Gratia" is a Financial Assistance Scheme by the Agriculture & Cooperation Dept. of the Govt. of Andhra Pradesh. The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹ 7,00,000 to the family members of the deceased farmers/tenant farmers, who commit suicide due to agrarian reasons.

farmer.name: Janta Juth Accidental Insurance Scheme
farmer.description: "Janta Juth" is a Farmer's Accidental Insurance Scheme by the Govt. of Gujarat to provide economic assistance of upto ₹ 2,00,000 to Farmers in case of Death or Disability. The objective is to assist the successor of the farmer in case of death or disability due to an accident.

farmer.name: Farmers Accidental Insurance Scheme
farmer.description: This scheme has been introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Dept., Gujarat. It aims to provide financial assistance to the families of registered farmers in the event of accidental death or disability.

farmer.name: Chief Minister Drought Relief Scheme
farmer.description: The Chief Minister Drought Relief Scheme aims to provide relief and financial assistance to families affected by drought in designated areas. The scheme ensures financial security, livelihood support, and basic resources for drought-hit communities.

farmer.name: Fishermen Accident Relief Scheme
farmer.description: The "Fishermen Accident Relief Scheme" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat, aims to support the families of Gujarat's active fishermen by offering monetary compensation in case of accidental fatalities.

farmer.name: Goat Rearing Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to increase the income and raise the living standards of tribal women beneficiaries. Through this scheme, financial assistance is provided for setting up goat units.

farmer.name: Krishak Bandhu Scheme
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide financial security to farmers through direct income support and death/disability benefits. It benefits small and marginal farmers with annual assistance of ₹5,000/- per acre and a one-time payout of ₹2,00,000/- in case of death.

farmer.name: Gopinath Munde Shetkari Apghat Suraksha Sanugrah Audhan Yojana
farmer.short_title: GOPINATH MUNDE SHETKARI APGHAT SURAKSHA SANUGRAH AUDHAN YOJANA
farmer.description: In order to give financial support to the aggrieved family members of the ill-fated farmers the State Govt. has provided accident claim cover through Gopinath Munde Shetkari Apghat Suraksha Sanugrah Audhan Yojana in the year 2023-24.

farmer.name: Tea Development Scheme
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme is to continue the experimental plantation of tea and to find the possibility of growing tea economically as well as on a commercial scale.

farmer.name: Assistance for Damage to Agricultural Land and Other Losses
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide financial relief to farmers whose agricultural land has been damaged due to natural disasters such as floods, landslides, avalanches, or river diversions. Under this scheme, a minimum of ₹5,000/- and a maximum of ₹47,000/- is provided to the beneficiaries.

farmer.name: Scheme to Provide Assistance for Setting Up Farmer Support Centers in State Market Committees and for Computerization of All Market Committees
farmer.description: This scheme has been introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat. It aims to establish farmer support centers at market committee levels and computerize all market committees in Gujarat.

farmer.name: Promoting to Farmer for Post Harvesting & Management (Value Addition)
farmer.description: This scheme has been introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat. It aims to support farmers in setting up crop value addition units using post-harvesting technology and management concepts.

farmer.name: Assistance for Tools, Equipment, Shorting/Grading Equipment, PHM Equipment for General Category (HRT-2)
farmer.description: Under the scheme, financial assistance is provided to farmers belonging to the General Category as well as to farmer groups and cooperative societies for purchasing various tools, equipment, sorting/grading equipment, and Post-Harvest Management (PHM) equipment.

farmer.name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana - Har Khet Ko Pani (Surface Scheme) Uttarakhand
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide every farmer with reliable surface irrigation facilities by developing sustainable irrigation infrastructure, ensuring that every field receives adequate water to improve agricultural productivity.

farmer.name: Integrated Pest Management Demonstration Cum Training
farmer.description: The “Integrated Pest Management Demonstration Cum Training” scheme provides financial assistance for 30 farmers to conduct pest management demonstrations and training using a cluster approach in Farmer Field Schools.

farmer.name: Chief Minister's Micro Finance Initiative
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote access to credit for economic activities undertaken by individual farmers, entrepreneurs, Self-Help Groups (SHGs), and Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs) in the state of Nagaland.

farmer.name: Assistance for Tools, Equipment, Shorting/Grading Equipment, PHM Equipment for Scheduled Castes (HRT-4)
farmer.description: Under the scheme, financial assistance is provided to farmers belonging to the Scheduled Castes category as well as to farmer groups and cooperative societies for purchasing various tools, equipment, sorting/grading equipment, and Post-Harvest Management (PHM) equipment.

farmer.name: Assistance for Tools, Equipment, Shorting/Grading Equipment, PHM Equipment for Scheduled Tribes (HRT-3)
farmer.description: Under the scheme, financial assistance is provided to farmers belonging to the Scheduled Tribes category as well as to farmer groups and cooperative societies for purchasing various tools, equipment, sorting/grading equipment, and Post-Harvest Management (PHM) equipment.

farmer.name: Harvesting Tools Assistance for General Category (HRT-2)
farmer.description: Under the scheme, financial assistance is provided to farmers belonging to the General Category as well as to FPOs (Farmers Producer Organizations), FPCs (Farmers Producer Companies), FIGs (Farmer Interest Groups), SHGs (Self-Help Groups), and Cooperative Societies for purchasing harvesting tools.

farmer.name: Harvesting Tools Assistance for Scheduled Castes (HRT-4)
farmer.description: Under the scheme, financial assistance is provided to farmers belonging to the SC category as well as to FPOs (Farmers Producer Organizations), FPCs (Farmers Producer Companies), FIGs (Farmer Interest Groups), SHGs (Self-Help Groups), and Cooperative Societies for purchasing harvesting tools.

farmer.name: Harvesting Tools Assistance for Scheduled Tribes (HRT-3)
farmer.description: Under the scheme, financial assistance is provided to farmers belonging to the ST category as well as to FPOs (Farmers Producer Organizations), FPCs (Farmers Producer Companies), FIGs (Farmer Interest Groups), SHGs (Self-Help Groups), and Cooperative Societies for purchasing harvesting tools.

farmer.name: Coffee Development Programme in North Eastern Region: Quality Upgradation / Certification: Support for Eco-Certification / Organic Certification
farmer.description: The scheme aims to encourage coffee growers to improve quality for higher returns and improve access to high-value specialty markets through the production of eco-certified coffees. It also encourages small growers to form groups/collectives to take up eco-certification.

farmer.name: Development Support for Coffee in Traditional Areas: Replantation
farmer.description: The objective of the scheme "Development Support for Coffee in Traditional Areas: Replantation" is to improve the production and productivity of coffee plantations by encouraging replantation of old/senile and unproductive plantations with high-yielding, disease-tolerant coffee varieties.

farmer.name: Kisan Vriksha Mitra Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to promote commercial tree plantation on private lands, ensuring buyback of produce, increasing farmers' income, and generating employment. The scheme benefits farmers, private institutions, and other stakeholders by providing financial assistance for plantation.

farmer.name: To Increase Fish Seed Production and Inland Fisheries Equipment in Non-Tribal Areas
farmer.description: This scheme has been introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat. It aims to enhance fish seed production and promote inland fisheries in non-tribal areas.

farmer.name: Chief Minister Floriculture Mission
farmer.description: This is a special scheme initiated during 2014-15 aimed at all-round development of floriculture sector in the State which incorporates cultivation, marketing, training etc. For the year 2015- 16 cultivation of Gerbera under Shade net house with tissue cultured planting materials is proposed.

farmer.name: Hand Pump Yojana
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide reliable and accessible drinking water facilities to farmers and villagers through the installation of hand pumps.

farmer.name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana - Har Khet Ko Pani (Groundwater Irrigation Scheme) Uttarakhand
farmer.description: The scheme aims to provide assured and sustainable irrigation to every farm by developing groundwater-based irrigation facilities for farmers. This scheme is a centrally sponsored scheme with a funding pattern of 90% Central share and 10% State share.
