child.description: The “Maternity Benefit” scheme by the BOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department, Odisha, is a welfare scheme that provides maternity benefits to the female beneficiaries subject to a maximum of two children who  are registered beneficiaries for a period of more than one year under the board.

child.name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme - Sikkim
child.description: The scheme “Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (IGNDPS)” was started by the Department of Women & Child Welfare, Govt. of Sikkim in 2014. Under this scheme, financial assistance is provided to differently-abled persons with severe or multiple disabilities of more than 80%.

child.name: Mukhyamantri Rajya Nirashrit Mahila Samman Pension Yojana
child.description: “Mukhyamantri Rajya Nirashrit Mahila Samman Pension Yojana” is a Social Security Scheme by the Department of Women, Child Development & Social Security, Government of Jharkhand, that aims to provide social security to women who are unable to sustain themselves with their own resources.

child.name: Mukhyamantri Sampoorna Pushti Yojana: Nutrition for Severely Underweight and Moderately Acute Malnourished Children (3 years - 6 years)
child.description: The scheme aims to improve child nutrition outcomes, with a specific focus on providing nutritional support to Severely Underweight (SUW) and Moderately Acute Malnourished (MAM) children aged between 3 years and 6 years.

child.name: Chief Minister's State Disability Pension Scheme - Sikkim
child.description: The scheme “Chief Minister's State Disability Pension Scheme (CMDPS)” was started by the Women & Child Welfare Department, Govt. of Sikkim to provide financial assistance to the differently abled person who has a disability of 40% and above.

child.name: Children Education Allowance
child.description: The scheme is implemented by the Nagaland Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board, Under this scheme, children of registered beneficiaries studying from Nursery to Class 10 are provided ₹500/- per month per child as education assistance.

child.name: Educational Allowances to the Children of Widows
child.description: “Educational Allowances to the Children of Widows” by the Dept of Women & Child Development, Govt of Pondicherry, was introduced to provide financial assistance (₹25 to ₹125) for the education of poor widows' children up to higher secondary or equivalent.

child.name: Protection Against Domestic Violence
child.description: The scheme "Protection Against Domestic Violence" by the Women and Child Development Department, Government of Haryana, aims to provide legal, emotional, and social support to women facing domestic violence through grievance redressal, offering protection, counseling, and mediation to victims.

child.name: Mukhya Mantri Vivah Shagun Yojna
child.description: This scheme is being implemented to honor the girl child and to ensure that the girls from poor families and daughters of widows/destitute women, sports women & orphan girl children, are married gracefully. For this purpose, grant is given to eligible persons to cover marriage expenses.

child.name: Mukhyamantri Rajya Bridhhabasta Pension Yojana
child.description: “Mukhyamantri Rajya Bridhhabasta Pension Yojana” is a Social Security Scheme by the Department of Women, Child Development & Social Security, Government of Jharkhand. The scheme provides financial asistance to the age group of 60 years and above.

child.name: Mukhyamantri Sampoorna Pushti Yojana: Nutrition for Severely Underweight and Moderately Acute Malnourished Children (6 months - 3 years)
child.description: The scheme aims to improve child nutrition outcomes, with a specific focus on providing nutritional support to Severely Underweight (SUW) and Moderately Acute Malnourished (MAM) children aged between 6 months and 3 years.

child.name: Allowance for 100% Blind
child.description: The scheme  “Allowance for 100% Blind" aims to provide a monthly pension of ₹2,000/- to the blind persons of Tripura aged 10 years and above having 100% blindness. The application should be submitted to the nearest Child Development Project Officer (CDPO).

child.name: RMEWF-Financial Assistance For Education Of Children & Widows Of Ex-Servicemen
child.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance of ₹1,000/- per month per child to a maximum of two dependent children of eligible Ex-Servicemen (ESM) or their widows for school and undergraduate education, and to widows pursuing a postgraduate degree.

child.name: Janani Shishu Suraksha Karyakram
child.description: The scheme aims to provide completely free and cashless delivery, including caesarean section, for pregnant women at government health facilities along with free treatment for sick newborns and infants.

child.name: Mangalya Scheme For Widow Remarriage
child.description: The “Mangalya Scheme” was launched by the Department of Women and Child Development, Government of Kerala. Under the scheme, financial assistance is to be provided to eligible widows and divorced women for their remarriage.

child.name: Grant of Financial Assistance for Performance of Marriage of Poor Brides Living Below Poverty Line
child.description: This scheme aims to provide financial assistance to poor brides living below the poverty line to meet their marriage expenses. The assistance amount is ₹25,000, and it is granted for the first marriage of the bride. The scheme is implemented through the Department of Women and Child Development.

child.name: Financial Assistance for Marriage (HPBOCWWB)
child.description: The Financial Assistance for Marriage scheme under the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board provides ₹51,000/- for the marriage of a registered unmarried beneficiary and ₹51,000/- per child for the marriage of up to two children of a married beneficiary.

child.name: Internship Programme for Women Students / Scholars / Social Activists / Teachers
child.description: The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched the Internship Programme aims to harness the talent of the trainees, giving them an opportunity to work towards bringing more efficiency in the implementation of the schemes.

child.name: Mamata Scheme
child.description: In order to improve the process in curbing the high rate of mortality and infant mortality rate, the Department of Women and Child Development, Government of Odisha introduced a scheme named "MAMATA" - a conditional cash transfer maternity benefit scheme.

child.name: Grant of Marriage Allowance to the Widow's Daughter
child.description: This scheme aims to provide financial support to widows for the marriage of their daughters. Eligible widows can receive ₹25,000/- to cover marriage expenses. The scheme is implemented through the Department of Women and Child Development, Government of Puducherry.

child.name: Sikkim Grant Of Award To Transgender
child.description: The scheme “Sikkim Grant of Award To Transgender (SGATT)” was launched in 2013 by the Department of Women & Child Welfare, Govt. of Sikkim. Under this scheme, the state government provides financial assistance to transgender belonging to a household of the Below Poverty Line category.

child.name: Foster Care Scheme (Vatsalaya)
child.description: The Foster Care Scheme (Vatsalaya) is being implemented by the Department of Women & Child Development, Government of Goa to provide temporary/substitute care for children in difficult circumstances, whose parents are unable to care for them due to illness, death, and desertion of parent, etc.

child.name: Annapurna Yojana
child.description: The scheme “Annapurna Yojana” implemented by the Women and Child Development and Social Welfare Department, Government of West Bengal aims to provide assured monthly financial assistance to eligible women residing in the state for their empowerment and socio-economic upliftment. Under the scheme, el

child.name: Scheme For Adolescent Girls
child.description: A support scheme by Ministry of Women & Child Development enabling self-development and empowerment of out-of-school adolescent girls, and improving their nutritional, health & hygiene  status. All out-of-school adolescent girls in the 11 to 14 years age group are eligible for this scheme.

child.name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme - Sikkim
child.description: The scheme “Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS)” by the Department of Women & Child Welfare, Govt. of Sikkim provides financial support is provided to the poor widows of the state of Sikkim. The beneficiary receives a monthly pension of ₹ 2000/- through DBT.

child.name: Children With Special Needs Under Goa Sarva Shiksha Abhivan
child.description: The "Children With Special Needs (CWSN)" scheme aims to provide comprehensive educational support and resources tailored to the needs of children with disabilities. It is designed to ensure that every child, regardless of their disability type (intellectual, physical, etc.), receives the necessary a

child.name: Sanitary Pad Supply Scheme
child.description: The "Sanitary Pad Supply Scheme" by the Women, Child Development & Social Security Department, Government of Jharkhand, aims to promote hygiene and health among women and adolescent girls. It provides free sanitary pads quarterly to women aged 20–40 years through Anganwadi centers.

child.name: Mukhya Mantrir Nijut Moina Aasoni
child.description: The scheme aims to support ten lakh girl students with financial grants as admission incentives for Higher Secondary, Graduation, and Post-Graduation studies to eradicate child marriage. It benefits unmarried girl students domiciled in Assam with monthly financial assistance.

child.name: Out of School Children scheme under Goa Sarva Shiksha Abhivan
child.description: The "Out of School Children" scheme is designed to ensure that every child in the 6-14 age group receives a complete elementary education. The primary objective is to identify children who are out of school and to reintegrate them into the educational system through specialized training programs.

child.name: Sikkim Unmarried Women Pension Scheme
child.description: The scheme “Sikkim Unmarried Women Pension Scheme (SUWPS)” was launched in 2013 by the Department of Women & Child Welfare, Govt. of Sikkim. Under this scheme, the unmarried women of Below Poverty Line (BPL) families in Sikkim would receive a pension of ₹ 2000/- per month.

child.name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme - Sikkim
child.description: The scheme “Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS)” was started by the Department of Women & Child Welfare, Govt. of Sikkim in 2014. Under this scheme, a pension is provided to elderly citizens above the age of 60 years belonging to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.

child.name: Swami Vivekananda Nishakta Swavlamban Protsahan Yojana
child.description: “Swami Vivekananda Nishakta Swavlamban Protsahan Yojana ” is a Social Security Scheme by the Department of Women, Child Development & Social Security, Government of Jharkhand. The aim of the scheme is to provide social security to differently abled people aged five(5) years and above.

child.name: Financial Assistance to Pensioners Suffering From HIV and AIDS
child.description: “Financial Assistance to Pensioners Suffering From HIV/AIDS” is a Social Security Scheme by the Department of Women, Child Development & Social Security, Government of Jharkhand which aims to provide social security through financial assistance to those persons who are suffering from HIV/AIDS.

child.name: Scheme for Adolescent Girls- Bihar
child.description: This scheme is implemented in Bihar since 2011 through the Anganwadi centers under the Integrated Child Development Project. Under this scheme, adolescent girls aged 11 to 14 years who are not attending school receive a monthly take-home ration and skill development training.

child.name: Scheme for Relief and Rehabilitation of Women Acid Victims
child.description: Under the scheme, the acid attack victim is entitled to receive 100% free medical treatment from government or government-approved hospitals. Additionally, an ad-hoc relief of ₹1.00 lakh is provided to the acid attack victim, which is later reimbursed by the Women and Child Development Department.

child.name: Gargi Puruskar Yojana
child.description: The benefit of Gargi Puraskar Yojana is given every year to the beneficiary girl child of Rajasthan on the day of Basant Panchami. This time also the beneficiary girl students were taken on a tour of the girl students on the occasion of Basant Panchami very year.

child.name: Bal Ashrams
child.description: The "Bal Ashrams" have been established by the Social Welfare Dept, Govt. of Jammu and Kashmir, to create a society free from child labor, where all children receive free, compulsory, quality, and meaningful education. Orphans and destitute children are provided free schooling, boarding and lodging.

child.name: Financial Assistance to Old Age Pensioner Suffering From TB/ Cancer
child.description: “Financial Assistance to Old Age Pensioner Suffering From TB/ Cancer” by the Dept of Women & Child Development, Govt of Puducherry, was introduced to extend a helping hand by way of financial assistance to Old Age/Destitute Pensioners who are suffering from TB/Cancer to meet the medical expenses.

child.name: Samekit Baal Vikas Chhtra Yojana: Dress for Pre-school Children
child.description: This scheme provides an amount of ₹400/- per child per year for uniforms to all children aged 3 to 6 years receiving pre-school education at Anganwadi Centres. The scheme aims to create a distinct identity for Anganwadi Centre children & bring a sense of uniformity & social harmony among children.

child.name: KULAVILAKKU
child.description: This scheme aims to enhance the nutritional status of Pregnant Women. ₹500/- assistance is provided to eligible beneficiaries during their pregnancies. The scheme is a crucial step towards ensuring the well-being of both mother and child. The benefit will be provided for upto two pregnancies only.

child.name: Non-Governmental Aided Organization (Special Schools And Vocational Training Centre) For Disabled
child.description: In this scheme, Special Education is provided to children with disability who are below the age of 18 years. The child should be enrolled in a special school run by a Non-Governmental Aided Organization. Vocational Training is given to special children above the age of 18 years.

child.name: Child Adoption - Tamil Nadu
child.description: The "Child Adoption - Tamil Nadu" scheme aims to provide a family for orphaned, abandoned, destitute and neglected children. According to Central Adoption Resource Authority (CARA), “adoption is a process by which a child who is abandoned/separated from biological parents, becomes the legitimate.

child.name: Tripura Scheme For Incentive To Girl Child
child.description: The scheme aims to provide an incentive to girl children. Through this scheme, an incentive is offered to the mother of the girl child or her legal guardian on a monthly basis.

child.name: Chief Minister’s Girl Child Protection Scheme-I
child.description: The 'Chief Minister’s Girl Child Protection Scheme-I' was launched by the Social Welfare and Women Empowerment Department, Govt. of Tamil Nadu. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to girl children of poor families as well as to promote girl child education & eradicate female infanticide.

child.name: Grant of Incentive to the Family having One Girl/Two Girl Children and Parents who have undergone Family Planning
child.description: This scheme aims to create awareness among parents about the desirability of the girl child and elevate the status of the girl child in the family. The scheme provides an amount of ₹30,000/- deposited in the name of the girl child, and in the case of two girl children, ₹15,000/- is deposited.

child.name: Grant of Financial Assistance to the Poor Parents Having Only One Girl Child Who is Studying 8th to 10th Standard
child.description: This scheme was introduced to promote small family norms, uplift the welfare of girl children, and ease the financial burden on parents during their daughter's marriage. The scheme provides ₹25,000/- financial assistance for single girl children studying in 8th to 10th standard.

child.name: Noni Suraksha Yojana
child.description: The scheme aims to improve the educational and health status of girls, lay the foundation for their bright future, prevent female feticide, promote a positive attitude towards the birth of girls, and prevent child marriage.  It provides financial benefit to girls who remain unmarried until 18 years.

child.name: Indira Gandhi Matritva Poshan Yojana
child.description: Under the scheme, women who conceive their second child and fulfill the prescribed conditions will receive cash assistance of ₹6,000/-, paid in five installments.

child.name: Ladli Beti Scheme
child.description: A social assistance scheme sponsored by the J&K Govt. for newborn girl children born on or after 01/04/2015. The objective is to arrest the declining female sex ratio. The scheme ensures that the girl child does not become a burden for the parent or guardian at the time of her marriage.

child.name: Vahli Dikri Yojana
child.description: The Vahli Dikri Yojana by the Government of Gujarat under the Women and Child Development Department . The scheme supports girl children by providing financial aid, free education, and insurance, encouraging higher education and reducing dropout rates.

child.name: Dikri Development Scheme-DNH&DD
child.state: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
child.description: The scheme aims at “Saving girls” as the primary objective and to improve the Sex Ratio at birth. Through this scheme, money is deposited in the name of the girl child under the LIC Single Premium Endowment Plan for the first two living girl children.

child.name: Thayi Magu Sahaya Hastha (K.B.O.C.W.W.B)
child.description: The "Thayi Magu Sahaya Hastha'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Karnataka, is a welfare scheme that provides financial assistance to registered women construction workers for pre-school education and nutritional support of their child for up to 3 years after delivery.

child.name: Sneha Sparsha
child.description: "Sneha Sparsha" is a healthcare scheme by the Dept. of Health & Family Welfare, Govt. of Assam. In this scheme, financial assistance shall be provided for specialized treatment of children below 12 years of age affected with some serious ailments.

child.name: Scheme for Supportive Money to the Parents of Transgender Children
child.description: The "Scheme for Supportive Money to the Parents of Transgender Children" aims to provide financial assistance of ₹1,000/- to the parents of transgender children. Transgender persons face stigma and discrimination in almost every sphere of their life including within the group, health facilities.

child.name: Mahtari Vandan Yojana
child.description: The scheme aims to eliminate discrimination and inequality against women, improve their health and nutrition, promote economic independence and empowerment, and strengthen their decision-making role in the family.  It offers financial assistance to eligible married women to support their well-being.

child.name: Mukhyamantri Noni Sashaktikaran Sahayata Yojana
child.description: Chhattisgarh State Government started "Mukhyamantri Noni Sashaktikaran Sahayata Yojana" for the girl child of the family.

child.name: Financial Assistance to Workers with a Visually, Physically or Mentally Disabled Child- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
child.description: Under this scheme, financial assistance is provided to the disabled (blind, mentally retarded, mute and deaf) children of registered workers employed in industrial and commercial establishments in the state of Haryana.

child.name: Palak Mata-Pita Yojana
child.description: The "Palak Mata-Pita Yojana" introduced on 29th August, 2009 by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Gujarat, provides a monthly assistance of ₹3000 to foster parents raising orphaned children or children whose mother has remarried after the father's death.

child.name: Mentally Retarded Children Benefit Scheme (HPBOCWWB)
child.description: In the scheme "Mentally Retarded Children Benefit" by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, an amount of ₹20,000/- per annum is provided to the registered workers to take care of their mentally retarded/disabled children.

child.name: Motor Vehicle Driver-Conductor Welfare Scheme- Maternity Assistance
child.description: This scheme provides financial assistance to eligible registered drivers and conductors to support their maternity-related needs during childbirth.

child.name: Bhagyalaxmi Scheme
child.description: The prime goal of this scheme aims to promote the birth of girl children in below poverty line(BPL) families and to raise the status of the girl child in the family in particular and society in general. Financial assistance is provided to the girl child through her mother/father or natural guardian.

child.name: Financial Assistance To Dependent Children Of Widows And Destitute Women
child.description: The scheme "Financial Assistance To Dependent Children of Widows And Destitute Women" is a Social Welfare scheme by the Dept. of Social Welfare, Women & Child Development, Govt. of Chandigarh; to extend financial assistance (₹ 1000/- per month per child) to children who are fatherless or orphan.

child.name: Beti Hai Anmol Yojana
child.description: The government of Himachal Pradesh launched a scheme for girls to encourage the education of the girl child below the poverty line (BPL) on 5 July 2010.

child.name: Sishu Saathi
child.description: “Sishu Saathi”, a scheme of the Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of West Bengal as part of the Rastriya Bal Swasthya Karyakram (RBSK) under National Health Mission is meant to treat Indian children of 0-18 years age group with zero out of pocket expenditure.

child.name: West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Maternity Benefit
child.description: This scheme provides financial assistance to a beneficiary on successful delivery of a child or miscarriage by such beneficiary or his wife.

child.name: Shaadi Mubarak Scheme
child.description: In order to prevent child marriages and support marriage expense of financially distressed families, on 02nd October 2014, Government of Telangana (GoTS) introduced a welfare scheme called “Shaadi Mubarak’’, providing financial assistance for marriages of minority community girls.

child.name: Supplementary Nutrition Programme- Bihar
child.description: The programme aims to enhance the nutritional level of normal/malnourished/severely malnourished children aged 6 months to 6 years, as well as pregnant women and lactating mothers, through Anganwadi centers. This programme is centrally sponsored with a funding ratio of 50:50.

child.name: Grant of Subsidy for Purchase of Mangalyam to the Bridegroom of Weaker Section
child.description: The scheme “Grant of Subsidy for Purchase of Mangalyam to the Bridegroom of Weaker Section” by the Dept of Women & Child Development, Govt of Pondicherry, was introduced to provide Financial Assistance of ₹ 2,500/- to the Bridegroom from Weaker Section.

child.name: Vishesh Yogyajan Palanhar Yojana
child.description: Under this scheme, children receive monthly financial support based on their age to ensure their well-being and education. To be eligible for assistance, the child must attend an Anganwadi/school between the ages of two to five years and study in school after the age of 6 years.

child.name: National Family Benefit Scheme - Sikkim
child.description: The scheme “National Family Benefit Scheme (NFBS)” was launched in 2016 by the Department of Women & Child Welfare, Govt. of Sikkim. Under this scheme, a lump sum amount of ₹ 30,000/- as financial assistance is provided to the bereaved household in the event of the death of the "Primary breadwinner"

child.name: Balri Birth Gift Scheme (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
child.description: "Balri Birth Gift Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Dept. of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that provides financial assistance in the form of a Fixed Deposit Receipt for the birth of a female child (upto two girls per family) which can be encashed only at the time of the daughter's marriage.

child.name: Goa Village & Municipal Child Committee Grants And Awards Scheme
child.description: GVMCCGS awards the Panchayats / Municipal Local Bodies to discuss and resolve the issues relating to children. The winner is awarded an annual financial grant of ₹ 10,000/- towards administrative expenses incurred on organizing the activities of the Committee.

child.name: ECCE (Early Childhood Care and Education)
child.description: The scheme provides quality early childhood care, development, and pre-primary education for children between the ages of 3 to 6 years. The scheme ensures safe, child-friendly infrastructure with furniture, TLM, & facilities in co-located Anganwadi centres within government school premises.

child.name: Bal Sakha Yojana
child.description: Under this scheme, all babies born to BPL mothers in the State (approximately 3,00,000 births per annum) will be covered for neonatal care by partnering pediatricians, including care in their Neonatal Intensive Care Unit (level 2 ) at no cost to the beneficiary.

child.name: Grant-In-Aid to Voluntary Organizations Working In The Area of ​​Disabled Welfare
child.description: "Grant-in-aid to Voluntary Organizations Working in the Area of Disabled Welfare" aims to support institutions working for the welfare of disabled individuals. Grants for free education, training, residential arrangements, and rehabilitation are provided to disabled immigrants.

child.name: Maternity Benefit (HPBOCWWB)
child.description: The "Maternity Benefit" scheme by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹25,000/- to registered female beneficiaries at the time of delivery or upon the birth of a child.

child.name: Kishori Balika Yojana (Madhya Pradesh)
child.description: The scheme "Kishori Balika Yojana" by the Department of Women and Child Development, Government of Madhya Pradesh aims to provide vocational, health, and hygiene training to adolescent girls. Through this scheme, training and material assistance are provided to the girls.

child.name: Mukhyamantri Madad Yojana
child.description: Under this scheme, a Scheduled Tribe family receives 50kg of wheat or rice and cooking utensils from the Panchayat upon the birth of a child. In the event of a family member's death, the family is provided with 1 quintal of wheat or rice.

child.name: Scheme For Welfare Of Children In Need Of Care And Protection
child.description: "Scheme for Welfare of Children in Need of Care and Protection" is a Social Welfare Scheme by the Directorate of Women & Child Development, Govt. of Goa. The objective of this scheme is to assist the Voluntary Organizations that cater to the needs of destitute and orphans up to the age of 18 years.

child.name: Mukhya Mantri Matritva Sahayata Yojana
child.description: The scheme "Mukhya Mantri Matritva Sahayata Yojana" by the Women and Child Development Department, Haryana, aims to provide a cash incentive of ₹5,000/- during maternity to eligible pregnant and lactating women on the birth of a second child (boy).

child.name: Mukhyamantri Balika Protsahan Yojana (Intermediate Pass)
child.description: The Mukhyamantri Balika (Intermediate Pass) Protsahan Yojana is a scheme by the Bihar government aimed at promoting girl child education, curbing child marriages, reducing fertility rates, and empowering girls to become self-reliant.

child.name: Working Women Hostel
child.description: The scheme "Working Women Hostel" by the Women and Child Development Department, Government of Haryana aims to provide safe and affordable accommodation to working women. Through this scheme, hostel facilities at subsidized rent are provided to employed women.

maternity.name: Maternity Benefit (A.B.O.C.W.W.B)
maternity.description: The scheme “Maternity Benefit” was started by the Assam Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (A.B.O.C.W.W.B), Labour Welfare Department, Government of Assam for providing financial assistance to the registered women beneficiaries as maternity benefits during the period of maternity.

maternity.name: Maternity Benefit
maternity.description: The "Maternity Benefit'' scheme by the BOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department,West Bengal, is a welfare scheme that provides maternity benefits up to two times in a lifetime to the eligible female beneficiaries or the wives of male beneficiaries.

maternity.name: Maternity Benefit (MBOCWWB)
maternity.description: Under the scheme, financial assistance in the form of maternity benefits shall be provided to women construction workers registered under the Board during their maternity period.

maternity.name: Maternity Benefit (ANBOCWWB)
maternity.description: The scheme offers maternity benefits to female employees registered as beneficiaries for one year during their maternity period. Additionally, male employees, registered for a year, with wives aged 19 or older and not beneficiaries, are also eligible for maternity benefits.

maternity.name: Maternity Benefit Scheme - Telangana
maternity.description: The "Maternity Benefit Scheme" was launched by the Telangana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, LET&F (Labour) Department, Telangana. The scheme provides financial assistance to registered women workers, as well as the wives & daughters of registered workers to support them.

maternity.name: Maternity Assistance Scheme (PBAOCWWB)
maternity.description: The "Maternity Assistance" is a Welfare Scheme by the Puducherry Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Union Territory of Puducherry. Through this scheme, the women employees who are beneficiary of the fund shall be given ₹5,000/- each as maternity benefit.

maternity.name: Maternity Benefit Scheme (HBOCWWB)
maternity.description: The scheme “Maternity Benefit Scheme (HBOCWWB)” was launched by the Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HBOCWWB), Labour Department Haryana.

maternity.name: Maternity Assistance for the Construction Workers
maternity.description: The "Maternity Assistance for the Construction Workers" by the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Delhi, is a welfare scheme  that provides maternity benefits of ₹ 30,000/- to the registered women members and wives of male members for upto 2 children.

maternity.name: Maternity Assistance Scheme- Gujarat Labour Welfare Board
maternity.description: The scheme provides financial assistance to female workers and the wives of workers to cover expenses incurred for health check-ups, medicine, nutritious diet, etc., during the maternity period.

maternity.name: Maternity Assistance for the Construction Worker
maternity.description: The "Maternity Assistance for the Construction Workers" by the Sikkim BOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department, is a welfare scheme of the board that provides maternity benefits in the form of financial assistance to the eligible  registered women members of the board.

maternity.name: Maternity Benefit (O.B.O.C.W.W.B)
maternity.description: The “Maternity Benefit” scheme by the BOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department, Odisha, is a welfare scheme that provides maternity benefits to the female beneficiaries subject to a maximum of two children who  are registered beneficiaries for a period of more than one year under the board.

maternity.name: Maternity Benefits Scheme (BBOCWWB)
maternity.description: "Maternity Benefits Scheme" provides an amount equivalent to 90 days of the minimum wages set by the State Govt. for unskilled workers at the time of childbirth. The applicant should be a female construction worker who has completed one year of membership with the Board.

maternity.name: Maternity Benefit Scheme (APB&OCWWB)
maternity.description: The Govt. of Arunachal Pradesh has proposed and implemented the scheme “Maternity Benefit Scheme” for Registered Workers of Arunachal Pradesh Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (APB&OCWWB).

maternity.name: Maternity Benefit For Female Workers- JKBOCWWB
maternity.description: The "Maternity Benefit for Female Workers" scheme by the JKBOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department, Jammu & Kashmir, is a welfare program that provides maternity benefits to eligible registered female workers up to two times in their lifetime.

maternity.name: Maternity Financial Assistance (UKBOCWWB)
maternity.description: The scheme aims to provide financial support to registered women construction workers during the maternity period. This assistance is admissible only for the birth of up to two children.

maternity.name: Maternity Benefit (CBOCWWB)
maternity.description: Under this scheme, financial assistance shall be given to the spouse of registered beneficiary woman employee after the delivery of child.

maternity.name: Maternity Benefit to the Female Beneficiary
maternity.description: The Manipur Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board (MBOCWWB) provide "Maternity Benefit" to female workers registered under the Act. Financial assistance is provided in cases of miscarriage or medical termination of pregnancy, up to two times.

maternity.name: Maternity Assistance Scheme (GBOCWWB)
maternity.description: The objective of this scheme is to provide financial assistance during pregnancy to women construction workers and construction workers' wives registered with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board and to provide social stability to construction workers.

maternity.name: Maternity Benefit for Women Beneficiaries
maternity.description: The "Maternity Benefit for Women Beneficiaries" scheme under the Nagaland Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board provides financial assistance to pregnant women registered as beneficiaries and wives of registered beneficiaries. The assistance can be availed up to two times.

maternity.name: Scheme for Maternity Benefit
maternity.description: The Scheme for Maternity Benefit was launched by the Tripura Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, under the Labour Department of Tripura. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to registered female construction workers after delivery

maternity.name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana
maternity.description: The scheme is to provide maternity benefits to women belonging to socially and economically disadvantaged sections of society. For first child the maternity benefit of ₹5,000 is provided in two instalments under PMMVY and second child, it is ₹6,000 is to be provided subject to second child is girl.

maternity.name: Mamata Scheme
maternity.description: In order to improve the process in curbing the high rate of mortality and infant mortality rate, the Department of Women and Child Development, Government of Odisha introduced a scheme named "MAMATA" - a conditional cash transfer maternity benefit scheme.

maternity.name: District Central Cooperative Banks and through its Branches: For Maternity Loan through Self Help Groups
maternity.description: The scheme offers financial aid to pregnant women via Self Help Groups. The loan is capped at ₹2,000/- with an interest rate of 11%, subject to changes.

maternity.name: Safai Karmakar Prasuti Sahayata Yojana
maternity.description: Chhattisgarh State Government started a scheme "Safai Karmakar Prasuti Sahayata Yojana" for unorganized worker on 11 March 2015.

maternity.name: Vandan Matru Yojana
maternity.description: This scheme is a Gujarat government initiative providing financial assistance to pregnant and lactating women from economically weaker sections to promote maternal and child health.

maternity.name: Bina Mulya Samajik Suraksha Yojana
maternity.description: "Bina Mulya Samajik Suraksha Yojana (BM-SSY)" is a comprehensive social security scheme for unorganized workers in the state of West Bengal, India. It was launched by the West Bengal government in 2017.

maternity.name: Laado Protsahan Yojana
maternity.description: The scheme aims to encourage the birth of girl children in poor families and promote their holistic development. Under this scheme, financial support ₹1,50,000/- is provided in seven installments to the parents or guardians of girls born in Government/authorized Medical Institutions.

maternity.name: Mukhyamantri Majadoor Suraksha Yojana
maternity.description: Mukhyamantri Majdur Suraksha Yojana provides registered rural and agriculture-based labourers in Madhya Pradesh with maternity assistance, scholarships for children, insurance cover and wage-loss compensation. Ensures social security for unorganised workers’ families.

maternity.name: Free Medical Assistance (Gujarat)
maternity.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance for expensive medical treatments to Scheduled Tribe individuals. Through this scheme, monetary aid is provided to Scheduled Tribe individuals suffering from various diseases and for serious maternity cases and anaemia in women.

maternity.name: Mukhya Mantri Shramik Seva Prasuti Sahayata Yojana
maternity.description: Launched on 1st April 2018, the scheme "Mukhya Mantri Shramik Seva Prasuti Sahayata Yojana" by the Labour Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, provides maternity support to pregnant women over 18 years of age who are registered as unorganized workers.

maternity.name: Maternity Benefit Scheme (APBOCWWB)
maternity.description: The Andhra Pradesh Building & Other Construction Workers have introduced the "Maternity Benefit Scheme" to registered construction workers. The scheme aims to support registered women workers, wives of registered male workers, & their daughters during maternity, reducing the financial strain.

maternity.name: Maternity Support Scheme (MPBOCWWB)
maternity.description: The "Maternity Support Scheme" by the MPBOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh provides an amount of ₹16,000/- as maternity allowance, payable in two installments.

maternity.name: Motor Vehicle Driver-Conductor Welfare Scheme- Maternity Assistance
maternity.description: This scheme provides financial assistance to eligible registered drivers and conductors to support their maternity-related needs during childbirth.

maternity.name: Maternity Benefit Scheme (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
maternity.description: The "Maternity Benefit Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that provides financial assistance to the female construction workers and spouse of the male workers to support childbirth-related expenses maximum for two living children.

maternity.name: Maternity Benefit/Delivery Assistance (K.B.O.C.W.W.B)
maternity.description: The "Maternity Benefit/Delivery Assistance'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Karnataka, is a welfare scheme that provides financial assistance to registered women construction workers for the birth of upto two (2) children.

maternity.name: Financial Assistance for Working Women Workers or Wives of Male Workers for Maternity- Haryana Labour Welfare Board
maternity.description: Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹10,000/- is provided to female workers and wives of male workers working in industrial and commercial establishments in the state of Haryana for the delivery of up to two children (three girls or two boys).

maternity.name: West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Maternity Benefit
maternity.description: This scheme provides financial assistance to a beneficiary on successful delivery of a child or miscarriage by such beneficiary or his wife.

maternity.name: Indira Gandhi Matritva Poshan Yojana
maternity.description: Under the scheme, women who conceive their second child and fulfill the prescribed conditions will receive cash assistance of ₹6,000/-, paid in five installments.

maternity.name: Maternity Benefit (HPBOCWWB)
maternity.description: The "Maternity Benefit" scheme by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹25,000/- to registered female beneficiaries at the time of delivery or upon the birth of a child.

maternity.name: Mukhya Mantri Matritva Sahayata Yojana
maternity.description: The scheme "Mukhya Mantri Matritva Sahayata Yojana" by the Women and Child Development Department, Haryana, aims to provide a cash incentive of ₹5,000/- during maternity to eligible pregnant and lactating women on the birth of a second child (boy).

pension.name: Pension To The Disabled Person
pension.description: The objective of the pension to the disabled person scheme is providing a pension to disabled persons is multifaceted, but generally centers around promoting social welfare, equity, and inclusivity.

pension.name: Mukhyamantri Vridhjan Pension Yojna
pension.description: The scheme “Mukhyamantri Vridhjan Pension Yojna” is an old-age pension scheme implemented by the Department of Social Welfare, Government of Bihar to provide financial assistance to elderly citizens. Under the scheme, a monthly pension is given to individuals who are aged 60 years or above.

pension.name: Pension Benefit
pension.description: The "Pension Benefit'' scheme by the BOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department,West Bengal, is a welfare scheme that provides a monthly pension after completion of 60 years to the eligible registered member.

pension.name: Nishaktata Pension Yojana (JBOCWWB)
pension.description: "Nishaktata Pension Yojana (JBOCWWB)" is a scheme by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training & Skill Development Govt of Jharkhand. The scheme provides a pension of ₹ 1,000/- per month to the registered beneficiary who is permanently disabled due to paralysis/leprosy/tuberculosis/accident.

pension.name: Jharkhand Sportsperson Pension Scheme
pension.description: The “Jharkhand Sportsperson Pension Scheme” aims to provide financial support to retired athletes for their lifelong contribution to sports. Athletes, including Arjuna, Dronacharya, and Dhyan Chand awardees, Olympic participants, and Commonwealth or Asian Games medalists, receive a monthly pension.

pension.name: Destitute Widow Pension Scheme
pension.description: 'Destitute Widow Pension Scheme' was launched by the Social Welfare and Women Empowerment Dept, Tamil Nadu. A pension of ₹1,000 is provided to destitute widows who are 18 years and above in age. One free saree during Pongal and Deepavali and free rice in the Anganwadi Centres are also provided.

pension.name: Disability Pension (HPBOCWWB)
pension.description: The scheme "Disability Pension," introduced by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB), Government of Himachal Pradesh, provides a monthly pension of ₹500/- to registered beneficiaries in case of disability caused by accidents or diseases.

pension.name: Pension Yojana (JBOCWWB)
pension.description: "Pension Yojana (JBOCWWB)" is a scheme by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training & Skill Development Govt of Jharkhand. The scheme provides pension of ₹ 1,000/- per month to those who have contributed to the JBOCWWB for at least 30 years.

pension.name: Mukhyamantri Kanya Abhibhawak Pension Scheme
pension.description: The Government of Madhya Pradesh has launched a scheme entitled “Mukhyamantri Kanya Abhibhavak Pension Yojana” on 01 April 201,  for the welfare of citizens having one daughter.

pension.name: Old Age Pension Scheme - Punjab
pension.description: Punjab State Government started a pension for old age person of the state people.

pension.name: Destitute/Deserted Wives Pension Scheme
pension.description: The 'Destitute/Deserted Wives Pension Scheme' is a state scheme to give a pension of ₹1,000/- to the destitute/deserted wives. The scheme provides other benefit every year during the Pongal and Deepavali festival one priceless saree is given.

pension.name: Differently Abled Pension Scheme
pension.description: The 'Differently Abled Pension Scheme' aims to provide a pension of ₹1,000/- to differently-abled persons with disability level 40% and above. The scheme provides other benefits every year during the Pongal and Deepavali festival saree, dhoti and a nutritious meal is supplied through Anganwadi.

pension.name: Continuation of Pension (K.B.O.C.W.W.B)
pension.description: The "Continuation of Pension'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Karnataka, is a welfare scheme that provides a monthly pension to registered construction workers who have paid subscriptions for at least three (3) years.

pension.name: Pension Scheme for the Journalists
pension.description: The "Pension Scheme for the Journalists" was launched by the Tamil Development, Religious Endowments, and Information Department, Government of Tamil Nadu. The scheme aims to give a monthly pension to retired journalists.

pension.name: General Pension (A.B.O.C.W.W.B)
pension.description: Under this scheme, a pension shall be given to a registered beneficiary working as a building worker for not less than one year after the commencement of the Rules on completion of sixty years of age.

pension.name: Widow / Destitute Women Pension Scheme - Punjab
pension.description: Punjab State Government started a pension for widow and destitute women.

pension.name: Widow/Abandoned/Single Woman Pension Scheme
pension.description: Widow/Abandoned/Single Woman Pension Scheme of Himachal Pradesh Government aims to provide financial help to widows, abandoned women, and unmarried women shall be entitled to receive pension for a sum of Rs.1,150/-per month.

pension.name: Destitute Widow Pension Scheme - Uttarakhand
pension.description: Under this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹1,500/- is provided to widows whose family income does not exceed ₹4,000/- per month or who belong to the BPL category, and who are not receiving any other pension benefits.

pension.name: Widow Pension (HBOCWWB)
pension.description: Under this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹3,000/- is given to the widow of a registered construction worker who is not receiving a similar benefit from any other Government/ Department/ Board/ Corporation /PSU.

pension.name: Mukhyamantri Aadim Jan Jati Pension Yojana
pension.description: The Mukhyamantri Aadim Jan Jati Pension Yojana, launched on 08-07-2015 by the Jharkhand Government, provides financial assistance to primitive tribal families. The pension is given to the adult married woman or, if none, the male head of the family.

pension.name: Swatantrata Sainik Samman Pension Scheme
pension.description: Launched on 15th August 1972, "Swatantrata Sainik Samman Pension Scheme" is a Pension Scheme by the Ministry of Home Affairs. The scheme provides the grant of pension to living freedom fighters and their families; if they are no more alive, and to the families or martyrs.

pension.name: State Disability Pension
pension.description: The Social Welfare & Tribal Affairs Department, Government of Mizoram launched the State Disability Pension scheme. It provides a monthly pension of ₹350 to 200 eligible individuals who are completely blind or bedridden.

pension.name: Disability Pension - West Bengal
pension.description: In 2010, the Women and Child Development and Social Welfare Department of the West Bengal State Government introduced the "Disability Pension" scheme.

pension.name: Viswakarma Pension Scheme
pension.description: The Scheme “Viswakarma Pension” was launched by the Backward Classes Development Department, Government of Kerala to give the pension to traditional workers of Viswakarma Communities.

pension.name: INDIRAMMA Widow Pension (Rural)
pension.description: "INDIRAMMA Weavers Pension (Rural)" scheme aims to disburse monthly pensions to widows, irrespective of their age. To be eligible, they should be from a BPL family, be a resident of the district, and not be covered under any other Pension Scheme.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Vidhva Pension yojana
pension.description: In the Indira Gandhi National Vidhva Pension Yojana (IGNWPS), financial support is provided to the widows of the state of Rajasthan.

pension.name: Pension Benefit for the Construction Worker
pension.description: The "Pension Benefit for the Construction Workers" by the Sikkim BOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department, is a welfare scheme of the board that provides pension to the registered beneficiaries of the Board who have completed the age of 60 years.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme
pension.description: A non-contributory pension scheme to provide social security to widows of poor family (BPL)  of the society.

pension.name: Old Age Pension - Chandigarh
pension.description: The Old Age Pension scheme aims to provide financial assistance and support to elderly citizens who have reached a certain age and require regular income for their sustenance.

pension.name: Chief Minister Pension Scheme
pension.description: The scheme provides a monthly pension of ₹500/- to senior citizens, widows, and abandoned women listed on the basis of the survey list of Social, Economic and Caste Census 2011 in the state.

pension.name: Media Person Pension Scheme
pension.description: The scheme “Media Person Pension Scheme” was launched by the Department of Information, Public Relations and Languages, Haryana on 07th December 2017.

pension.name: Pension Scheme for Cancer Patients
pension.description: The scheme “Pension Scheme for Cancer Patients" was launched by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura. The scheme aims to provide a monthly pension of ₹2,000/- to the person suffering from cancer.

pension.name: Pension Benefit (GBOCWWB)
pension.description: "Pension Benefit (GBOCWWB)" is a Welfare Scheme by the Goa Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Goa. A member of the Fund who has been working as a building worker for not less than one year, be eligible for a pension of ₹300/- per month, on completion of sixty years of age.

pension.name: Farmer Pension Scheme
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide financial support to elderly farmers residing in the state of Uttarakhand. Under this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹1200/- is provided to farmers who are above 60 years of age, own up to 2 hectares of land, and cultivate their own land.

pension.name: Pension Scheme - Folk Artist
pension.description: The "Pension Scheme" was launched by the Art and Culture Department, Government of Tamil Nadu. The scheme aims to provide a pension to the registered folk artists.

pension.name: Retired Sportsperson Pension Scheme
pension.description: The scheme "Retired Sportsperson Pension Scheme" by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat aims to ensure financial security to retired state-level medal-winning sportspersons aged above 50 years through monthly pension assistance.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (Punjab)
pension.description: "Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS)" provides a pension of ₹300/- per month to widows aged 40 years and above. After attaining the age of 80 years, the pension amount increases to ₹500/- per month.

pension.name: Matribhasha Satyagrahi Pension Scheme
pension.description: The scheme “Matribhasha Satyagrahi Pension Scheme” was launched by the Department of Information, Public Relations and Languages, Haryana on 12th December 2017. Under the scheme, the Govt. of Haryana is providing a monthly pension of ₹10,000/- to the Matribhasha Satyagrahis of Hindi Aandolan-1957.

pension.name: Deserted Women Pension Scheme
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide a monthly pension to Abandoned married women, mentally ill husband/wife & destitute unmarried women in the state. Under this scheme, beneficiaries receive a pension of ₹1,200/- per month, & in case of mental disability (spouse), the amount is ₹1,400/- per month.

pension.name: Disability Pension (A.B.O.C.W.W.B)
pension.description: The Board provides financial assistance as disability pension to a beneficiary who is permanently disabled due to paralysis leprosy, TB., accident etc. in addition to this pension he/she will be eligible for an one time Ex-gratia payment depending upon the percentage of disability.

pension.name: Pension to Indigent Sportspersons of Odisha
pension.description: The Scheme relating to "Pension to Indigent Sportspersons of Odisha" was launched by the Department of Sports & Youth Services Department, Government of Odisha to provide financial support to sportspersons of yesteryears who are living in indigent conditions.

pension.name: Pension Scheme for Transgender
pension.description: Launched on 1st August 2015, the scheme "Pension Scheme for Transgender" is a Pension Scheme by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura. The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹2,000/- to the transgender residents of Tripura who have no source of income.

pension.name: Bihar Disability Pension Scheme
pension.description: The objective of this scheme is to provide social security to the persons with disabilities (PwDs) of the poor family of the society by providing them financial assistance. Under this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹400/- is given to PwDs of 40% or more, regardless of their income or age group.

pension.name: Haryana Shubhra Jyotsna Pension Scheme
pension.description: The scheme “Haryana Shubhra Jyotsna Pension Scheme” was launched by the General Administration Department, Government of Haryana on 16th April 2018.

pension.name: Mukhyamantri Vishesh Yogyajan Sammaan Pension Yojana
pension.description: The "Mukhyamantri Vishesh Yogyajan Samman Pension Yojana" (MVSYS) stands as a testament to the commitment of the Government of Rajasthan towards the welfare of its citizens, particularly those with disabilities.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (Maharashtra)
pension.description: The "Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (Maharashtra)" is implemented by the Social Justice & Special Assistance Dept., Govt. of Maharashtra. The differently-abled individuals aged 18 to 65 years with a disability of 80% and above are eligible to receive a pension of ₹600/- per month.

pension.name: Old-Age Pension Scheme
pension.description: The Himachal Pradesh government offers an Old Age pension scheme to support elderly citizens who have no means of livelihood. The scheme offers financial assistance to eligible citizens based on their age and income level.

pension.name: INDIRAMMA Disabled Pension (Urban)
pension.description: "INDIRAMMA Disabled Pension (Urban)" scheme aims to provide monthly pensions to persons with disabilities of 40% or above who are 18 years of age or above. To be eligible, they should be from a BPL family, be a resident of Andhra Pradesh, and not be covered under any other Pension Scheme.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme - Tamil Nadu
pension.description: 'Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme' was launched by the Social Welfare and Women Empowerment Dept, Tamil Nadu. A pension of ₹1,000 is provided to destitute widows who are 40 years and above in age and belong to households below the poverty line.

pension.name: Scheme of Pension for Eminent Sports Persons
pension.description: The scheme “Scheme of Pension for Eminent Sports Persons” was launched by the Department of Sports, Government of Punjab to provide pension to eminent veteran sportsmen who have excelled at International level during their youth and made the nation proud.

pension.name: INDIRAMMA Weavers Pension (Rural)
pension.description: "INDIRAMMA Weavers Pension (Rural)" scheme aims to disburse monthly pensions to destitute weavers who are 50 years or above in age. To be eligible, they should be from a BPL family, be a resident of the district, and not be covered under any other Pension Scheme.

pension.name: Shravanbal Seva State Pension Scheme
pension.description: The "Shravanbal Seva State Pension Scheme" is a Pension Scheme by the Department of Social Justice & Special Assistance, Govt. of Maharashtra. In this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹ 600/- is provided to destitute persons in the state of Maharashtra.

pension.name: Widow Pension (CBOCWWB)
pension.description: Under this scheme, financial assistance in the form of a monthly pension shall be given to the widows of male registered construction workers of the Board.

pension.name: Uttar Pradesh Old Age Pension Scheme
pension.description: The Government of Uttar Pradesh has introduced an old-age pension scheme for the benefit of the senior citizens in the state. Under this scheme, eligible senior citizens will be provided with financial assistance in the form of a monthly pension.

pension.name: Old Age Pension
pension.description: In 2010, the Women and Child Development and Social Welfare Department of the West Bengal State Government introduced the "Old Age Pension" scheme.

pension.name: National Pension Scheme For Traders And Self Employed Persons
pension.description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme by the Ministry of Labour and Employment. This scheme is meant for old age protection and social security of Small Scale Traders and Retailers. The beneficiary would receive a minimum assured pension of ₹ 3000/- per month after attaining the age of 60 yrs.

pension.name: INDIRAMMA Disabled Pension (Rural)
pension.description: "INDIRAMMA Disabled Pension (Rural)" scheme aims to disburse monthly pensions to the individuals 18 years or above in age and having a disability of 40% or above. To be eligible, they should be from a BPL family, be a resident of the district, and not be covered under any other Pension Scheme.

pension.name: Teelu Rauteli Pension Scheme
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide a monthly pension of ₹1,200/- to male and female individuals above 18 years of age who have incurred a partial disability of 20% to 40% while performing agricultural activities.

pension.name: Disability Pension For Registered Worker (HBOCWWB)
pension.description: The scheme “Disability Pension for Registered Worker (HBOCWWB)” was launched by the Haryana Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HBOCWWB), Labour Department Haryana.

pension.name: Dwarf Pension Scheme
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide financial support to individuals suffering from dwarfism to help them meet their basic needs and improve their quality of life. Under this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹1200/- is provided to persons above 21 years of age whose height is less than 4 feet.

pension.name: Transgender Pension Scheme
pension.description: The Transgender Pension Scheme is a welfare initiative launched by the Government of Himachal Pradesh for the upliftment and support of transgender persons.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (Maharashtra)
pension.description: "Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (Maharashtra)" is a scheme by the Social Justice & Special Assistance Dept, Govt of Maharashtra. In this scheme, widows aged 40 to 79 years who are from a BPL family receive a pension of ₹1500/- per month.

pension.name: Jharkhand Patrakar Samman Pension Niyamawali
pension.description: The "Jharkhand Patrakar Samman Pension Niyamawali" aims to provide financial assistance to journalists who have served the state for an extended period. It ensures their well-being post-retirement and acknowledges their contribution to media and society.

pension.name: NSAP - Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme
pension.description: "Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme" is one of the five sub-schemes of the National Social Assistance Programme. Under IGNOAPS, citizens living Below Poverty Line and 60 years or above in age are eligible to apply. A monthly pension of ₹ 200 up to 79 years and ₹ 500 thereafter.

pension.name: Mukhyamantri Vriddhjan Samman Pension Yojana
pension.description: The Rajasthan government's Social Justice and Empowerment Department launched the "Mukhyamantri Vriddhjan Samman Pension Yojana" program, a social welfare initiative that aims to improve the lives of senior citizens residing in the state by providing them with monthly financial assistance.

pension.name: Varishtha Pension Bima Yojana
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide a pension scheme for senior citizens. Through this scheme, immediate annuity, pension, and return of purchase price on death are provided to citizens aged 60 years and above.
tags: Return Of Purchase Price On Death

pension.name: INDIRAMMA Widow Pension (Urban)
pension.description: "INDIRAMMA Widow Pension (Urban)" scheme aims to provide monthly pensions to widows irrespective of their age. To be eligible, they should be from a BPL family, be a resident of a district of Andhra Pradesh, and not be covered under any other Pension Scheme.

pension.name: Disability Pension (GBOCWWB)
pension.description: Through the "Disability Pension" scheme, the worker who is permanently disabled due to Paralysis / Leprosy / Tuberculosis(TB) / Accident is provided with financial assistance of ₹300/- per month, and an ex-gratia payment of not more than ₹10,000/-, depending on the percentage of the disability.

pension.name: Pension Scheme (K.B.O.C.W.W.B)
pension.description: The "Pension Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Karnataka, is a welfare scheme that aims to provide a monthly pension to registered construction workers who have reached 60 years of age and is a beneficiary of the Board for at least 3 years.

pension.name: Family Pension (HBOCWWB)
pension.description: Under this scheme, on the death of a pensioner worker who is being given pension by the Haryana Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, half of his/her pension is given to his/her spouse (as the case may be).

pension.name: Pension Scheme for Grade II Deformed Leprosy Patients
pension.description: The scheme "Pension Scheme for Grade II Deformed Leprosy Patients" is a Pension Scheme by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura. The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹2,000/- per month to Leprosy Patients who are residents of Tripura.

pension.name: Samagra Samajik Suraksha Vridhavastha Pension Yojana
pension.description: Initiative of the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities under the Madhya Pradesh started the scheme "Samagra Samajik Suraksha Vridhavastha Pension Yojana" for the older age people of the state.

pension.name: Haryana Divyang Pension Scheme
pension.description: This is a State scheme under which disabled persons of Haryana domicile with a minimum 60% disability and are 18 years of age and above, are given pension as per eligibility criteria laid down in the rules of the scheme.

pension.name: Mukhyamantri Kanya Abhibhavak Pension Yojana
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide financial support to parents or couples who have only daughters. The scheme provides a recurring monthly pension amount of ₹500/- (Rupees 500) to the eligible recipient or couple. The applicant must be a resident of the state of Madhya Pradesh.

pension.name: Pension Scheme (BBOCWWB)
pension.description: Under this scheme, registered construction workers who have completed a minimum of five years of membership and have attained the age of 60 are eligible for a pension of ₹1,000/- per month.

pension.name: Destitute Differently Abled Pension Scheme
pension.description: The "Destitute Differently Abled Pension Scheme" was launched by the Social Welfare and Nutritious Meal Programme Department, Government of Tamil Nadu. The scheme aims to give a pension of ₹1,000/- to destitute differently-abled persons aged 45 years and above with disability level of 60% and above.

pension.name: Social Security Pension Scheme
pension.description: Under this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹500/- is provided to Below Poverty Line (BPL) Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) and dwarf individuals to support them in living a respectful life.

pension.name: Atal Pension Yojana
pension.description: Atal Pension Yojana (APY) is an old age income security scheme for a  savings account holder  in the age group of 18-40 years,  who is not an income tax-payee. It helps to address longevity risks among  workers in unorganized sector and encourages the workers to voluntarily save for retirement.

pension.name: Pension Scheme (HBOCWWB)
pension.description: Under this scheme, a member of the fund who has been working as a building worker for not less than three years shall on completion of sixty years of age be eligible for a pension of ₹3,250/- per month.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (Jammu and Kashmir)
pension.description: Launched on 15th August 1995, the "Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme"is a pension scheme by the Department Of Social Welfare  Jammu and Kashmir. In this scheme, a pension of ₹1000/- is provided to differently-abled individuals from 'Below Poverty Line'.

pension.name: Sikkim Unmarried Women Pension Scheme
pension.description: The scheme “Sikkim Unmarried Women Pension Scheme (SUWPS)” was launched in 2013 by the Department of Women & Child Welfare, Govt. of Sikkim. Under this scheme, the unmarried women of Below Poverty Line (BPL) families in Sikkim would receive a pension of ₹ 2000/- per month.

pension.name: Goa Welfare / Pension Scheme For Seafarers
pension.description: The scheme "Goa Welfare / Pension Scheme for Seafarers" by the Home Department, Government of Goa, provides financial assistance in the form of a Gross Pension of ₹ 2,500/- per month to the beneficiaries. The applicant should be a retired seaman. The age of the applicant should be 60 years or above.

pension.name: Chief Ministergi Artistesinggi Tengbang (CMAT) – Artiste’s Pension
pension.description: The scheme "Chief Ministergi Artistesinggi Tengbang (CMAT) – Artiste’s Pension" by the Dept. of Art and Culture, Govt. of Manipur provides a monthly pension of up to ₹ 4,000/- to the aged reputed artists who have contributed to making the tradition, culture, and art forms of Manipur more vibrant.

pension.name: Chief Minister Small and Marginal Farmers Older Pension Scheme
pension.description: The "Chief Minister Small and Marginal Farmers Older Pension Scheme" by the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of Rajasthan, aims to provide financial assistance to elderly small and marginal farmers.

pension.name: Nirman Shramik Pension Yojana (NSPY) (O.B.O.C.W.W.B)
pension.description: The “Nirman Shramik Pension Yojana (NSPY)” scheme by the BOCW Welfare Board, Labour Department, Odisha, is a social welfare scheme that provides pension to the eligible registered beneficiary or their dependents in certain circumstances.

pension.name: Old Age Pension Scheme (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
pension.description: The "Old Age Pension Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that provides pension to registered construction workers aged above 60 years and have completed 3 years of membership with the Board.

pension.name: Toposili Bandhu (Old Age Pension) Scheme
pension.description: "Toposili Bandhu" (Old Age Pension) scheme was initiated by the Backward Classes Welfare Department and Tribal Development Department of West Bengal Government.

pension.name: Delhi Pension Scheme To Women In Distress (Widow Pension)
pension.description: Delhi Pension Scheme to Women in Distress is a welfare scheme to provide social security by way of financial assistance to widows, divorced, separated, abandoned, deserted or destitute women in the age group of 18 years of lifelong who do not have adequate means of subsistence and are poor & needy.

pension.name: Madhu Babu Pension Yojana
pension.description: "Madhu Babu Pension Yojana" under the Social Security and Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities Department, Government Of Odisha is a social welfare scheme designed to provide financial assistance to elderly citizens, widows, and disabled persons who are economically weaker.

pension.name: Swahid Kushal Konwar Sarbajanin Briddha Pension Achoni
pension.description: Launched on 2nd October 2018, "Swahid Kushal Konwar Sarbajanin Briddha Pension Achoni" is a Pension Scheme by the Govt. of Assam. The objective of the scheme is to streamline the payment of old-age pensions and funds. The beneficiary will get a sum of ₹250/- per month.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Vrdhavastha Pension Yojana
pension.description: In this scheme, pension is payable to persons below from BPL category families listed as per the norms of the Government of India.

pension.name: Widow Pension-West Bengal
pension.description: The West Bengal government's Women and Child Development and Social Welfare Department launched the "Widow Pension" scheme in 2010. This program aims to support widows facing economic hardship within the state.

pension.name: Pension Benefit (TBOCWW)
pension.description: The "Pension Benefit" scheme was launched by the Tripura Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department Tripura. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to the registered beneficiary who has retired due to old age attaining 60 years.

pension.name: Pension Scheme for Destitute Transgender
pension.description: The 'Pension Scheme for Destitute Transgender' was launched by the Social Welfare and Women Empowerment Department, Government of Tamil Nadu. The scheme aims to help poor transgender above 40 years of age who are unable to earn their livelihood.

pension.name: Pension Benefit (ANBOCWWB)
pension.description: Under this scheme, pension shall be provided to the Building and Other Construction Workers who are registered under the A&N Islands Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board upon attaining the age of 55 years.

pension.name: INDIRAMMA Weavers Pension (Urban)
pension.description: "INDIRAMMA Weavers Pension (Urban)" scheme aims to provide monthly pensions to destitute weavers aged 50 years or above. To be eligible, they should be from a BPL family, and be a resident of Andhra Pradesh. The eligible individuals will be identified by their respective Ward Sabhas.

pension.name: Disability Pension (UKBOCWWB)
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide a monthly pension of ₹1,000 to construction workers registered with Uttarakhand Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board who have become permanently disabled due to paralysis, leprosy, accidents, etc. In addition, an ex-gratia amount of ₹40,000 is also provided.

pension.name: Financial Assistance/Pension to Former Renowned Sportspersons
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide lifelong monthly financial assistance ranging from ₹4,000/- to ₹10,000/- in the form of a pension to former renowned sportspersons of Uttarakhand who are above 50 years of age and are financially weak.

pension.name: Pension to the Persons who lost 100% eye sight
pension.description: “Pension to the Persons Who Lost 100% Eye Sight" was launched by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura. The scheme aims to provide a monthly pension of ₹2,000/- to the differently-abled persons who are 10 years and above in age and have 100% blindness.

pension.name: World War II Pension
pension.description: The aim of the scheme is to provide lifelong financial assistance of ₹15,000/- per month as a measure of relief to World War II ex-servicemen and their widows who are not receiving any pension or financial assistance from any other source.

pension.name: Divyang Pension Scheme - Uttarakhand
pension.description: The scheme aims to support persons with disabilities in meeting their basic needs and improving their quality of life. Under this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹1,500/- is provided to persons with disabilities who are above18 years of age & have a disability of more than 40%.

pension.name: Pension to Widows and Destitute Women
pension.description: Under this scheme, widows and destitute women aged 18 years and above are eligible for a pension if they are domiciles of Haryana and have been residing in the state for at least one year at the time of application submission.

pension.name: Scheme for Grant of Pension Benefits to Journalists
pension.description: The scheme "Scheme for Grant of Pension Benefits to Journalists" offers a monthly pension of ₹6,000/- to qualified journalists who are 58 years or above in age, and have served for a minimum of 20 years in roles such as Full-time Editor, Reporter, Sub-Editor, Press Photographer/Videographer, etc.

pension.name: West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Pension
pension.description: The scheme provides pension benefits to transport workers of the state upon reaching 60 years of age.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme - Sikkim
pension.description: The scheme “Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS)” by the Department of Women & Child Welfare, Govt. of Sikkim provides financial support is provided to the poor widows of the state of Sikkim. The beneficiary receives a monthly pension of ₹ 2000/- through DBT.

pension.name: Old Age Pension Scheme (APB&OCWWB)
pension.description: The scheme “Old Age Pension Scheme” was started by the Department of Labour and Employment, Govt. of Arunachal Pradesh for the registered workers of Arunachal Pradesh Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (APB&OCWWB).

pension.name: Disability Pension for the Construction Workers
pension.description: The "Disability Pension for the Construction Workers" by the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Delhi, is a welfare scheme that provides monthly disability pension of ₹3000/- to the permanently disabled member due to paralysis, leprosy, T.B and accidents.

pension.name: Silicosis Rehabilitation Pension- Haryana Silicosis Board
pension.description: Under this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹4,000/- is given to the Silicosis-affected worker categorized as category (A, B, or C) according to the ILO Classification, until he/she remains alive.

pension.name: Old Age Pension To Handloom Weavers Under Coop. Fold
pension.description: The “Old Age Pension To Handloom Weavers” scheme implemented by the MSME and Textiles Dept., aims to provide monthly financial assistance in the form of pension to a weaver member of a handloom cooperative society.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme - Tamil Nadu
pension.description: The 'Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme' is a state initiative by the Social Welfare and Women Empowerment Department, Government of Tamil Nadu. Its goal is to offer a ₹1,000 pension to individuals aged 18 and above. Eligibility criteria include a disability percentage of 80% or above.

pension.name: Disability Pension and Ex-Gratia (K.B.O.C.W.W.B)
pension.description: The "Disability Pension and Ex-Gratia'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Karnataka, is a welfare scheme that provides a monthly pension to beneficiaries partially disabled due to worksite accidents or diseases, with an additional ex-gratia payment based on disability percentage.

pension.name: Pension to Unmarried Poor, Incapacitated Women of Age 50 years and above
pension.description: The 'Pension to Unmarried Poor, Incapacitated Women of age 50 years and above' was launched by the Social Welfare and Women Empowerment Department, Tamil Nadu. The scheme aims to provide a pension of ₹1,000/- to unmarried, poor, incapacitated women of age 50 years and above.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme-Madhya Pradesh
pension.description: The Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme is being operated to provide financial support to widowed women living below the poverty line.

pension.name: Mukhyamantri Rajya Bridhhabasta Pension Yojana
pension.description: “Mukhyamantri Rajya Bridhhabasta Pension Yojana” is a Social Security Scheme by the Department of Women, Child Development & Social Security, Government of Jharkhand. The scheme provides financial asistance to the age group of 60 years and above.

pension.name: Disability Pension (MBOCWWB)
pension.description: Under the scheme, the Board shall provide disability pension to a beneficiary who is permanently disabled due to paralysis, leprosy, T.B., accidents,etc. Additionally, workers will be eligible for an exgratia payment based on percentage of disability & subject to condition as may fixed by the Board.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme -Tamil Nadu
pension.description: The 'Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme' was launched by the Social Welfare and Women Empowerment Department, Government of Tamil Nadu. A pension of ₹1,000 is provided to destitute old age persons of 60 years and above belonging to households below the poverty line.

pension.name: Pension Benefit for the Construction Workers
pension.description: The "Pension Benefit for the Construction Workers" by the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Delhi, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board with a pension benefit of ₹ 3000/- per month after completion of 60 years.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme - Jharkhand
pension.description: Under this scheme, a monthly pension is provided to severely disabled and multi-disabled individuals aged 18 and above who are living below the poverty line (BPL) to support them in living a respectful life.

pension.name: Jai Johar (Old Age Pension) Scheme
pension.description: “Jai Johar (Old Age Pension)” is a Social Security Scheme by the Tribal Development Dept, Govt of West Bengal. The scheme is aimed at the ST residents of West Bengal of the age of 60 years and above. A monthly pension of ₹ 1,000/- is transferred to the individual bank account of the beneficiary.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Special Ability Pension
pension.description: National Disability Pension Scheme is a government scheme aimed at providing financial assistance to specially-abled individuals residing in India.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme- Bihar
pension.description: Under this scheme, a monthly pension is provided to elderly people (women/men) aged 60 years or above belonging to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families to support them financially. This scheme is centrally sponsored.

pension.name: The Andaman & Nicobar Islands Widow Pension Scheme
pension.description: The scheme “The Andaman & Nicobar Islands Widow Pension Scheme” is implemented by the Directorate of Social Welfare, Union Territory of Andaman and Nicobar Islands to provide financial assistance to poor and destitute widows in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

pension.name: Scheme for Pension Benefits to the Journalists/Photojournalists
pension.description: "Scheme for Pension Benefits to the Journalists/photojournalists" was launched by the Department of Information and Cultural Affairs, Govt. of Tripura aims to provide financial assistance in the form of a Pension of ₹1000/- to retired Journalists/ photojournalists.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme- Bihar
