pension.description: Under this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹400/- is provided to persons with disabilities in the age group of 18 to 79 years who belong to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families to support them financially. This scheme is centrally sponsored.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme- Bihar
pension.description: Under this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹400/- is provided to widows in the age group of 40 to 79 years who belong to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families to support them financially. This scheme is centrally sponsored.

pension.name: Pension Scheme for the Tappers in Small Rubber Plantations
pension.description: The scheme “Pension Scheme for the Tappers in Small Rubber Plantations” was launched by the Rubber Board, Ministry of Commerce & Industry, Government of India to motivate tappers and retain them in the tapping job.

pension.name: Old Age Pension Scheme under Chief  Minister’s Uzhavar Padhukappu Thittam
pension.description: The 'Old Age Pension Scheme under Chief Minister’s Uzhavar Padhukappu Thittam' is a state scheme. The scheme aims to give a pension of ₹1,000/- to old-age farmers and destitute agricultural labourers who have membership card under CMPUT.

pension.name: Integrated Social Security Scheme: Pension to Physically Challenged Persons
pension.description: Under this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹1,000/- is provided to poor physically handicapped persons (including those who are blind, deaf, or dumb) with a degree of disability 40% or above and having no source of income.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme- Chhattisgarh
pension.description: Under this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹500/- is provided to severely disabled (with a disability of 80% and above) and multi-disabled individuals aged 18 to 79 years who are living below the poverty line (BPL) to support them in living a respectful life.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (Jammu and Kashmir)
pension.description: Launched on 15th August 1995, the "Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme" is a pension scheme by the Social Welfare Department, Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir. The scheme provides monthly financial assistance of ₹1,000/- to Below Poverty Line (BPL) individuals.

pension.name: Old Age Pension Scheme - Uttarakhand
pension.description: Under this scheme, a monthly pension of ₹1,500/- is provided to senior citizens (both husband and wife) in the state who are 60 years of age or above and whose family’s monthly income is up to ₹4,000/- or who belong to the BPL category.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (Punjab)
pension.description: The "Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (IGNDPS)" provides ₹300/- per month to disabled persons (80% or more disability) aged 18 and above. After age 80, the amount increases to ₹500/-. Dwarfs are also eligible.

pension.name: Old Age Pension Scheme- On Completion Of 65 Years (UKBOCWWB)
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide a monthly pension of ₹1,500/- to construction workers registered with the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board who have attained the age of 65 years.

pension.name: Integrated Social Security Scheme: Old Age Pension
pension.description: Under this scheme, pension is provided to old aged persons males (60 years & above) and females (55 and above) and who are destitute in the sense of having no means of subsistence from their own source of income or through financial support from family members or other sources.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Disabled Pension Scheme (Madhya Pradesh)
pension.description: Launched on 1st April 2009, the scheme "Indira Gandhi National Disabled Pension Scheme" is a pension scheme by the Department of Social Welfare, Govt of Madhya Pradesh. In this scheme, a pension of ₹600/- is provided to differently-abled individuals from 'Below Poverty Line'.

pension.name: Integrated Social Security Scheme - Old Age Pension
pension.description: ISSS-OAP is a State Sponsored Scheme by the Social Welfare Dept. of the Govt. of Jammu and Kashmir wherein financial assistance is provided to Old Age Persons who have meager support or no source of livelihood. A pension of ₹1000 per pensioner per month is deposited directly into their Bank Account.

pension.name: Hiv / Aids Pension Scheme
pension.description: The Government of Puducherry introduced the HIV / AIDS Pension Scheme to extend a helping hand through monthly financial assistance to HIV/AIDS Affected People so that they could defray some petty personal/ medical expenses without depending on others.

pension.name: Integrated Social Security Scheme - Pension For Physically Challenged Persons
pension.description: ISSS-PPCP is a State Sponsored Scheme by the Social Welfare Dept. of the Govt. of J&K wherein financial assistance is provided to Physically Challenged Person who have meager support or no source of livelihood. A pension of ₹1000 per pensioner per month is deposited into their bank account.

pension.name: Additional Pension to Widow of Ex-Serviceman having No Child
pension.description: Under the scheme, an additional amount of ₹100/- per month, in addition to the pension amount, is provided to the widow of an ex-serviceman who dies while in service but not in any battle.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme - Sikkim
pension.description: The scheme “Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (IGNDPS)” was started by the Department of Women & Child Welfare, Govt. of Sikkim in 2014. Under this scheme, financial assistance is provided to differently-abled persons with severe or multiple disabilities of more than 80%.

pension.name: "Monthly Grant To Old Crafts Persons (Pension)" Component of the "Development of Handicrafts" Scheme
pension.description: "Monthly Grant To Old Crafts Persons (Pension)” by the Department of Industries and Commerce, Union Territory of Puducherry, aims to provide pensions to artisans in their old age, recognizing their contribution to traditional crafts. Eligible craftspersons receive a monthly grant of ₹2,000/-.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme - Sikkim
pension.description: The scheme “Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS)” was started by the Department of Women & Child Welfare, Govt. of Sikkim in 2014. Under this scheme, a pension is provided to elderly citizens above the age of 60 years belonging to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.

pension.name: Chief Minister's State Disability Pension Scheme - Sikkim
pension.description: The scheme “Chief Minister's State Disability Pension Scheme (CMDPS)” was started by the Women & Child Welfare Department, Govt. of Sikkim to provide financial assistance to the differently abled person who has a disability of 40% and above.

pension.name: Mukhyamantri Rajya Nirashrit Mahila Samman Pension Yojana
pension.description: “Mukhyamantri Rajya Nirashrit Mahila Samman Pension Yojana” is a Social Security Scheme by the Department of Women, Child Development & Social Security, Government of Jharkhand, that aims to provide social security to women who are unable to sustain themselves with their own resources.

pension.name: INDIRAMMA Old Age Pension (Rural)
pension.description: "INDIRAMMA Old Age Pension (Rural)" aims to disburse monthly pensions to old age citizens, both male and female, who are 65 years of age or above and are destitute (with little or no means of subsistence and no family or relative to depend upon). To be eligible, they should be from a BPL family.

pension.name: INDIRAMMA Old Age Pension (Urban)
pension.description: "INDIRAMMA Old Age Pension (Urban)" scheme aims to provide monthly pensions to destitute elderly persons aged 65 years or above with little or no means of subsistence and no family or relative to depend upon. To be eligible, they should be from a BPL family, and be a resident of Andhra Pradesh.

pension.name: Pension Scheme to the Dependents (Husband/Wife) of Identified State Agitators, Other than those Who either went to Jail for 7 Days or were Injured During the Uttarakhand State Movement, after the Death of the Agitator
pension.description: The scheme provides a monthly pension of ₹4,500/- to the dependents (husband/wife) of identified state agitators, excluding those jailed for 7 days or injured during the Uttarakhand movement, who were not receiving any government pension or in government service.

pension.name: Monthly/Family Pension for Freedom Fighters and their Widows of Uttarakhand State
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance in the form of a monthly pension of ₹25,000/- to the freedom fighters of Uttarakhand and to the widows of deceased freedom fighters, in recognition of their contribution to the nation.

pension.name: Additional Pension to 100% Disabled Ex-Serviceman having No Child
pension.description: The scheme provides an additional amount of ₹100/- p.m., in addition to pension amount, to 100% disabled ex-servicemen who were members of Armed Forces & have been released from service due to 100% disability/permanent incapacity to work, provided disability or incapacity is attributable to service.

pension.name: Additional Pension to 100% Disabled Ex-Serviceman having Children
pension.description: The scheme provides an additional pension to 100% disabled ex-servicemen who were members of the Armed Forces & have been released from service due to 100% disability or permanent incapacity to work, provided the disability or incapacity is attributable to service.

pension.name: Additional Pension to Widow of Ex-Serviceman having Children
pension.description: The scheme is implemented by the Sainik and Ardh Sainik Welfare Department, Government of Haryana. Under the scheme, an additional amount, in addition to the pension, is provided to the widow of an ex-serviceman who dies while in service but not in any battle.

pension.name: Divyang Pension Yojana
pension.description: The State Government of Uttar Pradesh has launched Divyang Pension Yojana to improve and uplift the standard of living of disabled citizens.

pension.name: Pension for Agitators Who were Jailed for 7 Days or Injured During the Uttarakhand State Movement
pension.description: The scheme provides a monthly pension of ₹6,000 to agitators who were jailed for 7 days or injured during the Uttarakhand State Movement and are unable to gain employment due to certain reasons.

pension.name: Mukhyamantri Avivahita Pension Yojana
pension.description: Madhya Pradesh State Government started the "Mukhyamantri Avivahita Pension Yojana" for unmarried women of the state.

pension.name: Old Age Pension Scheme - On Completion of 60 Years (UKBOCWWB)
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide a monthly pension of ₹1,000/- to construction workers registered with the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board who have attained the age of 60 years.

pension.name: Pension Scheme for State Agitators Other Than Category of Agitators Who Went to Jail for 7 Days or got Injured during the Uttarakhand State Movement
pension.description: The scheme provides a monthly pension of ₹4,500/- to state agitators not imprisoned for 7 days or injured during the Uttarakhand State Movement, who are not eligible for any other government pension and are not employed in government service.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (Punjab)
pension.description: In this scheme, persons aged between 60 and 79 years receive a pension of ₹200/- per month, while those aged 80 years and above receive ₹500/- per month. The scheme applies only to persons belonging to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.

pension.name: Disabled Person Pension Scheme - Punjab
pension.description: Punjab State Government started a pension for disable person of the state.

pension.name: Leprosy Pension Scheme
pension.description: This Leprosy Pension Scheme has been started by the Government of Uttar Pradesh and operated by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, Uttar Pradesh.

pension.name: Employees' Pension Scheme
pension.description: The scheme ensures retirement security for employees covered under the EPF Act, offering monthly pensions, family pensions, and withdrawal benefits. It targets workers in organised sectors, providing financial support post-retirement, during disability, or to dependents after the member’s death.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (Maharashtra)
pension.description: The scheme "Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension" is a social welfare scheme by the Social Justice & Special Assistance Department, Government of Maharashtra. In this scheme, ₹600 per month is provided to elderly individuals aged 65 years and above from BPL families.

pension.name: Samagra Samajik Suraksha Parityakta Pension Yojana
pension.description: Under the Scheme, Madhya Pradesh government provides financial benefits to Be from a family living below the poverty line.

pension.name: Integrated Social Security Scheme: Pension for Women in Distress
pension.description: The scheme is implemented by the Social and Tribal Welfare Department, Administration of Union Territory of Ladakh. Under this scheme, a financial assistance of ₹1,000/- per month is provided as a pension to destitute widows, divorced, or deserted women with no source of income.

pension.name: Pension Plan (MBOCWWB)
pension.description: Under the scheme, a member of the fund who has been working as a building worker for not less than one year after the commencement of these rules shall on completion of sixty years of age be eligible for the person.

pension.name: Ganga Swarupa Pension Scheme
pension.description: The scheme provides financial assistance to widowed women, ensuring self-reliance through monthly pensions.

pension.name: Dependent Children Pension Scheme - Punjab
pension.description: Punjab State Government started a pension for Dependent Children of the state.

pension.name: Integrated Social Security Scheme - Pension For Transgender
pension.description: ISSS (Integrated Social Security Scheme) is a State Sponsored Scheme by the Social Welfare Department of the Govt. of Jammu and Kashmir where financial assistance is provided to Old Age Persons, Widows, Divorcees, Women in Distress, Transgenders, and Physically Handicapped Persons.

pension.name: Pension Scheme for Craftsmen
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide financial support and social security to traditional artisans of Uttarakhand who are aged 60 years or above and belong to families living below the poverty line.

pension.name: Loktantra Senani Samman Pension (Police Line Yojana) for the democracy fighters of the state imprisoned under MISA/DIR during the emergency period (from 25.06.1975 to 21.03.1977)
pension.description: The scheme provides financial assistance in the form of a monthly pension of ₹20,000/- to the democracy fighters of the state who were imprisoned under MISA/DIR during the Emergency period (from 25.06.1975 to 21.03.1977).

pension.name: Loktantra Senani Samman Pension Scheme for Widowed Wife or Widower Husband of Democracy Fighters of the State Imprisoned under MISA/DIR during Emergency Period (from 25.06.1975 to 21.03.1977)
pension.description: The scheme provides financial assistance in the form of a monthly pension of ₹20,000/- to the widowed wife or widower husband of democracy fighters of the state who were imprisoned under MISA/DIR during the Emergency period from 25.06.1975 to 21.03.1977.

pension.name: Samagr Samajik Suraksha Pension Yojana
pension.description: Madhya Pradesh State Government started the "Samagr Samajik Suraksha Pension Yojana" for different age groups.

pension.name: Samagra Samajik Suraksha Vridh Ashram Mai Nivaasarat Ant-vasi Pension Yojana
pension.description: Under this scheme, the Madhya Pradesh government provides financial support to the citizens who live in old age homes recognized by the Department of Social Justice.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme- Chhattisgarh
pension.description: Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹500/- is provided to widows aged 40 to 79 years who are living Below the Poverty Line (BPL) to support them in living a respectful life. This scheme is centrally sponsored.

pension.name: Mukhyamantri Nirmaan Shramik Pension Sahayata
pension.description: Chhattisgarh State Government started a scheme "Mukhyamantri Nirmaan Shramik Pension Sahayata" for Building and other construction worker on 29 March 2023.

pension.name: Disability Pension (BBOCWWB)
pension.description: Under the scheme, eligible workers receive ₹1,000/- per month for permanent disabilities caused by conditions such as paralysis, leprosy, tuberculosis, or accidents. Additionally, a one-time assistance of ₹75,000/- is granted for total permanent disability, and ₹50,000/- for partial disability.

pension.name: Widow Pension Scheme (HPBOCWWB)
pension.description: The "Widow Pension Scheme" launched by HPBOCWWB is designed to support widows of registered beneficiaries. Through this initiative, the Board will disburse a monthly amount of ₹1,500/- to help widows manage their expenses and improve their quality of life.

pension.name: Lakshmi Bai Samajik Suraksha Pension Yojana
pension.description: Lakshmi Bai Social Security Scheme has been launched by the Bihar government for the widow women of the state.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Vridhavastha Pension Yojana
pension.description: Under this scheme, the Madhya Pradesh government provides financial support to the citizens whose families live below the poverty line.

pension.name: Union Territory of Lakshadweep Pension Scheme for Widows
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide monthly financial assistance of ₹1,500/- to widows in the Union Territory of Lakshadweep. It covers widows who have not remarried and have no source of income.

pension.name: Chief Minister Specially Abled Samman Pension Scheme
pension.description: Rajasthan Government started a scholarship for Specially Abled persons.

pension.name: Union Territory of Lakshadweep Pension Scheme for Old Age
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide monthly financial assistance of ₹1,500/- to senior citizens in the UT. The scheme covers persons above 60 years of age who do not have any source of income.

pension.name: Widow Pension - Uttar Pradesh
pension.description: Widow pension in Uttar Pradesh is a social welfare scheme by the Government of Uttar Pradesh to provide financial assistance to widows residing in the state. The scheme is administered by the Women Welfare Department of the state government.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide social security pensions to divyangjans aged 18 and above who belong to a poor family (BPL) in society.

pension.name: Old Age And Destitute Pension
pension.description: The Government of Puducherry introduced Old Age And Destitute Pension Scheme to extend a helping hand by way of monthly financial assistance to Old Age Persons/Widows/ Deserted Women/ Unmarried Women and Enuches so that they could defray some petty personal expenses without depending on others.

pension.name: Pension Scheme for Elderly, Destitute Artists, Litterateurs and Writers of Uttarakhand State
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to elderly and impoverished artists, litterateurs and writers who have dedicated their lives to the development of art, culture, and literature and have made significant and distinguished contributions.

pension.name: Destitute Agricultural Labourers Pension Scheme
pension.description: The "Destitute Agricultural Labourers Pension Scheme" was launched by the Social Welfare and Nutritious Meal Programme Department, Government of Tamil Nadu. The scheme aims to give pensions of ₹1,000/- to destitute agricultural labourers of 60 years and above and dhoti, saree supplied on festivals.

pension.name: Chief Minister Ekal Nari Samman Pension Yojana
pension.description: The Rajasthan State government started the "Chief Minister Ekal Nari Samman Pension Yojana" scheme for women's support and empowerment.

pension.name: Pension To Widows And Destitute Women-Chandigarh
pension.description: Under the Pension To Widows And Destitute Women scheme To provide financial assistance to Widows and Destitute Women of Chandigarh.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme- Chhattisgarh
pension.description: Under this scheme, financial assistance is provided to elderly people aged 60 years and above living Below the Poverty Line (BPL) to support them in living a respectful life.

pension.name: Union Territory of Lakshadweep Pension Scheme for Abandoned Ladies/Destitute
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide monthly financial assistance of ₹1,500/- to abandoned ladies & destitute women in the UT. The scheme covers women who have been divorced or abandoned by their husband or relatives, have not remarried, & are without any source of income or have only a nominal income.

pension.name: 60% and above Disability Allowances
pension.description: "60% and Disability Allowance" is a pension scheme by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Govt of Tripura. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance in the form of a monthly pension of ₹2,000/- to differently-abled persons with a disability of 60% and above.

pension.name: West Bengal Transport Workers’ Social Security Scheme: Family Pension
pension.description: Under this scheme, beneficiaries, upon retirement or unfortunate demise, will have 50% of their last drawn amount transferred monthly to their families through a Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) system.

pension.name: Monthly Pension for the Maintenance of the Parents of the Martyred Soldier of the Army
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance of ₹10,000 per month to the dependent parents of martyred soldiers for their maintenance and livelihood.

pension.name: Suraksha Laghu Evan Simant Vriddhajan Krishak Samman Pension Yojana
pension.description: The Rajasthan government's Social Justice and Empowerment Department launched the "Suraksha Laghu Evan Seemaant Vrddhajan Krshak Sammaan Penshan Yojana" program, a social welfare initiative that aims to improve the lives by providing them with monthly financial assistance.

pension.name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Maandhan Yojana
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide old-age social security through a minimum assured monthly pension of ₹ 3,000/- to Small and Marginal Farmers after attaining the age of 60 years.

pension.name: Union Territory of Lakshadweep Pension Scheme for Specially Abled Persons
pension.description: ​The scheme aims to provide a monthly financial assistance of ₹1,500 to persons with disabilities, including those who are deaf, mute, blind, intellectually disabled, or orthopedically impaired, with a disability level of 40% or more and without any source of income.

pension.name: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan
pension.description: The scheme aims to ensure old age protection for unorganised workers. It provides a minimum assured pension of ₹3,000/- per month and family pension benefits to unorganised workers aged 18 to 40 years who earn less than ₹15,000/- monthly, supported by matching government contributions.

pension.name: Manabik Scheme
pension.description: The Manabik Pension Scheme is a financial assistance program launched in 2018 to provide monthly pensions to individuals with disabilities in West Bengal.

pension.name: Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa - Persons with Disabilities
pension.description: The scheme “Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa - Persons with Disabilities" was launched by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura to provide financial assistance to Persons with Disabilities in the form of a monthly pension of ₹2,000/-.

pension.name: Samajik Sewa Bhatta Scheme
pension.description: The scheme aims to support traditional faith healers by providing a monthly pension. Under this scheme, ₹1,500 is provided to each eligible traditional healer across the state.

pension.name: Nirman Shramik Jeevan va Bhavishya Suraksha Yojana
pension.description: The scheme "Nirman Shramik Jeevan va Bhavishya Suraksha Yojana" aims to provide financial security and social protection through insurance and pension benefits to registered construction workers and their families. Benefits under various flagship schemes are provided.

pension.name: Pension Scheme to Old Age Persons (Senior Citizen)-DNH&DD
pension.state: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
pension.description: The objective of this scheme is to provide financial support to eligible senior citizens to cater to their basic needs. The scheme benefits senior citizens aged 60 years and above, providing a monthly pension of ₹1,500/- for those aged 60–69 years and ₹2,000/- for those aged 70 years and above.

pension.name: Allowance for 100% Blind
pension.description: The scheme  “Allowance for 100% Blind" aims to provide a monthly pension of ₹2,000/- to the blind persons of Tripura aged 10 years and above having 100% blindness. The application should be submitted to the nearest Child Development Project Officer (CDPO).

pension.name: Vishesh Samman Pension Yojana for the State Agitators who became Disabled and Completely Bedridden during the Uttarakhand State Movement
pension.description: The scheme aims to honor state agitators who became permanently disabled and bedridden during the Uttarakhand Statehood Movement by providing them with monthly financial assistance of ₹20,000/-.

pension.name: Scheme For Financial Assistance For Veteran Artists
pension.description: A pension scheme by MoC improves the financial and socio-economic status of the old artists and scholars who have contributed significantly in their specialized fields. Still, due to old age, they have to lead a miserable life or are in penury condition.

pension.name: Sarvajan Pension Yojana
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance of per month to elderly individuals, widows, destitute women, differently-abled persons, primitive tribes, and HIV/AIDS patients. The scheme eliminates previous age restrictions and targets inclusivity by guaranteeing pensions to all eligible.

pension.name: Mukhyamantri Kalyakani Pension  Yojana
pension.description: Under the Scheme, Every month financial assistance is provided to the Kalyani (widow) residing in the state for providing social security and subsistence.

pension.name: Old Age Samman Allowance
pension.description: "Old Age Samman Allowance" is a social security scheme implemented by the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of Haryana. The scheme provides a pension of ₹ 3000/month to senior citizens (60 years of age or older) who are unable to sustain themselves with their own resource

pension.name: Pension Scheme to Widow- DNH & DD
pension.state: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
pension.description: The scheme is implemented by the Social Welfare Department, aims to provide financial assistance to widows aged between 18-59 years. The scheme offers ₹1,000/- per month to help meet their basic needs, ensuring public assistance for widows in need of support to maintain a dignified life.

pension.name: Integrated Social Security Scheme - Women In Distress
pension.description: ISSS (Integrated Social Security Scheme) is a State Sponsored Scheme by the Social Welfare Department of the Govt. of Jammu and Kashmir where financial assistance is provided to Old Age Persons, Widows, Divorcees, Women in Distress, Transgenders, and Physically Handicapped Persons.

pension.name: Pension Scheme to Disabled Persons (Divyangjan)- DNH & DD
pension.state: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
pension.description: The scheme is implemented by the Social Welfare Department to provide financial assistance to individuals with disabilities. The primary objective is to provide financial benefits to eligible Divyangjan to cater to their basic needs. The scheme is targeted at Divyangjan aged between 18 to 59 years.

pension.name: Mukhyamantri Kalakar Sahayata Yojana
pension.description: Mukhyamantri Kalakar Sahayata Yojana is a scheme under the Odia Language, Literature & Culture Department, Government of Odisha aims to offer sustenance and assistance to elderly artists who, despite facing financial hardship, have consistently devoted themselves to enriching culture of the state.

pension.name: Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa - Old and Infirm
pension.description: The scheme “Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa- Old and Infirm" was launched by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura. This scheme aims to provide financial assistance to poor people of Tripura who are old and infirm, such as senior citizens.

pension.name: Contributory Social Security Scheme
pension.description: The "Contributory Social Security Scheme (CSSS)" intends to provide social security to marginal workers on a contributory basis. The duration of the scheme shall be for a period of 5 years, or 10 years. The amount will be deposited in a bank account opened for the beneficiary.

pension.name: The Andaman & Nicobar Islands Old Age Assistance Scheme
pension.description: The aim of the scheme is to provide social security by way of financial assistance to destitute, senior citizens who are without any means of subsistence and having no one to give financial support to them for their livelihood.

pension.name: National Family Benefit Scheme (Maharashtra)
pension.description: The scheme "National Family Benefit Scheme (Maharashtra)" is a social welfare scheme by the Social Justice & Special Assistance Department, Government of Maharashtra. Under this scheme, ₹20,000/-. is provided to the family in the event of the death of their primary breadwinner.

pension.name: Welfare Services for Disabled Persons
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide support, rehabilitation, and empowerment to persons with disabilities through financial assistance for healthcare, education, vocational training, and assistive devices.

pension.name: Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa - Vulnerable Women
pension.description: The scheme “Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa- Vulnerable Women" was launched by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura to provide financial assistance to Vulnerable Woman in need of special care, support or protection because of age, disability.

pension.name: Disability Relief Allowance Scheme
pension.description: The Disability Relief Allowance Scheme under the Department of Social Justice, Government of Himachal Pradesh provides financial assistance to individuals with disabilities to help them manage their living costs and improve their quality of life.

pension.name: Financial Assistance to Old Age Pensioner Suffering From TB/ Cancer
pension.description: “Financial Assistance to Old Age Pensioner Suffering From TB/ Cancer” by the Dept of Women & Child Development, Govt of Puducherry, was introduced to extend a helping hand by way of financial assistance to Old Age/Destitute Pensioners who are suffering from TB/Cancer to meet the medical expenses.

pension.name: Social Security for Tea Plantation Workers in Assam
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide institutionalized social security cover, including provident funds, pensions, and life insurance. It provides monthly pensions, provident fund advances, and lump-sum death benefits to tea plantation workers in Assam and their dependent families.

pension.name: Swami Vivekananda Nishakta Swavlamban Protsahan Yojana
pension.description: “Swami Vivekananda Nishakta Swavlamban Protsahan Yojana ” is a Social Security Scheme by the Department of Women, Child Development & Social Security, Government of Jharkhand. The aim of the scheme is to provide social security to differently abled people aged five(5) years and above.

pension.name: Financial Assistance to Pensioners Suffering From HIV and AIDS
pension.description: “Financial Assistance to Pensioners Suffering From HIV/AIDS” is a Social Security Scheme by the Department of Women, Child Development & Social Security, Government of Jharkhand which aims to provide social security through financial assistance to those persons who are suffering from HIV/AIDS.

pension.name: Bina Mulya Samajik Suraksha Yojana
pension.description: "Bina Mulya Samajik Suraksha Yojana (BM-SSY)" is a comprehensive social security scheme for unorganized workers in the state of West Bengal, India. It was launched by the West Bengal government in 2017.

pension.name: Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa - Unorganized Workers
pension.description: The scheme “Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa-Unorganized Workers" was launched by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura. The objective of this scheme is to provide financial benefits to Unorganized Workers (home-based, self-employed or wage workers).

pension.name: Uttarakhand Oon Yojana
pension.description: The scheme aims to promote traditional wool spinning and weaving activities in the state while providing sustainable employment opportunities to local artisans and weavers.

pension.name: Financial Assistance to Destitute Elderly and Disabled Person
pension.description: The scheme "Financial Assistance to Destitute Elderly and Disabled Person" provides monthly financial aid to elderly individuals (60+) and severely disabled persons who meet specific income and dependency criteria.

pension.name: Ganga Swarupa Punah Lagna Arthik Sahay Scheme
pension.description: The scheme supports remarried women who are beneficiaries of the Ganga Swarupa Pension Scheme, aiming to reintegrate them into society with financial support.

pension.name: Lokprasar Prakalpo Scheme
pension.description: The scheme “Lokprasar Prakalpo Scheme” was launched by the Department of Information & Cultural Affairs, Government of West Bengal in 2014 to revive the Folk and Tribal Culture in their respective genre across various zones and districts in the State.

pension.name: Financial Assistance for Stage III & IV Cancer Patients
pension.description: The scheme “Financial Assistance for Stage III & IV Cancer Patients” was launched by the Social Justice & Empowerment Department, Government of Haryana. Under this scheme, a monthly financial assistance of ₹3,000/- will be provided to Stage III & IV cancer patients of Haryana from 01.01.2024.

pension.name: National Handicrafts Development Programme: Direct Benefit to Artisans - Supports to Artisans in Indigent Circumstances
pension.description: The scheme "Direct Benefit to Artisans - Supports to Artisans in Indigent Circumstances" is designed to give a boost to the handicraft sector in India. Assistance of upto ₹8,000/- per month is granted to the Artisans in Indigent Circumstances in the form of monthly allowance/lump sum grant/ both.

pension.name: Goa State Working Journalists Welfare Scheme
pension.description: "Goa State Working Journalists Welfare Scheme" aims to improve the welfare of working journalists retired from service in Goa and their families. It provides a monthly welfare fund of ₹ 7,500/- and family pension benefits for eligible journalists and their dependents.

pension.name: NPS Vatsalya Scheme
pension.description: NPS Vatsalya is a contributory saving cum long-term financial security scheme designed exclusively for minors for the purposes of enhancing financial literacy and financial planning and to encourage empowerment of children, emanating from the vision of "Viksit Bharat@2047.

pension.name: Old Age Assistance
pension.description: Old Age Assistance or Pension is a welfare scheme of Delhi Govt to provide assistance to Old age needy persons. The primary goal of this scheme is to offer eligible senior citizens financial help in the form of monthly pensions.

pension.name: Financial Assistance To Women And Girl Acid Attack Victims
pension.description: The scheme “Financial Assistance to Women and Girls Acid Attack Victims” by the Govt. of Haryana, provides financial assistance in the form of a monthly pension to the women/girls residing in Haryana who have suffered disfigurement of any part of the body or have become disabled due to acid attack.

pension.name: Poompuhar State Award
pension.description: "Poompuhar State Award" was launched by the Dept of Handlooms, Handicrafts, Textiles & Khadi, Tamil Nadu. The awardees are given ₹50,000, a 4-gram gold medal, tamirapathiram and a certificate. The economically backward awardees are also eligible for monthly pension of ₹3,500/- after 60 years of age.

pension.name: Grant for the Marriage of Daughter of Destitute Widow
pension.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance of ₹50,000/- per daughter for the marriage of up to two daughters of destitute widows who are receiving widow pension and whose total monthly income from all sources does not exceed ₹4,000/-, or who belong to BPL families.

pension.name: Financial Assistance to Ex-Servicemen of and above the Age of 60 Years and their Widows
pension.description: Under the scheme, financial assistance of ₹3,000/- per month is given to ex-servicemen & widows of ex-serviceman of & above age of 60 years, who are not in receipt of any kind of pension/financial assistance or maintenance allowance either from any Department of Government of India/State Government.

pension.name: Financial Assistance to WW II Veteran and Widows of WW II Veterans
pension.description: Under the scheme, financial assistance of ₹10,000/- per month shall be given to Ex-servicemen of the Pre-Independence Era (World War II Veterans) & their widows, who are not in receipt of any kind of pension/financial assistance either from any Department of Government of India/State Government.

pension.name: Financial Assistance to War Widows of Defence Forces
pension.description: Under the scheme, financial assistance of ₹3,000/- per month shall be granted to War Widows of Defence Force Personnel’s (Army, Navy & Air Force) who were killed in action during declared war of 1962, 1965 & 1971 in addition to family pension already getting from Government of India.

pension.name: Family Pension (DBOCWWB)
pension.description: The scheme "Family Pension" was launched by the Delhi Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. In this scheme, upon the death of the pensioner, the surviving spouse is provided 50% of the pension amount or ₹1500/-, whichever is higher.

pension.name: Family/ Widow Pension Scheme (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
pension.description: The "Family/ Widow Pension Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that provides pension to the spouse of a registered construction worker in the event of his/ her death.

pension.name: Family Pension (BBOCWWB)
pension.description: The scheme "Family Pension" by the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (BBOCWWB), Labour Resources Department, Government of Bihar, provides pension to the dependent of the deceased pensioner of the Board.

pension.name: Parivaar Pension Yojana (JBOCWWB)
pension.description: "Parivaar Pension Yojana (JBOCWWB)" is a scheme by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training & Skill Development Govt of Jharkhand. The scheme provides 50% of the pension amount or a maximum of ₹ 500/- to the family members of the deceased pensioner.

pension.name: Family Pension (GBOCWWB)
pension.description: "Family Pension (GBOCWWB)" is a Welfare Scheme by the Goa Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of the Dept of Labour and Employment, Goa. Through this scheme, in the event of the death of the pensioner of the Board, a 50% pension or ₹200, whichever is higher, is given to the spouse.

pension.name: Pension for Journalists’ Family
pension.description: The "Pension for Journalists’ Family" was launched by the Tamil Development, Religious Endowments, and Information Department, Government of Tamil Nadu. The scheme aims to give a monthly pension to the wife of a deceased journalist pensioner.

pension.name: Annath Pension Yojana (JBOCWWB)
pension.description: "Annath Pension Yojana (JBOCWWB)" is a scheme by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training & Skill Development Govt of Jharkhand. In case of the death of the pensioner, the pension will be payable at the rate of the family pension and will be evenly divided among orphans below 18 years of age.

pension.name: Family Pension (MBOCWWB)
pension.description: In the event of the death of a pensioner who was receiving a pension from the Board, a family pension shall be given to the surviving spouse by the Meghalaya Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (MBOCWWB).

pension.name: Pension Scheme (HPBOCWWB)
pension.description: The "Pension Scheme" by the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, Labour Department, Himachal Pradesh provides registered beneficiaries with a minimum pension of ₹1,000/- per month upon reaching 60 years of age.

pension.name: Silpi Pension Scheme - Assam
pension.description: Silpi Pension Scheme is a Welfare Scheme provided by the Government of Assam to support the Artists of Assam. Those artists and families who have made outstanding contributions in the field of Art and Culture of Assam will be given financial aid and Pension.

pension.name: Family Pension Scheme (UKBOCWWB)
pension.description: The scheme provides financial assistance to the spouse of a registered construction worker in the event of the worker’s death, if the deceased was a pensioner. Under this scheme, the family pension will be ₹500/- per month or 50% of the pensioner's pension, whichever is higher.

pension.name: Permanent Disability Pension Scheme (P.B.O.C.W.W.B)
pension.description: The "Permanent Disability Pension Scheme'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Punjab, is a welfare scheme that provides pension to the registered beneficiary with at least 1 year of membership with the Board who becomes fully disabled.

pension.name: Samman Pension Yojana for All First-Generation Living Legal Heirs of Freedom Fighters of Uttarakhand State
pension.description: The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹4,800/- per month as pension to all first-generation living legal heirs of freedom fighters residing in the state of Uttarakhand.

pension.name: Pension to the Beneficiary Building Worker Who Has Completed 60 Years
pension.description: The Manipur Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board provides a monthly pension to workers who have completed 5 years of service as a registered beneficiary and reached 60.

pension.name: Family Pension- Haryana Silicosis Board
pension.description: Under this scheme, after the death of the worker due to Silicosis, financial assistance of ₹3,500/- per month is provided to the spouse of the worker until he/she remains alive. In the case of an unmarried worker, the financial assistance is provided to his/her living parents.

pension.name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (Jammu and Kashmir)
pension.description: Launched on 15th August 1995, the scheme "National Social Assistance Programme" is a welfare scheme by the Social Welfare Department, Union Territory of Jammu & Kashmir. It provides monthly financial aid of ₹1,000/- to widows aged 40 years and above who belong to Below Poverty Line (BPL) households.

pension.name: National Family Benefit Scheme (Punjab)
pension.description: The scheme "National Family Benefit Scheme (Punjab)" by the Department of Social Security and Development of Women and Children, Government of Punjab, provides a lump sum assistance of ₹20,000/- is provided to the bereaved household in the event of the death of their breadwinner.

pension.name: The Mizoram Provisional Employees Savings Scheme
pension.description:
This scheme laid out to allow the members to make defined contribution towards planned savings thereby securing the future in the form of pension. This Scheme shall be compulsory for all Provisional Employees whose
engagement is duly approved by DP&AR and Finance Department, Government of Mizoram.

pension.name: Financial Assistance to Blind Ex-Servicemen
pension.description: Under the scheme, financial assistance of ₹3,000/‐ per month shall be granted to those Permanent Blind Ex-servicemen who are bonafide resident of Haryana & are not in receipt of any kind of pension/financial assistance either from Haryana Govt. or any other Department of Govt. of India.

tribal.name: Promotion of Tribal Culture
tribal.description: The scheme provides financial grants (up to ₹3,00,000/- per organization) to registered NGOs/Trusts/Voluntary Organizations for promoting tribal culture among ST(P) communities in Assam's plain districts (outside BTC).

tribal.name: NEET/JEE Coaching For Tribal Students
tribal.description: NEET/JEE Coaching for tribal students scheme launched in Rajasthan by Tribal Area Development in the year 2001.

tribal.name: Honorarium for Pradhan Samajpatis of Indigenous Tribal Community
tribal.description: “Honorarium for Pradhan Samajpatis of Indigenous Tribal Community" was launched by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Govt. of Tripura on 1st July 2021 to provide financial assistance to to the Pradhan Samajpatis (Chief Community Headman) of indigenous tribal community.

tribal.name: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department: Stationary-Text Books
tribal.description: This scheme provides free textbooks to students from 1st to 12th standard in Adi Dravidar and Tribal welfare schools.

tribal.name: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department-Incentive/Award of Prizes
tribal.description: The scheme “Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department-Incentive/Award of Prizes” offers incentives to One Boy and One Girl each from Adi Dravidar/ Tribal/ Adi Dravidar Converted to Christianity who have secured highest mark who have achieved top marks in 10th and 12th standard examinations.

tribal.name: Admission of Adi Dravidar/Tribal Students in Plus One at Reputed Schools
tribal.description: This scheme provides education benefits by admitting top Adi Dravidar/Tribal Students into reputed private schools for Plus One, covering 320 students annually across districts.

tribal.name: Integrated Horticulture Development Program for Tribal Area Farmers (TASP) HRT-3
tribal.description: The scheme "Integrated Horticulture Development Program for Tribal Area Farmers (TASP) HRT-3" by the Agriculture and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to enhance the horticulture sector through financial assistance and subsidies to tribal area farmers across the state.

tribal.name: Provide Assistance to Farmers Bringing Goods to Market Committees for Cost of Goods Transportation (Tribal)
tribal.description: The scheme "Provide Assistance to Farmers Bringing Goods to Market Committees for Cost of Goods Transportation (Tribal)" aims to encourage tribal farmers to bring their produce to market committees by providing financial assistance towards transportation costs.

tribal.name: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department: Incentive/Award of Prizes-For Each Subjects
tribal.description: The scheme offers cash awards to students from the Adi Dravidar, Tribal communities, and Adi Dravidar converts to Christianity for excelling in academic subjects in 10th and Plus 2 examinations.

tribal.name: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department: Scholarship-Free Education upto 12th Std. to All
tribal.description: This scheme provides free education for Adi Dravidar, Tribal and Adi Dravidar (Converted to Christianity) students up to 12th standard, covering tuition fees, which are reimbursed by the government.

tribal.name: Large Sized Multi-Purpose Coop. Society: Medium Term Loan to Tribal Members
tribal.description: The scheme offers medium-term loans of up to ₹60,000/- without interest to tribal members, enabling them to fulfill various financial needs.

tribal.name: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department: Scholarship-Public Examination Fee for 10th and 12th Std.
tribal.description: The scheme provides reimbursement of public examination fees for 10th and 12th standard students from the Adi Dravidar, Tribal, and Adi Dravidar Converted to Christianity communities, with no income limit.

tribal.name: Burial Ground-Provision of Burial Grounds and Pathway to Burial Grounds
tribal.description: The scheme aims to support Adi Dravidar/Tribal communities by providing burial grounds and pathways in areas where such facilities are not available.

tribal.name: State Talent Search Examination in Mathematics and Science for Tribal Students of Meghalaya at the Close of Upper Primary Stage (Class VIII)
tribal.description: The scheme “State Talent Search Examination in Mathematics and Science for Tribal Students of Meghalaya at the Close of Upper Primary Stage (Class VIII)” was launched by the Education Department, Government of Meghalaya.

tribal.name: The Meghalaya Tribal Students’ Book Grant (Post-Matric Education)
tribal.description: "The Meghalaya Tribal Students’ Book Grant (Post-Matric Education)" scheme was launched by the Directorate of Higher and Technical Education, Meghalaya. Through this scheme, financial assistance is provided to the meritorious tribal students for the purchase of books for their studies.

tribal.name: Mukhyamantri Dairy Animal Distribution Programme (Special Backward Tribes Baiga, Bharia & Sahariya)
tribal.description: The scheme aims to improve the livelihood and nutritional security of tribal communities by providing two dairy animals at a 90% subsidy to eligible tribal livestock farmers.

tribal.name: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department-Hostels: Special Guides
tribal.description: The scheme provides hostel facilities to all boys and girls boarders studying up to XII Standard.

tribal.name: Economic Help To Tribal Girls For Higher Education (College Level)
tribal.description: Rajasthan Government started a scholarship for tribal girls higher education (College Level) In the year 1994-95.

tribal.name: To Increase Fish Seed Production and Inland Fisheries Resources in Tribal Areas: Training of Tribal Trainees - Gujarat
tribal.description: The scheme aims to boost fish production and provide employment through inland fisheries. Under this scheme, Financial assistance is provided as a scholarship to tribal trainees undergoing fisheries training.

tribal.name: Loans to Tribal Students for Higher Studies Abroad
tribal.description: The scheme's purpose is to offer financial assistance to tribal students for higher education abroad. It provides a loan of up to ₹15,00,000 at a 4% annual interest rate.

tribal.name: Economic Help To Tribal Girls For Higher Education (Class 11 & 12)
tribal.description: Rajasthan Government started a scholarship for tribal girls higher education (Class 11 & 12) In the year 2011.

tribal.name: Book Bank Scheme: II
tribal.description: The scheme provides free textbooks to Adi Dravidar/Tribal students pursuing professional courses like Medical, Engineering, Law, MBA, and others, which must be returned to the library after course completion.

tribal.name: Economic Help To Sahariya Tribal Girl Students (Class 11 And 12)
tribal.description: Rajasthan Government started a scholarship for Sahariya tribal girls' higher education (Class 11 and 12).

tribal.name: Pradhan Mantri Vanbandhu Kalyan Yojana
tribal.description: The scheme provides comprehensive support through six key components, including integrated village development under Pradhan Mantri Adi Adarsh Gram Yojana, development of Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups, support to Tribal Research Institutes, and Pre-Matric and Post-Matric Scholarships.

tribal.name: Assistance to Co-operative Irrigation Societies (Trible Area)
tribal.description: The scheme "Assistance to Co-operative Irrigation Societies (Tribal Area)" aims to enhance agricultural productivity by financially supporting co-operative irrigation societies in tribal areas through the installation of water distribution infrastructure.

tribal.name: Book Bank Scheme: I
tribal.description: The scheme provides free textbooks to Adi Dravidar/Tribal students pursuing professional courses like Medical, Engineering, Law, MBA, and others, which must be returned to the library after course completion.

tribal.name: Tribals Development Plan: Subsidy for Purchase of Motorcycle
tribal.description: The scheme aims to assist tribal families in Goa to purchase motorcycles for self-employment by offering a government subsidy, thereby promoting economic self-reliance among low-income households.

tribal.name: Construction of Free Houses for Tribals
tribal.description: This scheme provides free houses to tribals who already possess free house site pattas. The construction of the houses is carried out by government authorities, ensuring shelter for tribal communities.

tribal.name: Operation of Sports Hostels
tribal.description: The scheme aims to encourage tribal students to participate in sports and prepare them for national & international competitions. Tribal students from Classes 6 to 12 are provided with free accommodation, specialized training, nutritious meals & other necessary facilities in the sports hostels.

tribal.name: Assistance to Lawyers for Starting their Practice
tribal.description: This scheme provides financial assistance to unemployed Adi Dravidar and Tribal lawyers to help them start their legal practice, provided they have registered within the last five years and meet specific income criteria.

tribal.name: Kutirjyoti Yojana Gujarat
tribal.description: The scheme aims to provide free household electricity connections to Scheduled Tribe families in tribal areas.

tribal.name: Tribals Development Plan: Purchase of Tourist Taxi
tribal.description: This sub-scheme aims to empower Scheduled Tribe (ST) families by providing financial assistance to purchase tourist taxis for self-employment in Goa’s growing tourism industry. It supports tribal households in transitioning from traditional occupations to sustainable livelihood opportunities.

tribal.name: Fodder Minikits under Tribal Area Sub Plan (TASP) Scheme (ST Category)
tribal.description: The scheme “Fodder Minikits under Tribal Area Sub Plan (TASP) Scheme” is implemented by the Directorate of Animal Husbandry, Government of Gujarat to provide an improved variety of fodder seeds to Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries for widespread publicity, demonstration, and adaptation by farmers.

tribal.name: Awards to Bright Students
tribal.description: The "Awards to Bright Students" scheme offers financial assistance to the top two boys and two girls from Adi Dravidar/Tribal/Adi Dravidar Converted to Christianity in each district who have excelled in their 10th standard public examinations and continue their studies.

tribal.name: Award For Meritorious Students Of Meghalaya In The HSSLC Examination (Science Stream)
tribal.description: The Directorate of Educational Research & Training Meghalaya, Shillong is implementing a scheme in providing a one-time award for meritorious tribal students of Meghalaya who passed the current HSSLC Examination (Science) every year.

tribal.name: Award of ₹20,000/- to Best Writers
tribal.description: The scheme provides a cash award of ₹20,000/- to the top 10 writers from Adi Dravidar, Tribal, or Adi Dravidar communities converted to Christianity, with one additional spot for a writer from other communities.

tribal.name: Special Health Scheme for Primitive Tribes
tribal.description: The "Special Health Scheme for Primitive Tribes" scheme by the Welfare Department, Government of Jharkhand, aims to improve healthcare services for primitive tribal communities. Financial assistance of ₹38,41,632 is provided for targeted healthcare benefits.

tribal.name: Sheti Samrudhi Yojana
tribal.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to facilitate community level action for agriculture growth and management of natural resources. It offers financial assistance, wages for personnel, and funding for necessary office equipment for Tribal Farmers Co-operative.

tribal.name: Personality Development and Yogasana Training Camp (TASP)
tribal.description: The scheme "Personality Development and Yogasana Training Camp (TASP)" by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to enhance personality and promote physical, mental, and spiritual well-being through training for tribal youth by organizing seven-day camps.

tribal.name: Distribution of Seeds of more productions varieties/ Hybrids varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area: Power Operated Plant Protection Equipment Gujarat
tribal.description: The scheme aims to help farmers belonging to scheduled tribes and tribal cultivators residing outside tribal areas achieve higher production of agricultural crops and improve their livelihood.

tribal.name: Tribals Development Plan: Motor Driving Training [Light & Heavy Vehicles]
tribal.description: The scheme aims to provide full financial coverage for motor driving training, including courses for Light Motor Vehicles and Heavy Motor Vehicles, specifically recognizing Goa’s future in the transport sector. It benefits eligible Tribal families in Goa whose annual income is below ₹1,20,000/-.

tribal.name: Pre Examination Training Centre For All India Service Examinations Like I.A.S., I.P.S., I.R.S., Etc.
tribal.description: This scheme provides free pre-examination training, boarding, and lodging for Adi Dravidar and Tribal community candidates preparing for All India Service Examinations like IAS, IPS, and IRS.

tribal.name: Distribution of Seeds of more productions varieties/ Hybrids varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area: Plant protection pesticides Gujarat
tribal.description: The scheme aims to help Scheduled Tribe farmers and tribal cultivators residing outside tribal areas achieve higher agricultural crop production and improve their livelihood. Through this scheme, various tools and practices are provided to the farmers at subsidised rates.

tribal.name: Capital Subsidy Scheme for Setting Up Solar Plant in Milk Cooperatives in Gujarat State (Tribal Area)
tribal.description: The scheme aims to promote renewable energy and reduce electricity expenses. Through this scheme, a 75% subsidy is provided for setting up solar plants (1KW to 40KW) in milk cooperative societies in tribal areas.

tribal.name: Operation of Ashram Hostels
tribal.description: Under this scheme, free accommodation, nutritious meals, educational materials, uniforms, personal hygiene items, and other facilities are provided to Scheduled Tribe (ST) students residing in ashram hostels operated by the Tribal Area Development Department.

tribal.name: Lokprasar Prakalpo Scheme
tribal.description: The scheme “Lokprasar Prakalpo Scheme” was launched by the Department of Information & Cultural Affairs, Government of West Bengal in 2014 to revive the Folk and Tribal Culture in their respective genre across various zones and districts in the State.

tribal.name: Special Incentive Scheme for Women of Tharu, Boksa and Other Tribes (For Training and Marketing)
tribal.description: The scheme aims to empower tribal women artisans through specialized training and marketing opportunities and ensures the provision of financial assistance for travel, logistics, and stall rent for participating artisans.

tribal.name: Distribution of Seeds of more productions varieties/ Hybrids varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area: Field Demonstration (TASP & OTASP) Gujarat
tribal.description: The scheme aims to improve the agricultural production and livelihood of Scheduled Tribe farmers. Through this scheme, subsidies on seeds, fertilizers, and demonstrations are provided to tribal cultivators.

tribal.name: Animal Husbandry
tribal.description: This scheme provides subsidies on the distribution of sheep units, milch animals, and calves, as well as health coverage and artificial insemination for the animals. It is targeted at Tribal communities to improve their livelihood through animal husbandry.

tribal.name: Distribution of Seeds of more productions varieties/ Hybrids varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area: Water Carrying Pipe Line (TASP & OTASP) Gujarat
tribal.description: The scheme aims to improve the agricultural productivity of Scheduled Tribe farmers through subsidized seeds, fertilizers, and equipment. It provides direct support to tribal farmers for enhancing crop production and livelihood.

tribal.name: Distribution of Seeds of more productions varieties/ Hybrids varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area: 100% Soluble Chemical Fertilizer/Nano Fertilizer Gujarat
tribal.description: The scheme aims to help Adivasi farmers and tribal cultivators increase crop production and improve their livelihood. Through this scheme, subsidies are provided for various agricultural inputs and equipment such as seeds, fertilizers, plant protection equipment, and pump sets.

tribal.name: Hostel Rent Assistance Scheme for Tribal Students
tribal.description: The scheme provides financial assistance towards hostel rent for Scheduled Tribe students pursuing undergraduate and postgraduate courses in government colleges, who are staying in rented accommodation for the purpose of regular studies.

tribal.name: Competitive Exam Coaching
tribal.description: Competitive exam Coaching for tribal students scheme was launched in Rajasthan by Tribal Area Development Department in the year 2016-17, under which free coaching to tribal students for competitive examinations through Mankyi Lal Verma Tribal Research and Training Institute, Udaipur is provided.

tribal.name: Distribution of Seeds of more productions varieties/ Hybrids varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area: Castor cake/Neem cake/ Liquid Fermented Organic Manure / Green manure seed (For mix in the soil) Gujarat
tribal.description: The scheme aims to help tribal farmers improve their livelihood by increasing agricultural crop production. Through this scheme, subsidies are provided on high-yield and hybrid seeds, fertilizers, and other agricultural inputs to Scheduled Tribe farmers.

tribal.name: 6% Interest Subsidy on Loans taken through Banks up to a Limit of Rs.5 Lakh for Self-Employment
tribal.description: The scheme aims to promote self-employment among scheduled tribe (ST) individuals by making business loans affordable. Under this scheme, ST beneficiaries availing loans for self-employment are provided a interest subsidy to reduce financial burden and encourage entrepreneurship.

tribal.name: Tribals Development Plan: Tea/Coffee And Coconut Parlor
tribal.description: The scheme aims to attract tourists with better quality mechanized Tea and Coffee shops and tender coconut water parlors, as Goa is a tourist destination, ensuring better hygiene than other restaurants. The scheme provides entire project financing for establishing a parlor.

tribal.name: Afforestation Schemes Providing Incentives And Employment To Tribals In Forest
tribal.description: This scheme offers grants to unemployed tribals for afforestation work, providing them with incentives and employment opportunities in forest operations.

tribal.name: Pre-Matric Scholarship For Scheduled Tribe Students Studying In Classes 9th & 10th
tribal.description: The scheme aims to support Scheduled Tribe children in classes 9th and 10th to minimize drop-out rates and aid progression to higher education. The scheme provides a recurring monthly scholarship, an annual book grant, and disability allowances directly to the Scheduled Tribe student beneficiaries.

tribal.name: To Increase Fish Seed Production and Inland Fisheries Resources in Tribal Areas: Fish Cage Culture - Gujarat
tribal.description: The scheme aims to increase fish production and provide employment through reservoir-based inland fisheries. It offers financial assistance to Scheduled Tribe fishermen.

tribal.name: Financial assistance to Meritorious Scheduled Tribe Students who have Passed First Division from Board and University
tribal.description: The objective of the scheme is to motivate meritorious Scheduled Tribe students of Rajasthan by providing one-time financial assistance of ₹3,500/- to those pursuing regular college courses and achieving first division.

tribal.name: PM Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan (PM JANMAN)
tribal.description: The scheme aims to improve the socio-economic status of PVTGs by saturating families and habitations with basic facilities and services. It provides 11 critical infrastructure and welfare interventions, including housing, water, electricity, and connectivity.

tribal.name: Doodh Sanjivani Scheme
tribal.description: The scheme aims to enhance the nutritional health of children aged 6 months to 6 years and pregnant and lactating mothers by providing fortified flavored milk in Anganwadi centers across 20 districts in the state.

tribal.name: Cash Amount for Encouraging Scheduled Tribe Sportsperson Students
tribal.description: Under this scheme, cash awards, certificates, and mementos are provided as a form of encouragement to Scheduled Tribe  sportsperson students who secure the top three positions in national and international sports events.

tribal.name: Assistance for Funeral Rites
tribal.description: The “Assistance for Funeral Rites” scheme provides ₹500/- to support SC/ST families with low income, in covering funeral expenses, ensuring that they can conduct funeral rites for their deceased ones.

tribal.name: Mukhyamantri Nahri Kendra
tribal.description: The scheme empowers tribal women by providing them with livelihood opportunities through women-run centers. These centers promote the sale of tribal dishes and home-made products, thereby improving income and living standards. Tribal women-run groups receive a subsidy covering 50% of project cost.

tribal.name: To Increase Fish Seed Production and Inland Fisheries Resources in Tribal Areas: Fish Seed Rearing by Women - Gujarat
tribal.description: The scheme aims to increase fish production through inland fisheries and provide employment opportunities, particularly to Scheduled Tribe women engaged in fish seed rearing.

tribal.name: Aawaas Sahayata
tribal.description: Launched in 2016, the "Aawaas Sahayata" scheme by the Department of Tribal Affairs, Madhya Pradesh, The scheme provides housing assistance to tribal students pursuing higher education outside their hometowns where hostels are unavailable.

tribal.name: Housing Assistance (Madhya Pradesh)
tribal.description: The scheme "Housing Assistance (Madhya Pradesh)" by the Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, provides housing support to tribal boys and girls pursuing higher education outside their hometown, where hostels are not available.

tribal.name: Scheduled Tribe Relief Scheme
tribal.description: Launched on 31st March 2017, the "Scheduled Tribe Relief Scheme" by the Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, provides immediate support for destitute tribal brides and assistance to disabled, elderly, blind, and impoverished individuals.

tribal.name: Ashramshalas (Grant in Aid)
tribal.description: The scheme aims to provide schooling, boarding and lodging to Scheduled Tribe students at a single location, enabling better employment opportunities and competitive equality with other sections of society. The scheme offers residential education with complete support including free education.

tribal.name: To Increase Fish Seed Production and Inland Fisheries Resources in Tribal Areas: Training Kit - Gujarat
tribal.description: The scheme aims to provide training and equipment to boost inland fisheries among Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries.

tribal.name: Sri Ram Janmabhoomi Shabari Smriti Yatra Sahay Scheme
tribal.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to scheduled tribes and tribal communities for pilgrimage to Sri Ram Janmabhoomi in Ayodhya in memory of Maa Shabari. The scheme offers ₹5,000/- financial assistance per pilgrim to enable darshan of Lord Sri Ram for eligible tribal beneficiaries.

tribal.name: To Increase Fish Seed Production and Inland Fisheries Resources in Tribal Areas: Fish Seed Stocking - Gujarat
tribal.description: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance for fish seed stocking, including transportation costs, is provided to beneficiaries from Scheduled Tribes involved in inland fisheries.

tribal.name: To Increase Fish Seed Production and Inland Fisheries Resources in Tribal Areas: Fish Seed Rearing - Gujarat
tribal.description: The scheme aims to increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and provide employment to people involved in inland fisheries activities.

tribal.name: 6% Interest Subsidy on Loans taken for Vehicle Purchase through Banks
tribal.description: The scheme aims to support Scheduled Tribe (ST) individuals in purchasing vehicles for self-employment purposes. Under this scheme, tribal beneficiaries availing bank loans for vehicle purchase with interest subsidy. This financial support helps reduce the repayment burden on tribal entrepreneurs.

tribal.name: Assistance for Power Driven Chaff Cutter Scheme under Tribal Area Sub Plan (TASP- ST Category)
tribal.description: The scheme provides financial assistance to dairy farmers belonging to ST category on purchase of electric powered chaff cutters. By using power driven chaff cutter, dairy farmers can reduce wastage of green & dry fodder which is economically important for their profitable dairy farming.

tribal.name: Incentive To Saharia Students For B.Ed
tribal.description: In Rajasthan, "Incentive To Saharia Students For B.Ed" scheme is run by the "Tribal Area Development Department" from 2004-05.

tribal.name: Toposili Bandhu (Old Age Pension) Scheme
tribal.description: "Toposili Bandhu" (Old Age Pension) scheme was initiated by the Backward Classes Welfare Department and Tribal Development Department of West Bengal Government.

tribal.name: Rotavator/Thresher Machine Equipment Assistance Scheme
tribal.description: The scheme aims to support BPL Scheduled Tribe farmers in tribal areas by providing financial assistance to purchase rotavators and thresher machines.

tribal.name: Poshan Sudha
tribal.description: This scheme is a government initiative to provide nutrition and healthcare support to pregnant and lactating mothers in tribal districts of Gujarat.

tribal.name: IELTS & TOEFL Training Assistance
tribal.description: The scheme aims to help tribal students pursue higher studies abroad by offering professional IELTS/TOEFL training. It provides ₹3,10,000/- in training assistance and comprehensive guidance.

tribal.name: Loan For Commercial Pilot Training
tribal.description: The scheme aims to offer affordable loans of up to ₹25,00,000 at a 4% annual interest rate to tribal students who want to pursue pilot training.

tribal.name: Maa-Badi / Maa-Badi Day-Care Centre Scheme
tribal.description: The scheme aims to impart basic education, nutrition, and awareness to tribal children aged 6–12. The scheme offers meals, learning material, and other support through community-run centres.

tribal.name: Integrated Dairy Development Scheme Gujarat
tribal.description: Under this scheme, tribal women beneficiaries are provided with milch cattle, dairy equipment, and financial assistance of up to ₹90,000 per unit.

tribal.name: Reimbursement of Fees of ST Students Studying in Sainik School Private Institutions
tribal.description: The "Reimbursement of Fees for ST Students" scheme by the Tribal Welfare Department, Madhya Pradesh, offers free accommodation, school fees, and education for eligible students in Sainik Schools and private institutions.

tribal.name: Prakashan Sahayata Yojana
tribal.description: The scheme aims to support original literary/research work related to tribal communities. Authors are provided financial assistance of ₹6,000 to ₹12,000 for publication.

tribal.name: Kunwar Bai Nu Mameru Yojana Gujarat
tribal.description: The scheme aims to provide financial support to ST families during the marriage of girls, providing ₹12,000 per couple as financial assistance to eligible tribal women across the state.

tribal.name: Antya Sanskar Sahay Yojana
tribal.description: "Antya Sanskar Sahay Yojana" by the Department of Tribal Welfare, Government of Goa provides grants of financial assistance for the performance of funeral and religious ceremonies of Scheduled Tribe (ST) families.

tribal.name: State Welfare Scheme For Purohits
tribal.description: The scheme "State Welfare Scheme for Purohits" was launched in September 2020 to benefit the financially distressed practicing temple /tribal purohit or priests of other communities like Christians, Jain, and Buddhists.

tribal.name: Post Matric Scholarship for Class 11th 12th And College
tribal.description: The scheme "Post-Matric Scholarship" started in 1945 by the Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department, Madhya Pradesh, provides scholarships to Scheduled Tribe students studying in classes 11th, 12th, Colleges, PhD, and Professional Courses.

tribal.name: Atal Asra Yojana
tribal.description: "Atal Asra Yojana" by the Department of Tribal Welfare, Government of Goa provides financial assistance of up to ₹ 3,00,000 to individuals from Scheduled Tribes communities for construction/ re-construction and repairs of the house.

tribal.name: Self Employment Scheme Gujarat
tribal.description: The scheme provides unemployed tribal persons with affordable loans ranging from ₹50,000 to ₹5,00,000 at a 4% annual interest rate, aiming to help them establish self-employment opportunities.

tribal.name: State Disability Pension
tribal.description: The Social Welfare & Tribal Affairs Department, Government of Mizoram launched the State Disability Pension scheme. It provides a monthly pension of ₹350 to 200 eligible individuals who are completely blind or bedridden.

tribal.name: State Senior Citizens Home
tribal.description: The Social Welfare & Tribal Affairs Department, Government of Mizoram launched the State Senior Citizens Home for the homeless aged persons. It provides shelter and facilities to homeless elderly individuals aged 60 and above, who lack family support.

tribal.name: Junior Hostel (Scheduled Tribes)
tribal.description: Launched on 1st January 1966, the scheme "Junior Hostel" by the Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh provides free accommodation and mess facilities to students studying in classes 6th to 8th.

tribal.name: Shaheed Gram Vikas Yojana
tribal.description: The scheme "Shaheed Gram Vikas Yojana" by the Welfare Department, Government of Jharkhand, aims to develop villages linked to tribal freedom fighters. It provides housing, infrastructure, and other facilities to eligible Scheduled Tribes and Scheduled Castes.

tribal.name: Grant In Aid Hostels
tribal.description: The scheme aims to provide boarding and lodging facilities to tribal students who wish to pursue primary, secondary and higher secondary education. Free lodging and boarding facilities are provided to intended beneficiaries who are students studying in standards 5th to 12th.

tribal.name: Distribution of Seeds of more productions varieties/ Hybrids varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area: Tarpaulin Gujarat
tribal.description: The scheme aims to enhance crop production and livelihood. It provides subsidies on seeds, fertilizers, tarpaulins, and other agricultural inputs to Scheduled Tribe farmers.

tribal.name: The Post-Matric Scholarship For Scheduled Tribes Students
tribal.description: The scheme "Post-Matric Scholarship for Scheduled Tribes Students" by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs, Government of India, and Government of Mizoram aims to provide financial assistance to ST students pursuing post-matriculation education. It covers various recognized courses.

tribal.name: Mukhyamantri Anusuchit Jaati Vishesh Pariyojana Vitt Poshan Yojana
tribal.description: The scheme "Mukhyamantri Anusuchit Jaati Vishesh Pariyojana Vitt Poshan Yojana" under the Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste Department, Madhya Pradesh, aims to provide financial assistance for special project proposals received in the interest of the Scheduled Caste (SC) category.

tribal.name: Integrated Hostel Scheme
tribal.description: "Integrated Hostel Scheme" by the Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, provides free residential facilities, meals, and basic amenities to SC/ST/OBC students studying in classes 1st to 12th and also to the students pursuing college education.

tribal.name: State Government ST Scholarship (Class 1 to 8)
tribal.description: The scheme was launched by the Tribal Affairs Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, aims to give benefits for improving the educational status of scheduled tribe students. Under this scheme, scholarships are provided to Scheduled Tribe students in Classes 1 to 8.

tribal.name: Mukhyamantri Aadim Jan Jati Pension Yojana
tribal.description: The Mukhyamantri Aadim Jan Jati Pension Yojana, launched on 08-07-2015 by the Jharkhand Government, provides financial assistance to primitive tribal families. The pension is given to the adult married woman or, if none, the male head of the family.

tribal.name: Incentive For The Simple Marriage (Scheduled Tribe)
tribal.description: The scheme "Incentive for the Simple Marriage" was launched by the Tribal Welfare Department, Government of Karnataka. The scheme provides an incentive of ₹50,000/- to ST individuals residing in Karnataka who marry another ST individual on or after 11th August 2015.

tribal.name: Cycle Distribution Scheme
tribal.description: Under this scheme, tribal girl students in Class 11th who travel more than 2 km to reach school and did not receive a bicycle in Class 9th are given a cash amount equivalent to the cost of a bicycle.

tribal.name: Pre Matric Scholarship For Scheduled Tribe Students
tribal.description: A scholarship scheme by Ministry of Tribal Affairs for regular, full time students from Scheduled Tribe studying in Class 9th and 10th, in a Government School or in a School recognized by Govt. or a Central/State Board of Secondary Education.

tribal.name: Civil Service Incentive Scheme (Madhya Pradesh)
tribal.description: The "Civil Service Incentive Scheme (Madhya Pradesh)" scheme by the Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department of the Government of Madhya Pradesh provides an incentive amount to encourage SC/ST candidates to appear in Civil Services Examinations.

tribal.name: Ashram Schools (Scheduled Tribes)
tribal.description: The scheme "Ashram Schools" was launched on 1st January 1966 by the Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme provides free accommodation, mess, and education facilities to Scheduled Tribe students.

tribal.name: Study Abroad Scholarships (Scheduled Tribe)
tribal.description: Launched in 2003, the scheme "Study Abroad Scholarships" by the Tribal Affairs Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh covers tuition fees of 40,000 US Dollars per year, along with a living allowance of 9,000 US Dollars and a contingency allowance of 1,000 US Dollars.
